Extending the HSA shelf life ensures comliance with customer specification.   Impact assessment: - Logistics: logistics will need to order the material every three years instead of every year. therefore this results in reduction of resources and waste. - Documentation: Specification of Incoming Goods SP4170 for A9080 Albumin solution human must be updated to reflect the new expiry date (change action 529) - Training: No training is deemed needed as the method remains the same. Operators and logistics will be informed of the change (change action 535) - HSE: no impact on HSE, as there are no changes to the material used or how it is used.
Planned implementation data - comment: Change is needed for release of material according to customer timeline, and is aligned with customers experience and confirmed by inhouse testing.

Change Title: Implementing risk-based approach to periodic revision of documents
Change from:: All documents should be revied every 3 years.
Change to: The periodic review will be risk-based. Only documents cover  core organisational processes will be revised every 4 years.
Justification for Change: The proposed change reduces the mandatory periodic revision of all procedures and associated documents from every three years to only those procedures covering core processes. This change aligns with the principle of risk-based management, ensuring that resources are allocated more effectively.Core processes are the essential activities that directly contribute to achieving the organization’s objectives, such as delivering products or services that meet customer requirements and ensuring quality. These processes are identified, managed, and continuously improved as part of the Quality Management System, the ISO Process overview to ensure effectiveness and customer satisfaction. Critical documents in connection to a core process are any documents essential for ensuring the proper execution, control, and consistency of that process, such as procedures. Non-critical documents such as control forms,  worksheets, Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS), control plans, working instructions, and policies will no longer require periodic reviews unless a specific need arises (e.g., changes in regulations, customer requirements, or internal processes update). This adjustment prevents unnecessary administrative effort and ensures compliance focus is prioritized for areas critical to business and operational continuity.Documents unrelated to core processes do not inherently carry operational or regulatory risk if reviewed only on an as-needed basis. By focusing reviews on core process-related   documents, the organization enhances its ability to maintain compliance, product quality, safety and process efficiency. Quality policy and quality manual will be updates when needed, and the need will be evaluated during management review (Refer to SOP5016. Extending the review period from three to four years reflects a balanced approach to document management by aligning with industry practices and focusing on risk-based priorities. Many procedures, especially those governing stable processes, do not require frequent updates as they are unlikely to change significantly within this timeframe. The extension reduces administrative burden while maintaining compliance and dynamic processes, as these will still be reviewed more frequently if needed.  Positive Impacts1.	Improved Efficiency: Reduces the workload, allowing focus on value-added activities. Frees up resources for more impactful quality assurance and risk management initiatives.2.	Cost Savings: Minimizes time and resource consumption associated with unnecessary document reviews.3.	Flexibility: Adapts document management efforts to actual business needs while eliminating routine updates to static information.Negative Impacts1.	Inconsistent Review Frequency: Some non-critical documents may be overlooked if not flagged for review when needed, potentially leading to outdated information.2.	Training Requirements: Employees involved in document management may require training to understand the new review triggers and identify when updates to non-critical documents are necessary. Impact assessment: Minor. No impact on product quality, safety, validation status of equipment
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risk assessment 1. 	Risk of missed Updates for Non-Critical Documents: Non-critical documents may not be updated promptly if no need is identified, leading to potential miscommunication or errors. This is risk as minor as most of documents which will be excluded from periodic review play a support role: SOP for laboratory equipment, buffer and reagents recipes, and worksheets for documenting production activities.  2. 	Difficulty in Identifying Critical Documents and core processes: Misclassification of documents as non-critical could lead to oversight in reviews for those actually requiring periodic updates. Mitigation action: all core process will always be maintained using the Process overview list established a spart of change control CMID70.   When a new process is established/updated/discontinued, associated procedure metadata in eQMS record will be updated with periodic review requirements and responsibilities. What is more each of document in the Document Management collection will be marked if periodic review is required and it will be linked to the core process if applicable. Refer to change actions  3. 	Unclear responsibilities:   currently SOP Document management does not provide instruction who is responsible for document revision. eQMS setting currently notifies the document author one month before revision deadline. However several document authors are not process owners, but people who transfer and do the first document update document during software implementation in 2021. To mitigate the risk, document collection will be updated with information on who should be responsible for periodic reviews. Refer to change action 3. Impact on different organisation areas: - 	Personnel and competence – responsibility for periodic review must be clearly defined and reflected in eQMS. Refer to change action 1. Training must be give to dedicated personnel. Refer to change action 4. - 	Documentation – connected procedure with core process within the organisation (already covered as part of CMID70, no additional change action needed). Update SOP5042 must be performed to reflect an update of the periodic review process. Refer to change action 2. - 	eQMS BizzMine must be updated to reflect changes in the process. Refer to change action 3.
Planned implementation data - comment: The change becomes valid when the procedure is updated and all documents which require periodic review will be marked accordingly.

Change Title: Implement desalting/polishing procedure by use of Amicon spin columns in SOP0070
Change from:: SOP0070 only for concentration of antibodies 30K MWCO Amicon Spin column used in SOP0070
Change to: SOP0070 expanded to be relevant for both concentration of antibodies and desalting/polishing of antibodies
Justification for Change: Once in a while, some lower MW impurities can be observed in product. Being able to polish a final product, will reduce the occurrence of having to discard the product and have to start a new production. This will reduce  the material and resources used, while at the same time ensure good customer relationship. Therefore it is considered beneficial to expand on the procedure to include the possibility of polishing/Desalting. For this purpose, Amicon spin column of 50K MWCO will be used while 30K will continue to be used for concentration. Curida Diatec has desalting method in its reportoir (SOP0043 Desalting using PD-10 desalting column), which utilises FPLC instrument to remove low MW impurities. However this method is not applicable for larger volume products, therefore another possibility is needed. The method described in SOP0070m using Amicon spin column is a general method used for concentration of antibodies, but as according to materials information and previous experience with products in-house, can also be used to polish final product by removing smaller impurities as the membrane has a specific MW cut off (impurities below column MWCO size), in this case 30K or 50K cut off.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Amicon spin columns have been used by Curida Diatec for years, and indirectly also been used for polishing. There is a slight risk to the quality of the product by extra handling (sheer stress), low volumes/high concentration (aggregation) and buffer exchange, therefore the procedure will be implemented with a defined volume, short time and few rounds as opposed to the concentration step in SOP0070 (change action 507). Furthermore, neither concentraiton or polishing is to be performed without clear, written instructions from production management (change action 507).  - No risk to compliance or validation is identified.   Impact assessment: - Logistics: ordering of the 50K MWCO Amicon spin column. The columns have already been ordered and are waiting for implementation of process. this material does not require Specification of incoming goods. - Documentation and eQMS: SOP0070 needs to be updated to reflect method of polishing (change action 507). Worksheet for polishing needs to be implemented, consider updating existing worksheet (Doc0070.1) or make a new one for polishing (change action 508 ) - Training: Training module for concentration must be updated (change action 509).  Operators to perform this method must be trained prior to executing the method (change action 510) - HSE: no impact on HSE, as the material is same as previously used, no specifc HSE impact is identified
Planned implementation data - comment: Change to be completed as quickly as possible to be able to utilize this method.

Change Title: Implementation of Roche IsoStrip Isotyping Kit for Quality control of cell banks. Discontinuation of using Thermo Scientific™ Pierce Rapid Antibody Isotyping Kit
Change from:: Thermo Scientific™ Pierce Rapid Antibody Isotyping Kit is used for isotype determination as a one off step in cell bank quality control
Change to: Roche IsoStrip Isotyping Kit will be used for isotype determination as a one off step in cell bank quality control. Use of Thermo Scientific™ Pierce Rapid Antibody Isotyping Kit will be discontinued.
Justification for Change: Isotyping is a critical part of the Quality Control process for cell bank production. Over the past year, multiple isotyping analyses have produced results that led to non-conformances, either because the outcomes did not meet expected criteria or due to the appearance of multiple bands on the isotyping cassettes. These issues are documented in NC226 and NC306 and involve a total of 10 failed isotyping tests, including 5 retests conducted to confirm initial results. Currently the QC test is executed using Thermo Scientific™ Pierce Rapid Antibody Isotyping Kit. The feasibility study DEV166 demonstrated that these kits can give unspecific results for several in house monoclonal antibodies, probably due to unspecific cross reaction between antibodies used in assay and tested in house monoclonal antibodies. 2 different commercially available kits were evaluated as part of DEV166 feasibility study, Roche IsoStrip and Bio-Rad Mouse Monoclonal Antibody Isotyping Test Kit. Both assays found be robust and  suitable for its use. Roche IsoStrip was selected to be implemented as a replacement for currently used Thermo Scientific™ Pierce Rapid Antibody Isotyping KitThe Roche IsoStrip gave consistence results across all tested commercial antibodies controls and in house antibodies. Additionally, the signal strength for IgM antibodies appears stronger for  this assay than the signal observed with the currently used assay. In addition reagent availability and lead time were better Roche IsoStrip when compare to BioRad assay.Roche IsoStrip (Cat. No. 11 493 027 001) utilises lateral flow technique where latex beads are bearing anti-mouse kappa and anti-mouse lambda antibodies, which will react with any mouse monoclonal antibody regardless of its isotype. The basis of the kit is the isotyping strip itself, which bears immobilized bands of goat anti-mouse antibodies corresponding to each of the common mouse antibody isotypes (IgG1, IgG2a, IgG2b, IgG3, IgM, and IgA) and to the kappa and lambda light chains. Both sides of the strip also bear a positive-control band, which indicates that the antibody-coated beads have travelled up the strip.

Change Title: Update routine for calibration of pipettes
Change from:: Pipettes sent for maintenance every 6 months.   If a pipette does not pass a calibration “AS FOUND”, an NC needs to be initiated.
Change to: Pipettes sent for maintenance every 12 months.  If a pipette does not pass a calibration “AS FOUND” two consecutive calibrations, an NC needs to be initiated and the pipette will be evaluated for whether it should be discarded or not.
Justification for Change: Pipettes at Curida Diatec are used for multiple purposes, such as In-process, QC and formulation applications. None of the applications (SDS-PAGE, ELISA, vialling etc) require the accuracy maintained with the ISO 8655-2 standard, which is the standard service supplier VWR applies to the pipette calibration. It is not necessary to maintain this level of quality with a 6 month frequency.  To reduce resources used on pipette calibration, non-critical incidents and non-conformances this change aims to better reflect the frequency of maintenance with the applications the pipettes are used for. .
Impacted areas of the organization and r: impact assessment: - production: Will be less time when half of the pipettes are unavailable due to calibration which is more flexible for production. Pipettes may be in a state that would cause them to fail as found" at calibration service for a longer period of time. However this test measures at a very small scale that would not impact the use of pipettes in production. - QC: Current QC methods where pipettes are used: SDS Page, ELISA, Mycoplasma, Isotyping, Western Blot. These QC analyses are based on a antibody reactivity with specific antigen binding and are primarily qualitative in nature. All the QC tests (except Isotyping) use a previously approved batch as a standard to compare results with which would be affected similarly if the pipette was slightly off, thus giving the same final result of the QC for any QC requiring comparison of OD. Isotyping is strictly based on reactivity, a slight difference in pipette volume will not change the outcome of the test.  - logistics: This would lighten the logistics around pipettes since they would be sent to calibration at a lower frequency. - equipment: Pipettes maintain their integrity and function as long as they are stored properly and used regularly. - documentation: SOP4014 and 3038 need to be updated with the new calibraiton frequency and instructions on initiating NC handling (change action 495) - Training: all operators handling pipettes need to be informed of the changes and how that impacts their work (change action 505)   risk assessment and mitigating actions - Reducing pipette calibration frequency could potentially lead to undetected drift in pipette accuracy, which may impact results subtly over time. However, as none of the processes require ISO 8655-2 precision, the impact is minimal for current applications. Pipettes will remain in good condition if stored properly and used regularly. The calibrations will serve as a yearly checkup for the pipettes.   Risk assessment regarding non-conformance reports - Changing to only register non-conformance reports when a pipette fails two consecutive times will reduce the amount of time spent on eQMS work. It is expected that pipettes will occasionally fail the initial test as-found by VWR before calibration. A non-conformance was registered to keep track of this in case a pipette would fail multiple times. Currently the super-user of pipettes will be registering all calibrations for pipettes in a spreadsheet which already keeps track of how many times a pipette has failed. The additional non-conformance prior to any necessary action as would be the case for consecutive failed-as-found is seen as superfluous as the conclusion would always be to wait and see how the pipette performs at the next calibration. "
Planned implementation data - comment: Change is considered beneficial to the organisation and is approved for execution

Change Title: Improving document control procedure and collection in Bizzmine
Change from:: 1. The new or updated procedure is distributed to everyone in a department or everyone at Curida Diatec. 2. Cross-reference to documents included within the document it self.  3. Worksheets contain a section to verify the version, validity date, and training of the corresponding procedure 4. No link to process or process owning department or SME for the process. 5. No direct overview of pending CAPAs and change actions related to the procedure. 6. When a document is approved by QA it will become valid 24 hours after approval. 7. A printed copy of the document may be placed in the lab as an authorized copy, and production management is responsible for keeping the SOPs up to date.
Change to: 1. Procedure may be distributed to everyone, or to department or a functional group.  2. Cross reference to documents included in the meta-data to the document, linked in the Bizzmine collection. 3. Worksheets will not need to contain a section to verify the version, validity date, and training of the corresponding procedure. 4. Will link the relevant internal process to the collection in BizzMine. (thus also a SME, functional groups, and owning department will be defined). 5. Linked with CAPA and Change action collection for easy overview of pending improvements/updates to the document.  6. The valid date of the document must be manually defined when QA approves the document.  7. A printed copy of the document may be placed in the lab as an authorized copy, process owner /SME is responsible for keeping the SOP up to date. It shall be visible in the metadata if it exists a authorized copy in the lab and a notification system shall be in place to ensure old versions are replaced when a new version is published.
Justification for Change: 1. Currently, all SOP in category: 0XXX: Production, 1XXX Analysis, 2XXX Reagents, 4XXXX Equipment are distributed among the whole production team. The production department consists of several internal processes, and often personnel is only trained in some of them. The current document distribution process results in a person who is not trained in the procedure/process receiving notification for R&U training for each document update. Several operators currently have several not relevant notifications for document revision or training. This causes a challenge to prioritize correct document R&U training and creates a risk of missing document updates, therefore, performing the task according to the obsoleted procedure. This situation can be especially stressful for newly hired employees who receive notification for R&U training of over 200 procedures and worksheets on the first days. The functional groups will be defined in the crosslinked internal process overview, and it is the responsibility of the immediate manager to place employees in correct functional groups according to their job descriptions and to current tasks and responsibilities.  2. Currently, all cross reference to different procedures/worksheets is included within the document. When some of the document is obsoleted/changed a name/being implemented, its difficult to track all the procedures which have to be updated to remove/update/add the cross-reference. This results in several reference sections are not updated correctly. 3. The section for filling the corresponding document version number and validity date was established to ensure that the person executing the task is trained in the current procedure (used) version. Currently, all employees' training is maintained using the eQMS system, and personnel receives notification for R&U training whenever a procedure is updated. Electronic training records are robust and allow easy tracking if the employee is trained in the relevant procedure. 4. A new collection for internal process overview is implemented (CMID70), and an action is to link with the document management collection. However, the linking should be performed in the document management collection. This linking will give info regarding owning department, SME or process owner, and functional group / distribution list.  5. Often, more than one pending CAPA/change actions require an update of this same document. Since employees are not always aware of different pending CAPA/change actions, several minor document updates are executed in a short time. This approach negatively impacts internal resources. By crosslinking the relevant collections in BizzMine, a clear overview of pending actions will be visible in the document record in BizzMine. This will also help reviewers and QA when checking if changes to the document have been performed in accordance with these actions.  6. Currently, a new document version is released 24 hours after approval. This results that often two documents are valid on this same date (when a new version is released in the afternoon). Some times it is needed to have the procedure valid from the start of the next day, and some times it is necessary with a longer training period before publication.  7. There is currently no overview of which document has a printed copy in the lab. This can lead to not replacing a copy when updated. This change will ensure the copy in the lab is replaced when an update has been done.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risk assessment: Risk associated with proposed change 1:Procedure incorrectly assigned to different internal processes results in employees not receiving notification for document updates and performing an internal task using the obsoleted procedure.  The new procedure is not assigned to any process when established, which results that no one within an organization will receive notification for new document release.  Current personnel is wrongly assigned to function within BizzMine, resulting in the employee not receiving notification for document updates and performing internal tasks using obsoleted procedures.  A newly hired employee wrongly assigned to function within BizzMine results in the employee will not receive notification for document updates and perform an internal task using the obsoleted procedure.  A procedure might cover several processes across more than one functional group. This might pose a risk that not all the functional groups are added to the distribution list.   Mitigation actionsAll the processes have been mapped and assigned to a defined process (ref CMID70 change action 365). All documents in the document management collection will be connected to the correct process (Change action 491). They are already linked with the process (performed when processes was first defined in CMID 70), though the linking shall be reversed. A list with all Processes and their associated and currently linked SOPs was made when performing CMID70. A copy of that list will be used to track the action of reversing the linking of procedures to a process. The process owner or SME will verify if all associated procedures are correctly assigned to the process.   The SOP Document Management must be updated to provide instructions to connect newly established procedures with the relevant process. In addition, the BizzMine collection 1-Document management will be connected with a list of all internal processes. When a new document is established, the author will have to select a relevant process from the list or establish a new process in the list. (Change action 490). Defining the distribution list before the document is published is a mandatory field in the collection.  All current employees will be assigned the correct functions based on their training and responsibilities. A supervisor will provide information to QA (responsible for maintaining the organization chart in BizzMine) on how to assign employees within an organization. Ref change action 492.   The training module are connected with the collection Internal process", therefore, they will receive information about which function in Organisation Chart they have to be included. When a person starts training in a new module, they will be assigned to a new function in the organization chart. The first step in all the job specific training module is the theoretical training where the trainee needs to verify that they have R&U the relevant procedures and documents. If they are not put in the correct functional group in the organization chart they will not be able to perform this action. No mitigation needed.   Add instruction in the SOP for both the author and QA to verify the correct distribution list before approval.     Risk associated with proposed change 2:Wrong documents will be cross-referenced during changing process, which can result in the wrong procedure can be used or consulted.  If not all procedures are cross referenced correctly it might be missed when searching for the connected procedures.   Mitigation actions: The 1-Document Control collection in BizzMine has to be updated with a table for cross reference procedure. All the procedures/worksheets will have to be manually updated and the reference section has to be removed. During this update, all cross-references will be checked by the process owner/SME to which the procedure belongs.  Having the referenced procedures as linked meta data is a risk mitigation from the "as is" state. It will be much less likely to miss referenced procedures when they are linked in the collection that if you need to search manually in the documents.      Risk associated with proposed change 3:Operators execute the task using superseeded document versions, which can result in the procedure being conducted incorrectly. This can happen especially when several documents are updated at this same time, and the operator will not have time to properly practice R&U training.   Mitigation actions: If current employees will be assigned to the correct functions (process) in eQMS system based on their obtained training. Employees will receive a notification only for relevant procedures, which results in a decreasing number of pending R&U training for each employee.    Risk associated with proposed change actions 4: no risk was identified. Mitigation actions:  NA Risk associated with actions 5:  no risk identified. Mitigation actions:  NA Risk associated with actions 6: If the date is picked to be the next day, personnel might not be aware of the new version before performing a procedure. Risk of not performing a task in accordance with procedure. Mitigation action: There shall be instruction in SOP regarding updates that are major or need training has to have sufficient training period from approval of procedure to it becoming valid. If a shorter training period is needed (i.e. updated procedure is needed next day), it must be justified in the change log and the author is responsible for performing the necessary training for all relevant personnel before they use it.  Risk associated with actions 7: This action is risk reducing, thus no risks associated with this action.     The following shall be updated in the Bizzmine collection 1- Document management (Change action 492): Link the internal process overview collection to select the relevant process (ref change action 368). Link to the 1-Document collection where all referenced documents shall be listed with the header "References". Link to the CAPA and change action collection for overview of pending actions to the document. Change the distribution list to not be pre-filled so it can be chosen by the author in accordance with relevant process.  Remove the organization item "verification". Remove the need to verify the document from the process flow of document approval. Change the publication date (the time the document becomes valid) from 24h after approval to be a date chosen by the document approver. Add a tickbox or similar for defining if a copy of the procedure is located in the lab for use off line.      The following shall be updated in SOP 5042 - Document management (Change action 490): Description of required meta data in the document collection. New data added is: Link process from internal process collection. Link all referenced documents.  Instructions on who should be assigned the tasks of document author and reviewer. Instructions on who shall be on the distribution list: To find the relevant function groups from the process overview. The author will be responsible for defining the distribution list and QA is responsible for approving the chosen distribution list. Instructions on criteria for setting the date of publication of a SOP (training period). Add instructions on how to handle printed copies to be kept in lab for use off line, and define the responsibilities regarding this document. Add descriptions on how to document this in the meta data in bizzmine collection.  WIN 5042.1 and WIN 5042.2 shall be updated according to the changes in SOP and bizzmine collection"
Planned implementation data - comment: The change becomes valid when SOP5042 is updated and the BizzMine collection is updated and validated.

Change Title: Change the content of product specification (PS)
Change from:: Following information is included in product specification. Product specification is used for detailing specification of product as agreed by customer. Same document is also used as production instruction for production team.    Product specification includes the followin - Statement of purpose - Project information: Description of project, Product no, Product name, Clone, Isotype and species - Manufacturing and composition information: Production method, Medium, Serum, Other additives, Purification method Material, Purification method pH, Total amount, Buffer, Preservatives, Post manufacturing filtration, Aliquotation, Storage conditions at Curida Diatec, Other information - Quality Control: Sop no, Description of test, Method of test, Specification - Label information: Curida Diatec AS and/or Curida Diatec’s logo, Product name and clone, Product number, Batch number, Concentration, Volume, Production date (mm/yyyy), Azide concentration, 0,2 µm filtered, - Other information as specified by customer - List of documents to be included with product shipment: CoA, Other documents as requested by customer - Information on customer, project number and agreement effective date.
Change to: One document, divided in two sections, one for agreeing on product specifications with customer and the other as an internal document with specific details needed for producing the product.    Part 1: material specification, to be shared with the customer for agreement and signature. Part 1 is the relevant part of the PS for product release. - Statement of purpose - Project information, including description of project, project number, product number and name, clone, isotype and species - Technical information, detailing production platform, medium, serum, other additives, purification material and pH, final buffer, preservatives, post manufacturing filtration, vialling,  storage and transport conditions and other information. - Quality control: list of Quality control for product, including parameter, method and specification limits - Documentation and label information - Signatures for approval of document for both Curida Diatec and Customer   Part 2: internal part of the document, production order - Project information: same as for part 1 - Upstream: thaw and expansion phase, including nitrogen location of cell vial, details of expansion medium including serum and additives if applicable as well as possibility to add other information. - Upstream production phase, number and type of production units, details on medium for both cell side and outside, including serum and additives, estimated final harvest number and possibility for other information. - Upstream: in process control of concentration, including type of IPC, suggested reference batch and other applicable information. - Downstream: purification and dialysis. Information required is purification material, elution pH, final buffer with instructions on including recipe/sop ref if not standard buffer), and possibility for other information regarding downstream purification and dialysis. - Downstream: final formulation. Details on preservatives, filtration, vialling and storage conditions, with possibility for other information added. - Quality control, same information as in part 1, in addition ref to SOP number for each method, and suggested ref batch as well as other information. - Label information: same as pt.1 - Project number on every page to ensure traceability.
Justification for Change: The revised document structure introduces greater flexibility by dividing the content into two parts. This allows for the inclusion of non-essential details, such as last harvest numbers and reference batches, without causing additional work when production adjustments are required. The updated product specification will streamline internal processes, enabling faster reviews and minimizing the risk of documentation errors.  Part 1 focuses on key compliance-related parameters, which are presented to the customer and serve as the foundation for the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) and product release. Non-essential details are excluded from this section. Part 2 contains more detailed information relevant to production, such as suggested reference batches and harvest numbers, which are important for operational purposes but do not affect product compliance or specifications. This section is further organized by process to ensure clarity and relevance. Currently, some of this information is included in the weekly production plan, leading to errors when information is spread across multiple documents. By consolidating all necessary details into one comprehensive document, which follows the entire production process, the potential for errors will be significantly reduced. The new format will also improve communication with customers, providing them with clearer, more concise product specifications and quality details. At the same time, it simplifies and clarifies instructions for the production team, ensuring consistency from start to finish.  This change supports continual improvement and is initiated by CAPA05, CAPA76 and CAPA86.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risk associated with change control: There is not risk associated with the change control. No critical data will be omitted, and all necessary quality control/formulation information will still be provided in updated template to be agreed upon with the customer. The template will still allow to add information specifically requested by customer to ensure that customer requests and instructions are accommodated.   Impacted areas of organisation: Documentation: Following documents must be updated- DOC5011.1 Product Specification (see change action 466)  Training:- No training module need to be updated - Group training has to be provided for several groups (change action 467): Training Group – Production personnel responsible for preparing PS, performing technical revision and issuing Certificate of Analysis. Training Group – Production team, highlighting the changes in the new template and how to use it to produce according to specification. Training Group – QA personnel responsible for batch release Training Group – BD personnel – inform how new PS looks like and what information is included  The notification letter must be sent to the customer. The change is not defined as critical (as defined in individual quality agreements) and serves only an informational purpose. Therefore, it does not need to be sent within the time frame defined in the quality agreement (change action 468)
Planned implementation data - comment: change approved for execution, as this is a minor change it can be implemented after customer notification has been sent out.

Change Title: Implement SOP for desalting procedure
Change from:: SOP071 not included in current QMS system.   SOP071 being specific to cell line S4H9
Change to: Include SOP071 to current QMS system (Bizzmine).  Update SOP071 to current standards. Change the SOP071 to only concern the method itself not a specific cell line
Justification for Change: The method described in SOP071 is a versatile FPLC method, that was previously used for buffer exchanging S4H9 to final buffer to ensure minimal use of final buffer due to cost. As S4H9 has not been produced by Diatec since before implementation of eQMS Bizzmine, the procedure was not transferred to the new QMS system.  Even though SOP071 is specifically aimed at changing buffers in the final product of S4H9, the method described in the procedure can be applied as a polishing step as well, without much adjustments. The general method of desalting is a frequently used Size exclusion method in the industry to polish affinity purified proteins as it removes small molecular weight impurities (such as salts) from the sample, ensuring higher purity.  It is considered feasible at present times to re-implement and adjust the method to be used in a general way, as it currently is needed to polish an antibody that has lower purity than its specification and this method has been identified as a possible solution. Furthermore, it will expand the polishing/re-processing possibilities of Curida Diatec, ensuring better service to our customers, and lower cost.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risk assessment: - Desalting is a very gentle purificaton method and is therefore unlikely to impact the final product in other ways than increased purity. However as a precaution, the use of any re-processing methods is discussed with the customer before utilising it towards their final product. Instructions on contacting the customer will be added to the SOP (change action 459). - No risk to compliance or validation is identified. Impact assessment: - Logistics: no impact on logistics, as the column needed for this process is located in-house and still within its expiry.  - Documentation and eQMS: SOP071 must be brought in our eQMS, and adjusted to make it more general for desalting (change action 459) - Training: Training module must be made for this procedure (change action 469).  Operators to perform this method must be trained prior to executing the method (change action 470) - HSE: no impact on HSE, as there are no specific hazzards connected to this method.
Planned implementation data - comment: Re-implementation of Desalting producedure into Diatec QMS will expand our customer offering and polising of final product.

Change Title: Implementation of Cl1000 flask with a new membrane
Change from:: Membrane material WCL1000-3 flasks: Akzo 150M membrane Manufacturer: DWK Life Science  Supplier: Fisher Scientific and VWR
Change to: Membrane material in WCL1000-3 flasks: new, marginally thicker gas membrane (Manufacturer has increased membrane thickness but has not provided specific details on the increase).Manufacturer: DWK Life Science  Supplier: Fisher Scientific  and VWR
Justification for Change: Impact assessment: minor.  There is no change to the upstream production harvesting regime, supplementation, or medium exchange, the in-process control measures will remain unchanged. There is no impact on regulatory or compliance aspects: the same CL1000 flask will be used in the manufacturing process, with the same catalogue number, supplied by the same suppliers, and manufactured by the same company in the same manufacturing site.   Justification The WHEATON CELLine™ Flask (CL1000) utilises a dual membrane system designed for high-density cell culture, reducing maintenance and simplifying harvesting. This system features two key membranes: a dialysis membrane and a gas-permeable membrane. The dialysis membrane enables nutrient exchange between the bulk media compartment and the cell culture compartment, ensuring that cells receive the necessary nutrients during cultivation. The gas-permeable membrane maintains appropriate oxygen and carbon dioxide levels, which are critical for optimal cell growth. Over the past 12 months, quality issues with the CL1000 flasks were reported multiple times, and documented as nonconformances (refer to NC327, 310, 309, 298, 250, 235, 202). The primary issues observed were leakage and an increased incidence of infections. The root cause was traced to defects in the CL1000 flask membrane, which was investigated as a supplier complaint with both Fisher Scientific (primary supplier, SCR070) and VWR (backup supplier, SCR071). DWK Life Sciences Limited, the manufacturer of the CL1000 flask, was also contacted as part of the investigation. The leakage issue was attributed to a defect in the gas-permeable membrane, which was found to have variable quality. Specifically, irregularities in the thickness of the membrane were identified as the cause of the leakage. As a result, DWK Life Sciences Limited discontinued the previous membrane supplier and selected a new supplier with a more consistent quality standard. The new membrane is thicker to address the identified issues. DWK Life Sciences Limited evaluated the new membrane’s impact on cell proliferation and viability and found no significant differences compared to the previously used membrane in 21-day tests. (see attached email communication). The CL1000 flask with the old membrane will be discontinued, therefore Curida Diatec has to implement CL1000 flaks with the new membrane.   Risk assessment: The primary risk associated with the change in gas membrane thickness was its potential impact on the upstream production process, specifically the cell proliferation and quantity/quality of antibodies produced. The flask's performance was initially evaluated by the manufacturer in a 21-day run (see Attachment 1). Additionally, an in-house feasibility study was conducted, where the upstream production process was performed in parallel using CL1000 flasks with both the new and old membranes (refer to the DEV164 study intermediary report, version 2.0). The performance of the flask with the new membrane was assessed using two different in-house clones—one with a high antibody yield and one with a low yield. Parameters such as cell growth, viability, product quantity, and product quality (purity) were measured, analyzed, and compared with the results from the currently used flask and membrane. No significant differences were observed in any of the measured parameters, for both tested clones. The amount of antibodies produced and their quality were comparable to those produced using the old membrane. Similarly, cell growth and viability remained consistent under both tested conditions. What is more, no leakage or infection was observed during this study for Cl1000 flasks with new membrane.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impact on organisation: Production process: The feasibility study demonstrated that there is no need to adjust the upstream production process in any way. Harvest regime, medium exchange, IPCs remain unchanged. No further action is needed. Documentation: As the catalogue number of products remains the same, no need for an update of production procedures, worksheets or product specifications. Logistic: no change to material specification is required. The material will have the same catalogue number and will be supplied by the approved suppliers. The manufacturer of the flask also remains the same. All the remaining CL000 flasks with old membrane must be removed from the facility when the 3-month notification period ends, and cannot be used in the production process after that. They can be discarded or used for R&D purposes. See change action 1. Training: as the material and production process remains unchanged, no additional action is required. Customer notification: the quality agreements were evaluated to assess if the change notification should be sent. As CL1000 is considered critical material, change has to be communicated to the customer. Customers: For customers with specified notification periods ranging from 1 to 3 months, we have secured sufficient stock of CL1000 flasks with the old membrane to ensure continuous supply during the notification period (if necessary). For customers who require only notification without a specified timeframe, we will proceed with the use of the new membrane once the current stock is exhausted. Refer to change action 02.
Planned implementation data - comment: NA

Change Title: Implementing Cedex Bioanalyser for Glucose analysis & discontinuing Accu-Check Aviva strips (SOP1050)
Change from:: Justification for major: as the change could have potential impact on production runs as the results of glucose concentration measurements are used to adjust the parameters of production. Glucose measurements for in-process control in upstream production at the Diagnostic site at Curida Diatec, is performed using Accu-Chek Aviva strip test as described in SOP-1050.
Change to: Implement the off the shelf Cedex Bioalayser test method for glucose measurements in solutions that cover the following range:• GLC2B:  0.02 - 7.50 g/L (0.111 - 41.6 mmol/L)
Justification for Change: The manufacturer of the Accu-Chek Aviva have discontinued this meter. Although the test strips for this test are still available, the meter being discontinued no longer make this a sustainable test method that allows its continued use as an IPC check to support upstream production activities. Additionally, this test kit is designed for use in the measurement of blood glucose and has been adapted for the purpose of IPC measurements as described in SOP 1050. The accuracy of this test method is also not considered optimal, due to this reason. Based on historical monitoring data the range for measurement for glucose appears to be in the range of 1-5 g/L, therefore the testing range offered by Cedex for analysis is suitable for this application.  The Cedex Bioanalyser test method is a standardised protocol for glucose measurement offered by the vendor. The method has demonstrated in- run and inter-run precision from the vendor at 3 levels within the working range of the assay. This method is more suited for applications of IPC measurements. Potential disadvantages of switching to using the Cedex Bioanalyser for glucose measurements is that this test method will require more time to execute compared to the simpler Accu-Chek Aviva method. However, the execution time for this test is considered acceptable for use for IPC measurements. A preliminary comparability assessment fo the current test method vs the test method by Roche Diagnostics (Cedex Bioanalyser) was performed prior to this change and the results are available in Attachment 01 to this CMID. This assessment shows that the two test methods are sufficiently comparable to proceed with with this change without unexpected unfavourable consequences to the production process.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: The glucose measurement test is not used for batch release activities and only used to support in-process control of the upstream production activities. The results from the glucose measurements are monitored and used to adjust the media flow rates to maintain the required growth rate of the cells for production. Change Title: Update of customer complaint process: risk-based approach and digitalisation
Change from:: The customer complaint is registered using a paper form. ID numbers of complaints assigned manually by the Quality department. Customer complaints are classified in 3 categories critical, major and minor. Lack of definition of customer complaint.
Change to: The customer complaint is registered using an electronic form in BizzMine. Customer complaint unique number will be assigned automatically by the software. Customer complaint will be classified into 2 categories: low and high risk. In addition, compliant reoccurrence will be evaluated. Definition of complaint included in the procedure.
Justification for Change: This change is part of phase 3 of the BizzMine eQMS implementation at the Diatec Diagnostic Production facility. Refer to Change Management CMR204 “Change from a paper based to an electronic Quality Management system (eQMS). The new classification is aligned with already used classification in Non-conformance management process (refer to SOP5034) which allow unify classification among different process within an organisation. What is more will allow better allocation of different resources.   Positive side Digitalisation: Reduce time on administrative tasks related to the management of customer complaints like document scanning and archiving  Clear overview and easier training of the customer complaint status and trends  Possibilities of signing record electronically  Improve audit readiness  Reduce a risk of losing record   Risk-based approach Provide better allocation of resources based on the risk associated with a complaint.     Negative side Implementation of the module will require time and resources from Quality and Business development department. Impacted area of the organisation: Documentation – refer to change action  439,440,441. Training – refer to change action 442 and 443 BizzMine – refer to change action 437 and 438 Customers – the notification of the customer is not necessary based on current status of signed Quality Agreements. Infrastructure – no HMS – no   Risk assessment: The risk associated with using eQMS was evaluated as a part of CMR204. The risk analysis was conducted and documented (reference QRM134_Change from paper based to electronic QMS). Moreover, all new collections will be internally validated to prove their functionality (including electronic signature, data designed, document flow, permissions etc.) The validation will be carried out according to SOP5048.   Identified risk: Deleting information /records. The risk is mitigated by defining different permission levels. Only the selected user will have permission to delete records from the collection. If the record is accidentally deleted, it can be restored from Trash bin by Admin or BizzMine support. Overview of all deleted records can be found in Trash bin for the collection. Refer to change action 437. Untrained/unauthorized persons will perform a change in a documented record. Different permission levels (for reading, editing, creating, approving etc.) will be established and tested during collection validation. Only personal with authorized access will be able to edit or create a record in the collection. Refer to change action 437. Form filled incorrectly due to lack of competence or not clear instruction. This risk will be mitigated by updating relevant procedure, establish working instruction, and provide proper training. Refer to change action: 439,440,442,443 Wrong form used to register customer complaint – the user use paper form instead of electronic. Mitigate by action 441. Customer complaints reported in paper form will not be followed, completed or archived correctly. Refer to change action 444. Malfunction of established collection – the error in the collection can lead to the following issues: - The record is sent to the wrong person / wrong step – a high probability of the error as similar errors were experienced before (NC243), therefore, the updated collection will be validated. Refer to change action 437. - The wrong person can edit the record – a high probability of the error as similar errors were experienced before (NC243), therefore, the collection will be validated. Change Title: Update of supplier complaint process: Workflow, responsibilities and implementation in BizzMine
Change from:: Supplier complaints are registered and processed using a paper form (“DOC 5036.1 Supplier Complaint Report”) that is printed, signed, scanned, and archived manually. The completed forms are archived on the F-drive.   Investigation, root-cause analysis and CAPA performed internally and documented in the supplier complaint form. No description of effectiveness check or requesting a corrective action from the supplier. No description of handling the supplier complaint if not resolved by the supplier.  Responsibilities: Logistics team is always the caseworker. Process owned by Operations and Logisitcs department Noone has technical responsibility for ensuring the supplier response and actions.
Change to: Supplier complaints shall be registered, completed, signed, and archived in the established eQMS Software, BizzMine. The record for each supplier complaint will be linked with the supplier list.   Responsibilities: The Supplier complaint process shall be owned by the Quality department. Quality department will be responsible for maintaining the SOP. Registration of a new supplier complaint can be done by anyone.  The caseworker can, in addition to logistics team, be anyone with expert knowledge of or have a direct relationship with the supplied material/service.  Department manager or SME is responsible for technical verification of the complaint and supplier response.  QA is responsible for approval of the supplier corrective action plan, execution and effectiveness check.    Procedure:  All supplier complaints must originate from one or more Non-conformance(s). All investigations shall be performed in the NC. If the investigation concludes that the fault is with the supplier, all corrective actions and root-cause assessments shall be performed by the supplier. QA will approve the supplier corrective action plan, action execution, and effectiveness check. Description of when a supplier corrective action shall be requested Description of when an effectiveness check of supplier corrective action is needed. Description on how to proceed if a supplier does not perform actions in accordance with our claims and requirements, or if the corrective action taken proves to not be effective.  Definitions of quality impact (classification) and reoccurrence aligned with the SOP5034 Non-conformance.
Justification for Change: The transfer of Supplier complaint process into the established eQMS Software, BizzMine, is a part of the ongoing electronic QMS implementation. This change is part of phase 3 of CMR204 “Change from a paper based to an electronic Quality Management system (eQMS)”.   Advantages of changes: *Implementing an electronic system for supplier complaint handling:Simplification of the supplier complaint handling process reduces time and resources used on document handling.  Provides a clear status of each supplier complaint registered  Use of electronic signatures  Improves visibility of registered complaints for each supplier, and availability of information used for supplier monitoring and re-qualification   Reduced risk of losing paper documents  Responsible personnel with responsibilities in the supplier complaint process will be notified of new tasks to be performed by them.   * Changes in responsibilitiesResponsibilities align with structure and resources in the organisation.  Technical verification will ensure better quality and control of the supplier chain.  QA approval of supplier corrective action plan, execution and effectiveness check will ensure suppliers are continuously monitored and followed up to prevent reoccurrence of non-conformances and will improve the supplier management process.     * Other changes to the workflow and SOP:Definitions of quality impact (classification) and reoccurrence will be aligned with already used definitions in “SOP5034 Non-conformance”.   All supplier complaints originate from the NC-process will increase traceability and tracking of non-conformances related to suppliers and detection of reoccurring events.   Requesting investigation and corrective action plan from suppliers and evaluation of effectiveness is aligned with industry standards for supplier management.      Disadvantages of the change: Implementation of a new collection in BizzMine will require time and resources from the Quality department.   Impacted areas of the organisation: Documentation: SOP for supplier complaint must be updated. Paper form for supplier complaint report must be obsoleted (ref change action 426 and 427). Training: Module for supplier complaint must be created. Personnel must be trained. (ref change action 428 and 429). BizzMine: A new collection must be created, validated and implemented (ref change action 425. The existing collection Suppliers must be updated to include the linked supplier complaint (ref change action 430). Customers: This change does not impact any customer or quality agreements.  HSE: This change has no impact on HSE.  Validation/qualification: The new BizzMine collection and workflow must be validated (ref. change action 425). Product: This change does not affect product quality.  Other department: This change will affect the logistics department. The changes will be communicated by training (ref change action 429). The transfer of process ownership from logistics to quality will affect those two departments. Description of process owner and SME will be updated in the internal process overview collection (ref change action 432).   Risk assessment: The risk associated with using eQMS was evaluated as a part of CMR204. The risk analysis was conducted and documented (reference QRM134_Change from paper based to electronic QMS). The risks associated with implementing an electronic system for supplier complaint handling are: There is a risk that ID numbers for records go missing if an error occurs in the registration step in BizzMine (ref NC301 and NC108). This risk will be mitigated by making the first step of the workflow to be very simple to minimize the risk of generating an error in this step.   Untrained/unauthorized personnel performing a change to a documented record or deleting information. Mitigated by different permission levels in BizzMine (for reading, editing, creating, approving etc.). This will be validated in accordance with SOP5048 and DOC5048.1. Refer to change action 425.  Loss of information: all information is stored on external servers, maintained by a software provider and can be restored if needed. BizzMine is an approved Curida Diatec supplier. No further action will be taken.  Form filled incorrectly due to lack of competence or not clear instruction. This risk will be mitigated by updating relevant procedure, establish working instruction, and provide proper training. Refer to change action: 426, 428 and 429.   Wrong form used to register supplier complaint – the user use paper form instead of electronic. Mitigate by Obsoleting the paper form, ref change action 429.  Ongoing supplier complaints reported in paper form will not be followed up, completed or archived correctly. Refer to change action 431.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: The Supplier Complaint Collection will be a process workflow that consists of the following steps: Registration of supplier complaint and assignment of a caseworker. (By registrant with NC) Description of complaint, our claims, and request for supplier corrective action if necessary. (By caseworker) Generation of pdf report to be sent to the supplier. (By logistics/or other relevant personnel) Investigation/supplier communication/ documentation and evaluation of supplier response. (By caseworker) Technical verification. (By relevant department manager or SME) Approve supplier corrective action plan (if Supplier Corrective Action was requested). (By QA) Approve supplier corrective action execution and effectiveness check (If required). (By QA) Approve and close the Supplier complaint. (By QA)   The collection in BizzMine eQMS software will be tested and validated in accordance with SOP 5048 and documented using DOC 5048.1.
Planned implementation data - comment: The change will be valid when action 426 is executed and the training is provided.
