Change Title: Implementation of Stability Study Management Program
Change from:: No stability studies currently      being performed in-house on products produced using Curida Diatec owned      clones. No dedicated procedure providing instructions on how to conduct a stability study and obtain stability data for a shelf-life claim
Change to: Defined procedure and training for conducting stability studies.
Justification for Change: Curida Diatec is a Contract Manufacturing Organization (CMO) that produces products based on customer requests and specifications. Until now, all produced products were released without any stability claims, with customers responsible for defining the shelf life of their products. Each production batch was initiated based on customer-approved specifications, and products were labelled with the customer's name. No stability claim/shelf life was provided on the Certificate of Analysis. If stability data were required, studies were designed based on instructions provided by the customer. This approach was used for products from customer-owned clones and and products from Diatec-owned clones.    Currently, Curida Diatec plans to establish an internal production process to produce antibodies under its own label/mark (reference change control CMID57). Curida Diatec will produce a product that can be sold to multiple customers simultaneously. Therefore, representative stability data need to be obtained to define the shelf life of Curida Diatec's products.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: The scope of this change includes the following: 1. Establish a procedure for conducting a stability study. In addition, prepare a template for the stability study protocol and report. 2.Establish a training module for the personnel responsible for the stability study coordinator 3.Provide job-specific training for personnel responsible for conducting stability studies 4.Establish a stability tracker in Bizzmine. 5.Update SOP 5011 Product Realisation and DOC5011.1 Product specification, ensure that the product specification document has a section to document the requires incubator conditions to ensure that the equipment required for the study are available. 6.Update SOP5037 Out of specification procedure on how to handle OOS and OOT results related to stability studies. Risk Assessment﻿﻿    1. Potential for not having the equipment required (eg. suitable incubator that can be in the range) for a particular stability study from a customer.Mitigation: Update of the DOC5011.2 Product Specification document will need to incude a section for review and documentation of equipment at the suitable conditions on site to perform the activities of the stability study. 2.New responsibilities will be introduced with the implementation of the stability study program. Mitigation: The responsibilities of different personnel must be clearly defined in the procedure to ensure that tasks are performed by the correct personnel (refer to change action 1). Additionally, a job-specific training program must be established to ensure that all personnel coordinating stability studies receive the necessary training (refer to change actions 2 and 3). 3. Potential for timepoint to be missed if the responsible stability coordinator is absent.Mitigation: A Bizzmine tracker will be established showing the timepoints for the various stability timepoints, so that all timepoints can be initiated on time (action 4).
Planned implementation data - comment: The change will be valid when the procedure will be approved.

Change Title: Implementation of HRM system Talentech by Talmundo
Change from:: 1. HR data, including but not limited to, contracts of employment, agreements and personal information are archived in paper form and/or scanned in several personal folders on individual computers. The documents are archived in locked cabinets where only HR has access and in personal folders where only HR and the manager with personnel resposibilities has access. 2. The onboarding process is planned and completed in the eQMS system. All new employees or other personnel that are to be onboarded to the company need a license to the eQMS system to complete onboarding. 3. The template document for performing the Performance Appraisal Dialogue is maintained in the Bizzmine eQMS (Doc 5015.7). The process where the managers and employees download the word file and fill it out before, during and after the dialogue is performed on paper/in a simple word document. The goals for each employee that is agreed on in the Performance Appraisal Dialogue are listed in the document. The document is then signed and archived in individuals’ computers. The routine for following up on agreed goals and deadlines are manual and depending on the managers and employees creating their own systems for keeping the goals/performance on track.
Change to: Use of HRM system: Talentech by Talmundo, with the following modules: 1. HR data, including but not limited to contracts of employment, agreements and personal information are archived in the HRM system, in a secure and structured way at one place. Archiving the HR data in the HRM system guarantees that data is only accessible to the HR department and the manager with employee responsibility. 2. The onboarding process is planned and completed in the HRM system, where there are functions for automatic send-outs and notifications for the hiring manager to prepare the pre-boarding and for completion and follow-up of the onboarding when the new employee has started. The HRM system will also include an offboarding module, to secure all steps to follow when an employee is to leave the company. 3.The performance appraisal dialogue is planned and followed up in the HRM system, where the completed forms are archived in a GDPR approved manner to secure sensitive information of the employees. The goals for each employee agreed on in the Performance Appraisal Dialogue are tracked and followed up in the system and gives the employees and managers better opportunity to get overview and analyse the progress of the development plan.
Justification for Change: The current eQMS based on ISO9001:2015 integrates various HR procedures, including onboarding (part of the training module) and documents for Performance Appraisal Dialogues (Doc 5015.7). While this integration has been functional, the increasing complexity of HR requirements and the need for compliance with GDPR regulations necessitate a specialized approach. A dedicated HRM system that meets GDPR standards and digitalize the process for planning and following up on onboarding and Performance Appraisal Dialogues will better serve our organisation’s needs. Out of scope:Introduction Training, Job specific training and Group training that includes training in quality documents such as SOPs and connected Doc/Worksheets is still to be performed in eQMS Bizzmine, according to SOP 5047 Handling of Training.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: What we would like to achieve with this change: Improved compliance with GDPR:A specialized HRM system ensures that HR data is handled in compliance with GDPR regulations, offering better data protection and privacy management. Enhanced efficiency and accuracy:An HRM system designed specifically for managing HR-/manager tasks will streamline processes such as onboarding and performance appraisals, increasing efficiency and accuracy. The HRM will also include modules for preboarding and offboarding, with lists and tasks to follow up on. This will secure that all tasks are done to create the best employee experience within the company, and be more efficient for the HR department and the hiring manager. Focused functionality:By moving these HR procedures to a dedicated system, the eQMS can better focus on core quality management processes, improving overall effectiveness.   Risk assessment:  Qualification/Validation:No qualification of supplier or validation of system is needed. Justification: The use of the HRM system to store HR data, perform onboarding and Performance Appraisal Dialogues is considered to be low impact to the quality management system. The HRM system will mostly be used for administrative tasks/HR work, and training and competence mapping will still be performed in the eQMS BizzMine. System administrator (HR) will perform an access control to secure that the managers only have access to their listed employees. This will be documented and attached to this change report.  Workload:A new, additional system to learn and work in can cause extra workload for the managers and employees, and lead to resistance to learning a new system.Mitigation: HR will prepare and complete a thorough training session and will work as a support function following up closely to help when needed. Loss of data/Integrity:As of today, HR data and records of Performance Appraisal Dialogues are archived in several folders in paper format, and the digital files/scanned documents are archived in several folders on individuals’ computers. The implementation of a HRM system with dedicated folders for each employee with restricted access according to current GDPR regulations would increase the security of the personal information and sensitive information. This would also secure that all data are stored in one place. The Data Processing Agreement (DPA, see attachment 1) between Curida Diatec and Talentech is in accordance with applicable data protection laws (GDPR) and ensures that personal data is not used in an unlawful manner or that unauthorized parties access the personal data. Therefore, the risk of losing data/information is considered higher in the current system than in the new HRM system.
Planned implementation data - comment: The HRM system is to be implemented step by step, starting with collecting HR data in the system and perform training for all employees within September 2024. The modules for Performance Appraisal Dialogue and Onboarding will be imlemented and be finished for use within October 2024. Inactivation of listed Introduction training, approval of SOPs and voiding of Doc 5015.7 can be performed when System Administrator (HR) have tested the finished modules.

Change Title: Change to use peroxide for cleaning procedures instead of Spor-Klenz
Change from:: Cleaning procedures for LAF bench (SOP4005), refrigerator and cold room (SOP4045), and cleanroom floor (SOP1072) all describe using acid based cleaning detergent (Spor-Klenz).  The supplier for Spor-Klenz have been delayed multiple times, and no stock is currently available for use.
Change to: As Spor-klenz consist in large parts of peroxide (a detergent also used for cleaning procedures) a change to use peroxide will be issued where Spor-Klenz was previously used.
Justification for Change: Continous lack of available stock of spor-klenz since June 2024 lead to a non conformance registered (Ref. NC315). Since this acid based reagent consists in large parts of peroxide, the peroxide reagent used for other maintenance procedures is considered to be enough. Enviromental montioring performed in the time since Spor-klenz became unavailable show no increase in bioburden, which indicates the change has no impact on the effectiveness of our cleaning and sanitisation practices since Spor-klenz became unavailable (Ref. Attachment 1 and 2). The number of infections in production is tracked as one of Curida Diatec's KPIs, and we have stayed within the limit of five or less infections per year. It is therefore decided that the discontiuation of Spor-klenz in our cleaning routines, and instead using peroxide, will be a permenant change.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: 1. Impact on facility, utility, equipment: No impact on used software. No impact on facility or equipment as cleaning with peroxide is already performed on a monthly basis for analysis lab and on a weekly basis for cold/clean room.  2. Production No direct impact on manufacturing process. 3. Quality Control No changes to quality controls  4. Logistic Supply chain needs to ensure sufficient peroxide.  5. Validation and Qualification Change does not impact any validation status.Simplifying the disinfection routine will also be more cost and time effective. 6. HSE No impact. No additional action is needed. 7. Training and personel.  Production personnel have to be aware or receive training to ensure that cleaning is performed with correct material. Use of Spor-klez after change is implemented is not considered a risk as it is not and will not be in stock. 8. Documentation Systems The following documents and worksheets need to be updated: SOP1072, 1099, 4045, 4005 DOC1072.2, 1099.1, DOC4045.1, 4005.1 9. Customer Updated procedure remains in agreement with the Quality agreement between Curida Diatec. Change notification is not required. 10. Compliance No identified risk of incompliance with ISO9001 standard. No additional action is needed. 11. BizzMine No need of update of any BizzMine collection/module training etc. No additional action is needed. Risk The lack of cleaning with acid based cleaning detergents every 6 months for analysis lab and every month for cold/clean room is not considered to add a significant sporicidal risk. All lab areas are continuously monitored for environmental contamination and microbial growth. Peroxide is a strong antiseptic and one of the main components of the acid based cleaning detergent used at Curida Diatec (Spor Klenz). The hydrogen peroxide used is not as effective against fungal spores as the Spor Klenz detergent, but combined with the general aseptic and sterilization techniques employed it is considered to be effective enough as a replacement.
Planned implementation data - comment: Peroxide is already in use as Spor-klenz has been unavailable.

Change Title: Temporary change for extension of calibration/service deadline for AcuSyst Systems
Change from:: This change applies to AcuSyst Systems (AcuSyst Maximizer (ASM) and AcuSyst Xcellerator (ASX)) that are in the Diagnostic production facility, DIA029, DIA030, DIA202 and DIA408. Last calibration on these ASMs (DIA029, DIA030 and DIA202) was performed on 04.JUL.2023 and ASX (DIA408) on 05.JUL.2023. Service of AcuSyst systems by external service supplier is performed annually, which according to SOP5043 version 4.0 is defined as Every 12th months ± 1 month on date". Thus, the due date for next calibration is 04.AUG.2024 and 05.AUG.2024. According to SOP5043 v. 4.0 equipment must be calibrated at specific intervals prior to use.  "
Change to: Extend the deadline for calibration of AcuSyst systems from 04.AUG.2024/05.AUG.2024 to 04.DEC.2024.  Allow production on AcuSyst Maximizer before external service/calibration is performed (outside of normal calibration time frame).
Justification for Change: Calibration/Service of AcuSyst systems is usually performed in July considering production downtime during the summer. Service is provided by Cell Culture Company (C3), and calibration/service during the production downtime allows service of all the instruments at the same time when the service personnel visits the site. Since their office is outside of Norway, it requires planning between service personnel and Curida Diatec production department to agree on a date that fits both parties. As a production batch was started on ASX 18.APR.2024, the annal service was not scheduled for July this year. This batch was started to serve both as a production batch and process qualification for the ASX. Since the ASX is a relatively new instrument in the facility as well as the clone used had not been produced in ASX before in the facility, this led to uncertainty about termination date of the production batch. It is not preferred to perform calibration during a production batch, so calibration was not ordered for the summer. The batch was terminated on 12.07.2024.  Service is scheduled in October 2024, but the extension date in this temporary change will be set to December 2024 to allow for possible delays. To meet the planned delivery date for product 2D2 in November 2024, production in ASM needs to be started after the original deadline for calibration/service. This is considered to be low risk on product quality (see below) and will therefore be allowed while this temporary change remains open. Risk assessment: Extension of deadline for calibration/service has been performed previously (Ref. CMID25 and QRM-2021-136), and the risk assessment report (QRM-2021-136) concluded that the risk is mainly associated with production timeline rather than product quality. The identified risks were low and considered to be acceptable without any further actions needed. The major risk of producing on ASM before service is performed is therefore considered to be termination of production batch earlier than expected. AcuSyst systems are used in the up-stream production phase and the harvest collected are processed further in several downstream and quality control steps. Therefore, the chances of affecting product quality is considered quite low. In addition, the equipment has an internal alarm system that notifies the users if any internal factors superseeds the settings, and in-process samples are taken from the system and tested externally during the production run. If there is any potential failure during the run, it is believed that this will be discovered by these systems. If any problems occur during the production run, this will be handled through the non-conformance system, where quality impact to the product will be assessed.  Curida Diatec has been in contact with other companies using AcuSyst systems to share experience and knowledge. Information was received that there are companies who has experience with calibration/service every 2-5 years without any issues, supporting the low risk conclusion from the internal risk assessment (QRM-2021-136).  Status of instrument: All of the instruments were calibrated in July 2023. DIA029 and DIA408 were used for production after calibration and no non-conformance related to the instruments has been registered for either of these, indicating that they are quite stable. DIA030 and DIA202 has not been used for production after the last calibration. As DIA202 is under implementation (CMID28), it will not be used for production until the implementation process is complete and approved. Since DIA030 has not been used since the last calibration, the chances of wear on mechanical parts of this instrument in considered to be quite low. There was a planned production with DIA030 in October 2023, but the production plan was cancelled due to unusual noise from the ASM (Ref. NC270). The problem was classified as minor by service personnel and fixed by Curida Diatec's facility manager in collaboration with service personnel - i.e. this is not believed to have any impact to the instrument. Considering this, DIA030 will be prioritized to use for the next ASM production batch.  Based on our internal assessment/experience, information from other companies using similar instrument as well as status of the instruments, it can be considered as a low-risk change for product and its quality.  Benefits of performing change: -Delivery on time to customer (good for customer relationship)-Allows use of AcuSyst flowpath and media that would have been expired if not used before the new service date (economical benefit)
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: Relevant AcuSyst System must be labeled with Service postponed according to CMID71" and all relevant personnel must be informed about start of this temporary change before this change can be implemented."

Change Title: Implementation of process overview in BizzMine
Change from:: No clear overview of the internal processes. No procedure or list provides a full process overview at the Diagnostic Production site.
Change to: Clear overview of process mapping, with clearly defined processes,owning department, SME and connected procedures, training modules, change control, non-conformances etc.
Justification for Change: To identify all the QMS records (non-conformance, changes etc) associated with the process, a manual search has to be performed using keywords. This approach is time-consuming and prone to errors. This can be especially challenging during an external audit or when responsibilities of the process are handed over to different personnel or departments. Currently implemented eQMS system allows to connection of several collections (modules) across. For example, non-conformance collections are linked to projects, instruments, supplier collections etc. This solution allows easy navigation in the QMS system and subtracts all needed information from the system. For example, it's easy to find all non-conformances related to a selected project or instrument. What is more, several core processes at the Diagnostic site are split into subprocesses, which are executed by different departments. For example, the product realisation process is owned by the Business development manager. However, only order receiving and shipment are performed by this department, and the rest of the process is performed by the Production and QA Department.  What is more, planning improvements should be based on evidence and data. The mapping process in BizzMine will allow easy assessment of underperforming areas within the organisation, detect trends, and allocate better resources. Collection for process mapping in BizzMine will be working documents that will be updated every time a new process is created, a process is overtaken by a different department or SME responsibilities are changed. If a process is no longer in use, it will be discontinued by changing it's status from active" to "not active". Benefits: -          Clear overview of all internal processes,  -          Clear overviews of all procedures, working instruction, worksheets etc, which belongs to its process. -          Clear overview of who is currently SME for each process -          Clear overview of which department own the process -          Clear overview of all non-conformances, changes, and training modules related to the process. -          Clear overview when the process was inspected internally,    Disadvantages: -          Some information, like process owner or owning department will be listed in two places, in the SOP and in the project overview collection. This have to be updated manually in the BizzMine collection.  -          Collection must be managed and kept up to date. This is not a resource demanding task, as most of the information and linked documents will be updated automatically, and only changes in SME, process owner/owning department, or when implementing new processes will generate a need for action.  Risk Analysis: 1. Some information, like process owner, will be listed in 2 places: in the procedure and in the BizzMine collection. This will require that when responsibilities are changed in the procedure, the BizzMine collection will also have to be updated.  There is a risk that information in both the procedure and BizzMine collection will not be aligned. The risk will be mitigated by establishing a procedure for the process mapping, which will include instructions on connecting the procedure with an internal process. 2. For the process overview to be effective, it is depending on all employees and QA to check that all documents and records are linked to a process and that the linked information is correct. This risk will be mitigated by updating all the procedures where it is expected that a process shall be linked, creating an own SOP for the process overview, and to provide training to the organization. "
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: The change becomes valid when the procedure is published. The process mapping list in BizzMine is a working list which will be populated with data after procedure is establsihed.

Change Title: Transfer responsibility for environmental monitoring to Production team
Change from:: CMID16 and CMID44 were raised to document the transfer of responsibilities related to environmental monitoring (detailed in SOP1076 ) from the Diagnostic production team to QC Services team which was within the previous clinical arm of the company.
Change to: The responsibilities related to environmental monitoring that were transferred to the QC Services team as detailed in CMID16 and CMID44, will be reversed and taken over by the Diagnostic Production Team as detailed in this change. The following responsibilities currently listed in SOP1076 V4.0 to be QC responsibilities, will be transferred back to the Diagnostic Production team under this change.    Sampling background environment monitoring weekly Incubation of plates at QC lab    Reading of plates/media     Document results in doc1076.4     Update EM tracker with results     Inform production management if results are at  alarm or action limits Send positive samples for typing of strain according to SOP1064 when needed The following documents associated with EM monitoring will also be updated to remove references to  QCS and reflect the current practices followed by the Diagnostic production team when performing this activity. List of documents associated with this CR.         SOP4011               SOP1074 &   DOC1074.1               SOP1075               DOC1075.1   & DOC1075.2               SOP1064               DOC1064.1               SOP1076               DOC1076.4
Justification for Change: Due to business changes documented in change request CR1251 within the Clinical QMS, the clinical operations within Curida Diatec were terminated. Therefore, the QC Services team no longer exists within the company. The responsibilities associated with environmental monitoring can no longer be performed by this team.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: The incubators used for the environmental monitoring incubations are currently placed within the previous QC labs within the Clinical Production area of Curida Diatec. This equipment was qualified for use under the Clinical QMS. After the restructure of the business described above, change CMID 63 (Integrating Clinical production site facility into Diagnostic site infrastructure) has been raised to integrate the Clinical production area of Curida Diatec  into the Diagnostic footprint. An associated CR will be raised to transition the equipment in this space into the Curida Diatec Diagnostic QMS (CRID 76). Scheduling of the EM activities described in SOP1076, is built into the planning process done by Diagnostic production team currently. There are no other changes to the EM process other than the change in responsibilities, the procedure will be performed using the same instruments, same materials, same procedures and regime as when it was being performed by the QC department. Therefore no additional actions are required as part of this CR to transfer this activity. Risk assessment: ·       The incubators that are currently being used for environmental monitoring of the Diagnostic production site of Curida Diatec are not fully transitioned to be under the Diagnostic QMS at present. However, this is considered to present a low risk, as the equipment has been qualified within the company’s clinical QMS which was to GMP standards, and there is active ongoing work to transition the equipment to the Diagnostic QMS. The qualification to GMP standards is considered sufficient and suitable to permit the use of these equipment for the environmental monitoring process in this transition period. ·       Incubators are currently placed in a lab area outside the current Curida Diatec Diagnostic footprint, however this is also considered a low risk as the incubators used for this process are being continuously monitored within the Diagnostic site monitoring system (DIA359 is monitoring incubator FEQ1040 and DIA351 is monitoring incubator FEQ1041), although they are outside the current Diagnostic footprint. Additionally, this space will be integrated into the Diagnostic footprint with the completion of CMID 63.            ·       After the site wide communication of the restructuring of the business, the responsibilities related to the EM activities were taken over by the Diagnostic Production team immediately. This was prior to formal transfer of responsibilities as per this CR, due to the sudden change of circumstances of the business. This is considered low risk as these activities were previously being performed by the Diagnostic production team before they were transferred to the QCS team, the procedure described in the SOP has not been changed when it was taken over by QCS and therefore the Diagnostic production team did not require special training. Change Title: Changes in Organisation structure, roles, and responsibilities
Change from:: Two organisation charts are established: one for the Diagnostic production site (ISO) and one for the Clinical production site (GMP). The organisation chart for Diagnostics is documented in DOC5016.1 (version 3.0, valid 10/12/2022). The chart contains employee names, positions and departments that are no longer correct according to the current organization.  The majority of current valid QMS documents have assigned responsibility to titles or roles that are very specifically defined, which leads to problems when the organisation is changing, new roles are introduced, and some titles no longer exist.Change CMID35 Changes in Organisation structure, roles, and responsibilities was opened in July 2023 to reflect changes in the organization, including new position names/reporting relationships and a name change from Research and development (R&D) department to Manufacturing Science and Technology (MSAT) department. The changes were approved for execution, but the change has not been fully completed. Due to additional organizational changes in 2024 (reduce number of employees and departments), some of the information in this change is no longer relevant to be completed.
Change to: Create one organisation chart showing the functions of the whole company of Curida Diatec AS. The department names and position names will be updated to reflect the new organization. The employee names will be removed (only position names should be listed), to make the chart more flexible and robust. Number of employees is reduced from 36 to 22, and several departments will be discontinued (see details below). Attachment 1 shows the chart of the new organisation structure after the restructuring process was completed in July 2024. The organisation chart reflects the connection with roles at Group level, where the colour defines if the role is a part of Curida Holding AS or Curida Diatec AS and with straight or dotted lines. The heads of departments will report to the Director of Curida Diatec. All Job descriptions shall be aligned with current roles, defined responsibilities, authorities, and reporting relationships. In addition, all valid QMS documents shall refer to the correct role/title/department when assigning responsibilities. The roles with assigned responsibility will be defined to be flexible and robust for minor changes in the organisation, though the terminology must be unambiguous. The assigned responsibilities in these documents shall be aligned with the job descriptions, workflow, workload, and current ISO 9001 standard. The main changes from the previous roles/titles/departments are listed below (includes relevant changes listed in CMID35). Titles that no longer exist:- General Manager/Site Manager (Replaced/equivalent with Director)- Quality Assurance Director/Manager (Replaced/equivalent with Head of Quality)- Business Development Director/Manager (Replaced/equivalent with Head of Business Development)  Departments that have been discontinued and no longer exist: - Clinical production site (GMP)- R&D/MSAT department- QC department New roles/titles and department names: - Director: Reports to the CEO at Group level of Curida Holding AS.- Head of Quality: Reports to the Director.- Head of Business Development: Reports to the Director.- Head of Operations: Reports to the Director.- Operations department: Includes Diagnostic Production department, FEU department and Logistic department. Lead by the Head of Operations.- Production Scientist: Employees from the previous MSAT department are now included in the Operations department as Production Scientists.  Due to the additional organisational changes in 2024, CMID35 should be closed with referral to this new change control and all QMS actions remaining to align with the new organisation will be followed up in this change.
Justification for Change: Due to business decisions made in 2023 (ref CMID35) and spring 2024, the organisational structure of Curida Diatec has changed. Roles, titles, and departments have been introduced or removed and some reporting relationships have changed. Many SOPs refer to outdated departments and roles/titles and need to change accordingly. The QMS should be updated to reflect the current organisation and be flexible and robust for minor changes in titles.  Advantages: A more unified and efficient organisation. The QMS will reflect current roles and responsibilities and will be more flexible for minor changes in the structure of the organisation. Disadvantages: Changes to documentation must be done and is time consuming.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risks and mitigating actions: Risk1: Responsibilities are inconsistent across documents, role descriptions, and job descriptions.Mitigating action: Job descriptions and QMS documentation will be reviewed and updated to comply with this change. Risk 2: Personnel not aware of their responsibilities. When roles or titles are listed in a document, the terminology used should be unambiguous, leaving no doubt about to whom the assigned responsibilities belong.Mitigating action: All documents must be read and understood by relevant personnel. When new documents are authorized or personnel get new roles, they shall be made aware of their responsibilities through reading the document and be provided training where applicable in accordance with SOP5047 Handling of training.  Risk 3: Responsibility assigned to a title that no longer exists.Mitigating action: All the instructional documentation shall be reviewed to detect where there are referred to titles, roles or departments that no longer exist. The documentation shall be updated and, where possible, be made more flexible for minor changes in the organisation. Documents to be updated are listed in attachment 2.   Risk 4: Responsibility no longer assigned when updating the document.Mitigating action: Documents are written, reviewed, and approved in accordance with SOP5042 to assure all responsibilities needed are defined. A matrix listing the current documents and their responsibilities have been made (ref attachment 2) to assure they either is accounted for or removed/changed with justification.  Risk 5: Responsibilities assigned to personnel not able to perform their task.Mitigating action: Responsibilities should be assigned at the lowest possible level, and where possible, not be assigned to single people. If single person roles must have a defined responsibility, the organisation must ensure that this role always is available in the organisation to perform that task and has a deputy in case of absence.
Planned implementation data - comment: The decision for this organisation change was made in Q2 2024, and the process for restructuring the company has been implemented gradually since. However, the implementation date in this change is set to the date when the updated DOC 5016.1 Organisation chart will be approved. The affected internal documents, procedures and job descriptions will be reviewed and updated over a period of four months.

Change Title: Implementing the Tuttnauer autoclave (DIA401)
Change from:: The Diagnostic site of Curida Diatec is currently using the Systec Vx-55 (DIA086) as its main autoclave. The equipment is located in room 107.
Change to: In addition to the Systec Vx-55 (DIA086) autoclave, the Tuttnauer LAB-G-5075ELCG-AD (DIA401, serial number: 18051613) will serve as a back-up autoclave. The new equipment will be located in room 206. The room is rented by Curida Diatec and will be included as part of the floorplan in SOP5017: Infrastrucure (CMID: 40).
Justification for Change: A back-up autoclave is convenient if the main autoclave breaks down or the capacity of the main autoclave is exceeded. It also allows for continuous rotation, which increases capacity and improves the availability of sterile equipment.         On 30.AUG.2022, the pH probe (DIA367) for the AcuSyst Maximiser (DIA029) was autoclaved in the Tuttnauer LAB-G-5075ELCG-AD autoclave (NCID: 75). A planned deviation occurred on 21.OCT.2022. The autoclave was then used for autoclaving equipment and pH probes for the AcuSyst Maximisers (NCID: 76), as the Systec Vx-55 (DIA086) was not working and parts for repair would not arrive before the autoclave had to be used to prevent a stop in production. The Tuttnauer (DIA401) was not implemented into the Diagnostics site QMS and has not been qualified according to Curida Diatec Diagnostics site's requirements. As of JUN.2024, the Systec Vx-55 (DIA086) has broken down again, which accentuates the need for a back-up autoclave.  On 27.NOV.2021, the Tuttnauer LAB-G-5075ELCG-AD autoclave (DIA401) was calibrated by Langeland Labservice AS with satisfactory results (Attachment 1). The last known service of the autoclave was also performed by Langeland Labservice AS on 20.OCT.2022 (Attachment 2). This was a periodic check-up to ensure its functioning prior to use. Programs were checked (Bowie and Dick, Liquid, Tubes, Ion exchange) and preventive maintenance service was performed according to the Operation and maintenance manual. The autoclave was calibrated and Erbo certified. Normal functioning was verified after service.  Though the equipment is second-hand and meets the criteria included in the URS (Attachment 3), the risk is still considered minor as the service ensures that functionality of the instrument is within specification.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impact assessment: According to SOP5038: Change management, this is a major change, as additional validation or qualification activities are to be completed before implementation. The autoclave is a second-hand instrument which means that there is possible wear and tear on the performance. This will however be mitigated by routine service.Service providers and external partners: Tuttnauer Europe Kiritec/Tuttnauer Europe B.V. performed an IQ (Attachment 6) and OQ (Attachment 7) while the instrument still was in Catapult Life Science’s possession. Langeland Labservice AS also attended the qualification. The already done IQOQ is considered sufficient and should be reviewed and approved by Curida Diatec as an approved qualification.    A service provider is not needed to give training to Curida Diatec operators. A new equipment SOP will be created (ref change action: 362), based on the equipment SOP from Catapult Life Science (Attachment 4 and 5), and this will be used for training. This is sufficient as the production operators already have experience with similar equipment. Langeland Labservice AS, who is a Curida Diatec approved supplier, will perform maintenance and service of the Tuttnauer autoclave.  Qualification: Qualification (IQOQ) has been performed by their supplier when the equipment was in Catapult Life Science’s possession. The results must be reviewed. ID numbers for the qualification reports shall be generated in Curida Diatec’s QMS in accordance with SOP5018: Qualification and validation. The IQOQ reports shall be reviewed by QA and approved if found to be sufficient.  As it is part of the equipment routine of the Tuttnauer autoclave to always run a Bowie and Dick test before use for dry loads, it is not necessary to perform a PQ. Process/product: The procedure for material sterilization is covered in SOP4011: Cleaning and autoclaving of laboratory equipment and the content of this SOP is also applicable for the Tuttnauer autoclave. Materials to be autoclaved are packed with heat reacting tape (black stripes turn green when activated by sterilizing temperatures). Proper sterilization of materials can therefore be visually confirmed, which means that the detectability is high. There is a risk of infecting the materials, but this will be mitigated using autoclave tape and correct gowning (covered in SOP1095: Gowning for ISO production, Fornebu facility). Documentation: Equipment must be implemented in the Diagnostic site QMS according to SOP5043: Equipment. Form DOC5043.2: Instrument implementation must be filled out. The IQOQ protocol from Catapult Life Science must be reviewed and approved by Curida Diatec. Write a new SOP for the equipment based on the SOP from Catapult Life Science (Attachment 4) and upload it to Bizzmine. Align with how Curida Diatec uses the Systec Vx-55 (DIA086).HSE: An autoclave is a pressure vessel where the products placed inside are subjected to pressure, and in most cases, reach high temperatures. There are potential hazards in connection to the high pressure and temperatures, however, Curida Diatec personnel is well trained in similar processes (Bizzmine training module: Autoclave – general use) and will be trained in using this specific autoclave as well. The HSE is thus covered. Facility: The room (206) will be included in the Diagnostics floorplan (ref CMID 40). Utilities defined and addressed in IQ. Electrical power: 3-Ph+N+E, 16A-400V, 180°/6h. City water: 2-3 bar, 1/2’, 3/4‘’, male. DEMI water: 2-3 bar, 1/2’, 3/4’’, male. Drain: 40-50 mm open and vented. Compressed air: 6-8 bar, 50 l/min. Room temperature: 5-40°C, relative humidity: 85%.  Logistics: At this point there is no demand for logistics resources (no need for spare parts etc at this point). Spare parts will be provided by Langeland Labservice AS.     The Tuttnauer LAB-G-5075ELCG-AD (DIA401) autoclave was purchased 23.AUG.2018 by Catapult Life Science AS. After JAN.2021, Curida Diatec took over its ownership, together with an equipment SOP (Attachment 4), which will be used as a reference for the new equipment SOP that is aligned with Curida Diatec processes. When owned by Catapult Life Science AS, the autoclave was qualified and used for contract autoclaving (including for the Diagnostics site). It was operated by a trained person (Jhansi Panga) from Surviva AS.
Planned implementation data - comment: Review done. Accepted and ready for approval.

Change Title: SOP5043 update. Change Responsibility, document name and title, implement risk-based approach, and digitalise instrument implementation form.
Change from:: 1.	Responsibilities: The responsibilities of equipment users and super users are defined in the procedure.2.	Document name and number: SOP belongs to the series 5XXX Management system, with the number 5043.3.	3. Instrument implementation form: The paper form “Instrument Implementation form” must be completed when a new instrument is implemented into the Production Facility. The form is filled with different collections from the eQMS system: such as Instrument Lits, Document management collection, and Folder list etc. The form is filled manually by the Production Engineer or Facility engineer and approved, scanned and archived by the QA department.4.	Need of initiated change control for instrument implementation was defined on the categoriztaion of instrument on “minor” or “major” and dependend on level of qualification.5.	If equipment is relocated, regardless of moving distance, control or calibration must be performed to verify correct functionality
Change to: 1. Responsibilities: Add responsibilities of facility manager and QA department. 2. Document name and number: SOP will be renamed to “Equipment management”. SOP will move to folder 3XXXX, renamed to Facility, Equipment, Facility. The new procedure number will be 3001. 3. Instrument Implementation form: An electronic form in BizzMine will be filled out for every new piece of equipment implemented in the production facility. This same form will be used also for like-to-like equipment. The electronic form will be interconnected with several of the existing collections in BizzMine for ease and traceability.  4. Define clearly which equipment required change control during implementation: direct impact on quality, used in a critical stage of production and required IQOQPQ. 5. Include division of the instrument on Stationary/Movable. Movable instruments will not require calibration/control when changing the location.
Justification for Change: 1.	Change of responsibilities:The main responsibility of the process has not been well defined so far. The facility manager is a new role in the Diagnostic Production organisation, with the competence and experience to well oversee equipment management.  2.	Document name and numberAll procedures related to facility, utility, and equipment management will provide a better overview of all procedures under the responsibility of the facility manager.  3.	Electronic form for instrument implementation.Currently, the introduction of the new instrument requires manually filling out a form DOC5043.2 “Instrument Implementation”. Trained production engineers of the facility team are filling forms manually with different information subtracted from several BizzMine collections: “Document Management” collection, Instrument List, Job-specific training module, and Folder list. First, dedicated Production/Facility employees filled out the instrument list in BizzMine with the requirement information stated in SOP5043. Next, the same information is transferred into paper form, which later is approved and archived by the QA department. The process requires manual transfers of information from electronic systems into paper form, which later has to be double-checked. This process is not efficient and prone to human/copy-pass errors. In addition, QA resources are needed for scanning and archiving documentation.Implementation of the electronic form, which will be cross-connected with several collections in eQMS system, will decrease the time needed in the instrument implementation process, improve the traceability of the information across eQMS system and reduce the number of errors.The form will be cross-connected with different lists/collections across eQMS such as:-	Instrument List-	Folder List-	Job-specific training-	Change control collection-	Document Management collection.This solution will ensure that all information is entered into the system only once, and several connected lists/collections will be automatedly updated with corresponding information.The process flow will reflect the current process, where the first form will be filled out by trained production/facility personnel and sent electronically via the system to QA for approval. The approved form will be stored in the system. No manual document scanning or archiving is needed. 4. Change control requirements for instrument implementation Provide clear criteria on which instruments require initiation of change control prior to implementation. 5. Instrument classification Currently, the procedure requires that calibration or qualification will be performed when the instrument is relocated. The instruction does not distinguish between stationary and movable equipment. Movable equipment like reference thermometers are moved around a facility when needed. Their performance accuracy /precision is not impacted by localisation.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risk evaluation: 1. Change in responsibilities: Risk: Lack of competence and knowledge in equipment management. Evaluation: The Facility manager, Jim Eero Lamppu was currently trained in Equipment management at the Diagnostic site. Refer to training records: 419. In addition, he has several years of facility and equipment management in a regulated environment. No risk was detected. 2. Change in document name and number Risk: Correct documents can be found due to wrong cross reference in production documents/training modules. Mitigation action: SOP4004, SOP4005, SOP4010, SOP4042, SOP4014, SOP4025, SOP4030, SOP4032, SOP4051, SOP4042 must be updated with correct reference.SOP must be updated. The training module “ Instrument (equipment) management” must be updated. DOC5043.1 must be updated as well. Refer to change actions 2, 4, 3, 7 and 8. 3. Electronic form for instrument implementation Not authorised access– the “Instrument Implementation collection” will be accessible only by personnel who received training in that process. Permission setting will be tested during the validation of the collection. Functionality of the collection – the form is sent to the wrong person. The collection will be tested regarding its flow functionality during the validation. Loss of information: all information is stored on external servers, maintained by a software provider and can be restored if needed. Bizzmine is an approved Curida Diatec supplier Electronic form is used incorrectly – the training has to be provided to users to perform the process correctly, this includes training of Production, Facility and QA team. All fill out forms will be always inspected and approved by QA. If errors are detected, a form will be sent back to the user for correction. The document flow and access rights will be tested during the validation of the BizzMine collection.' Mitigation action: The new collection in BizzMine has to be validated. The access, document flow etc will be assessed during validation. Refer to change actions 1 and 5. 4. Change control requirements for instrument implementation  Risk: the change control is not initiated when needed. All fill-out forms will be always inspected and approved by QA. The document flow and access rights will be tested during the validation of the BizzMine collection. Mitigation action: The new collection in BizzMine has to be validated. The access, document flow etc will be assessed during validation. Refer to change action 1 and 5. 5. Instrument classification Risk - an instrument not classified correctly, requalification/calibration not executed if needed. The risk is considered minor and will be mitigated by providing suitable training for users The BizzMine instrument collection must be updated, to ensure that instrument classification on Stationary/Movable is clearly defined and documented. All records of already registered instruments must be updated. Refer to action 10. In addition training module will be updated and training will be provided for users.  Refer to change actions 1, 5, 9 and 10.

Change Title: Temporary change for use of expired material: STD Flexboybag for harvest and harvest medium for production B8863
Change from:: Use of STD Flexboy bag 5 L within expiry date.
Change to: Extend Use of STD Flexboy bag after expiry, bags expired 31.MAY.2024 for 1 month
Justification for Change: STD Flexboy bags 5 L are used for harvesting and harvest medium for the Xcellerator run, production B8863 (project 2024-25). Flexboy bag with cat no: FFB110904, Lot no: T0027720 and exp. date: 2024-05 have been used until 30. MAY.2024. Currently, Curida Diatec has an expedited outstanding order with Sartorius that is estimated to arrive at 07.JUN.2024. The 5L bags currently need to be replaced latest on 05.JUN.2024 to ensure medium supply to the cells and continuous harvesting of product. Due to unforeseen issues with the autoclave (refer to NC311), a backup solution of using sterilized tubes and glass bottles can not be used in this situation. Therefore the expired 5L bags are needed for manufacturing to avoid premature termination of B8863. Manufacturer states 3 years expiry date from the date of production. However, the quality of the material does not change immediately. It it therefore considered a small risk to use this expired bags for a few weeks before using a new lot of bags. Impacted areas:  Production: inspection and confirmation of intact material before use is to be performed to ensure extended suitability of the material (action 340) Documentation: comment needs to be added to batch documentation with reference to this CMID and crosslinked with the bags that were used after the expiry. Training: The production team is to be informed of this temporary change and to be instructed on documenting the use of expired goods in the batch documentation. (Action 340) Facility: no impact on the facility HSE: no impact on HSE as a material is the same Logistics: label all expired flexboy bags with this CMID nr and use them within 1 month (action 341), and discard all remaining bags on 01.JUL.2024 (Action 342) Risk assessment and mitigating actions: Sterility: With the expiry date, the manufacturer guarantees the sterility of the bags. If bags are not sterile, it can cause an infection in the production. However, the Flexboy bags are double bagged and heat sealed and packaging will be inspected and confirmed intact prior to use (action 340) Integrity: Over time, material can deteriorate causing particles and rifts in the bags. However the bags are made from durable material  (Film S71: Ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA)), are double bagged and stored in cardboard boxes for protection. There is low likelihood that they will start to deteriorate immediately after the expiry date, but material will be inspected and confirmed intact prior to use (Action 340) and after 1 month all remaining bags will be discarded at the end of this temporary change (Action 342).
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: Planned implementation date is 05.JUN.2024, planned termination of temporary change is 12.JUL2024

Change Title: Implementing freezer EQ1112
Change from:: Warehouse room 205 does not have a freezer for storing quarantined goods.
Change to: Warehouse room 205 will have a freezer (EQ1112) to store quarantined goods.
Justification for Change: A freezer is needed for storage of quarantined goods. EQ1112 was previously used for this purpose before moving of site from Oslo science park to Technopolis Fornebu.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impact assessment: According to SOP5038, this is a major change, as additional validation or qualification activities are to be completed before implementation.   Validation: Equipment should be qualified according to SOP5018: Qualification and validation activities  a.            Installation: performed in house by Curida Diatec employees   b.            Operation: performed in house by Curida Diatec employees   c.            Performance: performed in house by Curida Diatec employees      Process/product/security: The freezer will be used to store quarantined goods. The freezer should therefore be stored in a room equipped with a lock, and access to it should be distributed to dedicated personnel (Logistics).        Documentation: Equipment must be implemented in the Diagnostic site QMS according to SOP5043: Equipment. Form DOC5043.2: Instrument implementation must be filled out. An IQOQPQ protocol and report must be carried out and documented.       HSE: The freezer holds ≤-18°C. Protective gloves are not necessary to use.      Facility: The freezer requires ambient temperatures in the room between 10°C and 32°C. Space, electrical power requirements and utilities are defined and addressed in IQ.       Risk assessment: Little risk is associated with taking EQ1112 into use, as it has been used for this exact purpose previously.  - Correct voltage to use the instrument (220-240V and 50 Hz) will have to be ensured.  - If the freezer fails, this will ruin materials. The air temperature inside the freezer will therefore be monitored continuously with SenseAnywhere facility monitoring system. To mitigate the risk of temperature fluctuation, the freezer will always be at least 50% loaded to prevent quick temperature spikes from activity. The full capacity of the freezer is 60 liters.  - Some risk associated with the instrument being damaged from the move or deteriorating over time. Any damage to the instrument or malfunctioning will be uncovered in the IQOQPQ.
Planned implementation data - comment: DIA409 ultra freezer implemented according to plan.

Change Title: Change frequency of external service for glassware washer (DIA085)
Change from:: Yearly external service (Miele) for glassware washing machine.
Change to: Biennially external service (Miele) for glassware washing machine.
Justification for Change: The glassware washer is routinely checked once a month by super user, cleaning filters if needed. The washing machine does not handle large amounts of lipids and proteins which commonly cause more erosion or deterioration in machinery. The items are therefore rinsed thoroughly before entering the glassware washer.  The external personnel providing service have often remarked that the machine appears unused" and has not performed any corrective maintenance thus far. It is therefore proposed to update SOP4051 to change maintenance from every year to every other year (change action.329). The benefits of this change is to reduce costs and time used on unnecessary maintenance. Identified risks and mitigating actions - with less frequent service the instrument could break down and cause a delay in washing glasware in the lab, which in turn might cause delays in production. Mitigating actions: As there have never been incidents of malfunction based on wear and tear of the machine and service personnel usually remark that the machine appears almost unused and no maintenance has been performed, this risk is considered unlikely and therefore no mitigation actions will be performed. - there is a risk that the new maintenance frequency will be forgotten. therefore instrument will be labelled with the service date (change action 330), SOP4051 will be updated (change action 329) and instrument list will be updated with new maintenance regime (change action 332). - The Miele supplier qualification does not at this time include instructions on service of the machine. It should be evaluated if it is still sufficient or needs to be updated (change action 331). "
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: NA
