Change Title: Temporary change for selling Curida Diatec owned products to multiple customers
Change from:: Products produced under customer-owned projects are only sold to project owner as outlined in SOP5011 Version 2.0.
Change to: Effective immediately, there will be a temporary modification to the to the existing policy outlined in SOP5011 Version 2.0 regarding the sale of product produced under customer owned projects. The change entails expanding the scope of customers to whom products produced under customer owned projects can be sold.This policy applies only to product in stock produced with hybridoma clones that Curida Diatec holds the license rights to.
Justification for Change: Description: Curida Diatec have licence rights to produce and sell antibodies for research and in vitro diagnostic purposes from a selection of hybridoma cell lines (Ref: Attachment 1). For these clones, no customer affiliation is necessary. However, the governing procedure for the realization of a product is centred around contract manufacturing for a particular customer. Thus, all products produced to date have customer affiliation. This prohibits the sale of surplus products from a customer-owned project to different customers. CMID57 has already been raised to establish a process for internal productions, which will ultimately serve this issue. However, until the permanent changes are active, this change will serve as an interim solution. Affected Batches: B8479, B8733, B8549, B8679, B8766. Impact assessment: Operational: The temporary change will provide increased operational flexibility by allowing the sale of surplus products to multiple customers, potentially optimizing production capacity and revenue streams during the interim period. Customer: There may be positive impacts on customer relationships, as Curida Diatec can provide products more efficiently, potentially strengthening business relationships. Financial: The modification could lead to increased revenue generation by selling surplus products to multiple customers. This can minimize waste, offsetting production costs and potentially improving financial performance. Documentation: This change directly leads to an increased workload of Production management and Quality assurance as further documentation is necessary for sale. Information must be added to Batch documentation to ensure traceability. An additional Certificate of analysis (CoA) without customer affiliation will have to be created for all the affected batches to maintain confidentiality. ERP system: Products are registered without customer affiliation in Nova and are therefore unaffected by this change. Products: Products under the scope of this change are finalized and approved. Hence, products will not be affected by this change. Risk assessment: To mitigate the risk of document traceability, ChangeActionID320 is raised to create a quality note, which will be attached to the Batch documentation of products sold under the scope of this temporary change. To mitigate the risk of sharing confidential information, a new CoA will be made without customer affiliation, under a new project number. This CoA will be approved by Quality assurance in compliance with the original CoA. ChangeActionID321 is raised to perform training of Quality assurance and Production management in this change control to ensure compliance.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: This change will last for the duration of 6 months from execution date or until CMID57 is executed.
Planned implementation data - comment: NA

Change Title: Implement process for internal production of owned or licnsed clones for sale, marketing or R&D purposes
Change from:: Antibody production is exclusively dedicated to customer-owned projects, ensuring alignment with agreed-upon specifications and terms. The procedural guidelines for this process are detailed in SOP5011 Version 2.0, titled Realization of Product.""
Change to: The proposed change involves expanding the scope of antibody production to include internally produced products for sales marketing and R&D purposes. Internal productions can only be conducted with owned or licensed clones (Ref: Attachment 1).This will only be an alternative to customer-owned projects and will not replace them.
Justification for Change: Justification for change: Prospective clients have expressed interest in obtaining samples of our catalog antibodies. However, since all our projects are customer-owned, the existing stock cannot be used for this purpose, without reprocessing the product and/or documentation. Currently there are no procedures describing this process. To address this, we aim to handle production of licensed or Curida Diatec-owned clones as internal projects. This strategic production approach allows us to keep stock, market and offer these products to multiple customers and provide samples to potential clients. Our objective is to optimize product utility, increase business size, minimize waste, and meet customer demand.   Impact assessment: Positive Impact: The implementation of this change can increase product utility, by minimizing waste, increasing availability and sales volumes. The internal product specifications will offer greater flexibility for optimization, thereby elevating product quality and overall production yield. This expanded flexibility allows for the sale of internal products to any customer, consequently broadening Curida Diatec's product portfolio. Additionally, this will equip sales with product samples as a tool to support their efforts. This change will also standardize how we handle production of our licensed and owned clones, providing procedural guidance. Negative Impact: The implementation of this change increases the complexity of production planning by adding additional kinds of projects.  This will include additional procedural approaches which is crucial to differentiate. Ultimately imposing an increased workload on production management. As the production amount is not predetermined by customer orders for such productions there is a risk of over- or under-production. This together with the costumer samples that will be produced internally potentially challenges our storage capacity for the finished product. Risk assessment: Facility: With this change's implementation comes a slight increase in production and storage of products. The risk to facility is low since both storage and production facilities are currently far from capacity. However, a thorough assessment of the existing facility was performed to identify any necessary upgrades or modifications. It was found that storage facilities are sufficient, and storage was designated to marketing samples in both DIA363 (–20°C) and DIA293 (2-6°C) (Ref: Figure 1).       Figure 1: Dedicated storage for marketing samples in left (DIA363) and right (DIA293)     HSE: MSDS will be created for all products in Curida Diatec Diagnostic portfolio to ensure compliance with current standards (Ref: ChangeActionID307). No additional risk to HSE could be established. Suppliers: No new consumables or equipment will be necessary for the implementation of this change. Therefore, no risk to suppliers could be established. QMS: With the implementation of this change, costumer involvement will need to be substituted. The costumer's role in production will need to be identified and replaced in all steps they serve a vital role.  First a procedure will be established to direct all involved personnel through internal productions, ChangeActionID309 is raised to do so. This will outline everything from production planning to final vialling and storage. Secondly, ChangeActionID311 will be raised to create a custom product specification document to be used in internal productions. This document will outline a standardized production, defined chemical composition and quality control regime. Thirdly, ChangeActionID312 is raised to create a custom Certificate of analysis document to supplement internally produced product.To do so shelf life of each product will need to be established. The product stability will be tested and documented according to ChangeActionID313. ChangeActionID314 is raised to establish a new control plan document to support the technical revision of batch documentation from internal productions. Consequently, ChangeActionID315 is raised to update quality assurance procedures to reflect or cross-reference the new documents.    Personnel: With all the new documents and procedures created in connection with this change, new training modules will have to be created and all relevant personnel will receive training. ChangeActionID316 and ChangeActionID317 are raised to ensure that all personnel involved receive the appropriate training to conduct internal productions. Logistics: Curida Diatec is already registered as a customer in our ERP system and will be used for internal projects to ensure traceability of the product in stock.  Additional risk: As there is overlap between the current production process described in SOP5011, and the procedure that will be established (Ref: ChangeActionID309), there is a risk of different or contradicting information. To mitigate the risk of the wrong procedure being used, ChangeActionID318 is raised to update SOP5011 with references to the new procedure. Both processes will be well defined to ensure that all involved personnel will use the correct procedure.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Production planning:Bizzmine clone list is updated with Curida Diatec as clone owner for all the relevant products (Ref: Table 1). ChangeActionID319 is raised to restrict access to this list, to exclusively Quality assurance and business development. This is done to maintain separation between customer owned and Curida Owned/Licenced clones.  Production planning will be executed according to procedure established in, ChangeActionID309. Product specification created for internal production of clones established in , ChangeActionID311 will be used. Production: Curida Diatec will be recorded as customer in all production documents. Production will be executed identical to the current production process according to procedures and product specification. Marketing samples will be labelled as “Marketing Samples”.Approval: All batch documentation from internal production will be technically verified supported by control plan established in , ChangeActionID314, to ensure production is in accordance with product specification and internal procedures.  Certificate of analysis established in, ChangeActionID312 will be used for internal product exclusively.  Quality assurance will review and approve batch documentation in the same manner as with current productions. Marketing samples will be technically verified but not approved by QA, this will be stated in procedure established in, ChangeActionID309. Products will be recorded in our ERP system NOVA under Customer Curida Diatec with the same product code (MAB). Marketing samples will be recorded in our ERP system NOVA under Customer Curida Diatec, but under the new product code (Ms). Storage: Internal products will be stored in accordance with current production process. Marketing samples will be stored in designated area with in DIA363 (–20°C) and DIA293 (2-6°C).  Storage of both internal product and marketing samples will be described in procedure established in ChangeActionID309.
Planned implementation data - comment: Procedures, PS and CoA need to be updated before implementation. The procedure and associated documents must be updated before the end of June 2024. The remaining actions should be executed by the end of 2024.

Change Title: Implementation of Code of Conduct into Curida Diatec Quality Management System
Change from:: Statements regarding the company’s ethical guidelines and information to the employees of the company’s expectations, values and rules to protect the business is written in several separate  documents and agreements, such as Quality Manual, Quality Policy, Personnel handbook and individual employee contracts.
Change to: The company’s ethical guidelines and information to the employees of the company’s expectations, values and rules to protect the business are gathered in one document - the Code of Conduct - easily accessible to present to any interested parties.  The Code of Conduct is implemented and established in the Curida Diatec QMS and will be managed (distributed, updated, read & understood) via Document Management module in eQMS BizzMine.
Justification for Change: The implementation of a Code of Conduct into the QMS will: Enhance transparancy, ethics and integrity in all organisational activities. Provide clear guidelines for employee behaviour and decision-making progress. Align organisational practices with legal requirements, industry standards and stakeholder expectations.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Background:As part of our commitment to continuous improvement and adhearance to ISO 9001:2015 requirements and expectations from all stakeholders, it is necessary to integrate a comprehensive Code of Conduct into the QMS of Curida Diatec.The Code of Conduct will be implemented in addition to the already existing documents and agreements to have an easy accessible document with the collected information to the company’s ethical guidelines, values and rules to protect the business. Risk assessment:Potential risks associated with this change include a resistance of change, lack of understanding or buy-in from employees, and non-compliance with the Code of Conduct. Mitigation strategies will be implemented to address these risks including effective communication, stakeholder engagement, and ongoing monitoring and evaluation lead by the Leader team of Curida Diatec. CEO is responsible for drafting the document (draft is attached below), ensure approval from the Board of Curida Diatec and sign final approved version (in document). CEO is also responsible for communication of the implementation of the Code of Conduct to all employees. HR team will be responsible for uploading the approved document in eQMS BizzMine.
Planned implementation data - comment: NA

Change Title: Temporary change for rescheduling management review in 2024
Change from:: Management review performed annually at Q1.
Change to: Management review in 2024 will be performed annually at Q2.
Justification for Change: The SOP 5016 Management responsibilities, section 4.7 defines timeline for performance of Management review: “Management review is performed annually at Q1 and is meant to be a meeting for the Management to exchange experiences, ambitions, and concerns for the recent and coming years.” Due to organisation change (reference to CMID35), new roles were established within the organisation. As the roles and responsibilities are not fully defined by the end of Q1 2024, the management review can not be conducted by Q1 2024. The management review will be postponed until Q2 2024 (change action 287). The SOP 5016 is under revision to align with new organisation structure (according to CMID35).
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risk management: 1. Company goals and objectives are not defined and presented to the organisation: The company goals were defined during an offsite meeting performed by leadership in March 2024 and presented to all employees on Allmøte Curida Diatec Q1 2024 (22.03.2024). No risk was detected. The presented goals will be incorporated into the Management review report 2023/2024.  2. Internal audit plan not defined: The internal audit plan is presented during management review and communicated to the organisation. Scheduling an audit plan 3 months later can increase the risk that the audit plan will not be executed (i.e. too little time to prepare and execute the audits). The risk will be mitigated by scheduling an audit plan before management review and communicating it to the organisation by end of April 2024. See change action 285. 3. Periodic revision of documentation: The periodic revision is planned during the management review. Periodic review is ongoing process and currently almost 100 documents are pending revision. Several core processes are ongoing revision as well as planned in CAPA186. A delay in management review will not impact the periodic revision of the documents. Therefore, no additional action will be taken. 4. Adjustments to Diatec Business Plan. The Business Plan must be presented to Board.: A business plan is under development. No risk detected. 5. Improvement of the effectiveness of the QMS and Processes:  Improvements of QMS were incorporated into goals and objectives, presented to employees at Q1 Allmøte on 22.03.2024. Several QMS improvements are currently ongoing and will not be impacted by the rescheduled management review. No risk detected. 6. Improvement of the product related to customer requirements:  Already incorporated into goals and objectives, presented to employees at Q1 Allmøte on 22.03.2024. No risk detected.  7. Supplier Audit Plan:  Currently supplier qualification program is ongoing full revision according to CMID12. Needed supplier audits were initiated. The budget for audits were secured in 2024 budget. No risk detected. 8. Resources: Not sufficient resourses in the organisation, or lack of specific knowledge/competence can impact execution of planned improvements, goals and objectives. However this risk is accepted, therefore no action will be taken. 9. Internal communication:  Leader group must be informed regarding temporary change. The employees have to be notified that management review will be rescheduled in 2024. See change action 286.
Planned implementation data - comment: The change should be closed when management review is performed in Q2 2024

Change Title: Updating CAPA module in BizzMine for handling records without effectiveness evaluation
Change from:: CAPA records which were executed corectly are sent to the CAPA effectiveness evaluation step, regardless of whether effectiveness evaluation is required for the CAPA record or not.
Change to: All executed CAPA records for each effectiveness evaluation is not required will automatically get to the STOP step (completed) after “CAPA execution – QA approval” step.
Justification for Change: The effectiveness check is not required for all executed CAPAs. Especially Preventive actions are initiated as an internal improvement initiative, and effectiveness can not always be assessed for them. Refer to CAPA 150. Currently, the setup of the CAPA collection in BizzMine (workflow 5) forces all CAPA records that are executed correctly to go through the effectiveness evaluation step. The QA, when approving the record, fills the form manually, NA for effectiveness evaluation, which requires additional time and is unnecessary.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impacted area of the organisation: BizzMine software: validated the status of the BizzMine collection – CAPA collection requires updating and revalidation to ensure the new workflow works correctly. The change will impact the following steps of collection: CAPA execution - QA approval", "CAPA effectiveness evaluation", and "CAPA effectiveness evaluation - QA approval". Only these three steps will be invalidated. Therefore, they will have to be revalidated. There will not be a change in collection permission. Therefore, these parameters will not be tested. Refer to change action 01.  Documentation: The SOP5039 “Corrective action and Preventive action” must be updated to provide information on handling CAPA without an effectiveness check. Refer to change action 02. The WIN should be established to inform the QA team how to handle CAPA with and without effectiveness evaluation. Refer to change action 03. Risk analysis: A) Malfunction of updated BizzMine collection – the error in the collection can lead to the following issues: 1) The record is sent to the wrong person / wrong step – a high probability of the error as similar errors were experienced before (NC243), therefore, the updated collection will be revalidated. Refer to change action 01. 2) The wrong person step can edit the record – a high probability of the error as similar errors were experienced before (NC243), therefore, the updated collection will be validated. Refer to change action 01. B) The QA team responsible for CAPA approval will wrongly handle CAPA without effectiveness evaluation – the following errors could be observed: 1) The CAPA record, which was not executed, will be sent for effectiveness evaluation. 2) The CAPA record, which does not require effectiveness evaluation, will be sent for effectiveness evaluation.  3) The CAPA record, which requires effectiveness evaluation, will be sent to the STOP step and effectiveness evaluation will not be performed. The CAPA collection will be updated with an additional tick-off point: “Effectiveness evaluation:  Required / Not required” which will be obligatory. For not executed CAPA and CAPA without effectiveness evaluation, QA personnel will have to select the correct option. Depending on which option will be selected from different scenarios will take place: Scenario 1:  Effectiveness evaluation = Required 1. Data “Deadline for effectiveness evaluation” becomes visible and obligatory. 2. Closed records will go to the “effectiveness evaluation step.” Scenario 2:  Effectiveness evaluation required = Not required 1. Data “Deadline for effectiveness evaluation” becomes not obligatory. 2. Closed record will go to the “stop” step, and its status will change to “completed.” Both scenarios will be tested during collection revalidation. Refer to change action 01. What is more, working instructions for the QA team will be established to ensure that CAPA approval will be performed correctly.  Refer to change action 03."
Planned implementation data - comment: The change action is valid when updated CAPA collection and SOP is released.

Change Title: Discontinuation of an analytical method for specificity testing SOP1039
Change from:: Products 5130 and 5020 are tested prior to release using the following analytical methods:  - OD measurement for concentration determination according to SOP0026 - SDS-Page for purity determination according to SOP1066  - ELISA for specificity determination according to SOP1039
Change to: Products 5130 and 5020 are tested prior to release using the following analytical methods:   - OD measurement for concentration determination according to SOP0026 - SDS-Page for purity determination according to SOP1066
Justification for Change: The SAH specificity test utilised the ELISA technique as an analytical method for conforming the products' 5130 and 5020 specificity. The customer, Axis Shield Diagnostics Ltd., owns this analytical method, which was implemented at Diatec at the customer's request to be used in the quality control of products number 5020 and 5130. The method utilised reagents/material provided by the customer.   The method will be discontinued at the customer's request, see attached email (attachment 1). The customer notified Diatec on 09.FEB.2024, there is no need to perform this method at the Diatec site, therefore, they will no longer supply Diatec with reagent/ material to execute this analysis.   Positive site: Less resources will be needed for executing release testing for products 5020 and 5130. The production timeline can be shortened by 1-2 days.   Negative site: Internal documentation must be updated to comply with customer requests.    Risk assessment:   The SOP1039 method detects the specificity of products 5020 and 5130. There will not be an implemented replacement method after method SOP1039 is discontinued.   There is a risk that an issue with the specificity of antibodies will be detected on the customer site during their production process, not before the release of the product at Diatec. The customer is willing to take the risk based on the justification that there have never been reported issues with specificity for antibodies supplied by Diatec, and that antibodies have passed all testing since 2008. On 13.FEB.2024, customer provided RPBB379 MEMO describing the rationale and justification for the removal of specificity testing for product 5130 concluding that the likelihood of the antibody losing its specificity is low (attachment 2). On 29.FEB.2024, customer provided RPBB278 MEMO explaining the rationale for removal of SAH specificity testing for product 5020 (attachment 3).  There is a risk that the discontinued method will still be listed in quality control requirements in the product specification for future production (as in NC32). However, change action 279 will mitigate this risk.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impact on organisation:   1. Production:   Currently executed production for product 5310 was carried out according to project 2023-46. Production for product 5020 was executed according to projects 2022-25, 2022-41 and 2022-46. The product specification prepared for that production includes specificity testing. The control plans K2 5020 and K2 5130 have to be manually corrected, with reference to the change control. What is more, product specifications prepared for that production have to be updated accordingly to reflect requirements for quality control.  Refer to change action 273.   New production of products 5020 and 5130 should be carried out according to the updated control plans. Refer to change actions 273, 274 and 275.   2. Materials:   Evaluate if the reagent/material supplied by the customer has to be removed from the ERP system. Refer to change action 281.  3. Customer:   The customer has to be notified that the method was discontinued and impacts on currently started production. Refer to change action 280.
Planned implementation data - comment: The change actions 273 and 274 must be executed before approval of the already executed production. The change is valid from day approval, and all tasks should be finalised within April.

Change Title: Extending shelf life of peroxide mop wipes with one week in combination with using unexpired peroxide spray
Change from:: Temporary change for extending the shelf life of one single lot of expired peroxide mop wipes by one week. The timeframe for change is from 02.FEB.2024 (start) to 09.FEB.2024 (end). The KlerWipe Sporicidal Low Residue Peroxide Sterile mop wipes have a 12 months shelf life. Material cat no: 6600556.
Change to: Extend the shelf life of KlerWipe Sporicidal Low Residue Peroxide Sterile mop wipes from 12 months to 12 months + 1 week for lot 4053SW2702. This is for a total of three packs of 20 wipes each for this lot. Original expiry date is 02.FEB.2024.   Peroxide mop wipes will be used for cleaning on 08.FEB.2024 when sprayed or soaked in additional unexpired peroxide from spray bottles.
Justification for Change: Justification: On 08.FEB.2024 yearly cleaning of the analysis lab, cold room, AS room and wardrobe will be done. All available surfaces, including shelfs, cabinets, chairs, trolleys, work surfaces and floor must be wiped with peroxide. We usually use big KlerWipe Sporicidal Low Residue Peroxide Sterile mop wipes for this as it is a big cleaning where we need a lot of cleaning material. We had three packs of peroxide mop wipes that had expired on 02.FEB.2024, but none that were unexpired. New wipes had been ordered earlier this week, but had notr arrived yet. Curida Diatec usually does not buy large amounts of peroxide materials at a time as they only have 12 months shelf life from the manufacturer Ecolab, before they are sent to our supplier AET, and then sent to us as we purchase them. We have exprienced in the past that what is left in stock at AET can have as little as 4 weeks life of shelf life. It therefore happens that we end up having to discard expired peroxide materials, or end up out of stock as we didn't purchase too much ahead of time. This is an issue that the logisitics department are aware of and they try to keep a small amount of unexpired materials in stock. Unfourtunetally, at the time of our yearly cleaning we were out of all unexpired big peroxide mop wipes, but had spray bottles and small wipes we that were still within their expiry date. The amount of small wipes we had would not be enough to do all the yearly cleaning, and they are also critically needed for wiping materials into cleanroom on a regular basis. The Peroxide spray bottles that are unexpired will be sprayed or soaked onto the expried big mop wipes as they are used for yearly cleaning.   Risk assessment: The KlerWipe Sporicidal Low Residue Peroxide Sterile mop wipes are sterile filled under Grade A laminar flow and bagged in a Grade B cleanroom and double bagged. They are frequently used in clean room and for disinfecting materials going into clean room. Yearly cleaning to be performed on 08.FEB.2024 is only outside of cleanroom, specifically these rooms: - Wardrobe for gowning according to which process to perform- Analysis lab for in process controls, preparations, purification and QC analysis of finished products.- Production suite for cell production in hollow fibre system (Maximizers and LAF bench)- Washing, autoclaving and LN2 room. Vented room with dishwasher, autoclave and dewars for LN2 storage. - Cold room for storage of materials and a locked cabinet inside the cold room dedicated for finished products and file samples. The risk of using expired peoxide cleaning material is that the amount of the active substance had decreased over time. Ecolab performed internal testing of their peroxide spray bottles in 2018, where they justified extending the spary bottle's shelf life from 12 months to 24 moths. This is documented in Statement on shelf-life data for Klercide Sporicidal Low Residue Peroxide" from 2021 which is added as an attachment. They also here reference an internal Ecolab report 20283 where they performed a study on package integrity at 24 months of shelf life, providing that if stored propely the integrity of the packaging was maintained. The three packs of expried peroxide mop wipes remained unopened until 08.FEB.2024. It is there fore unlikely that using expired peroxide map wipes within a week after their expiry date is a risk to the laboratory environment and quality of the cleaning. Any potenital decrease in active substances in the the peroxide mops will be made up for by soaking/spraying the wipes in unexpired peroxide.  "
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: The timeframe for change is from 02.FEB.2024 (start) to 09.FEB.2024 (end).

Change Title: Update of SOP1066
Change from:: The change applies to current version of SOP1066 i.e. version 3.0.  Preparing image for analysisAccording to pt.9.8.14, the SOP mentions step for cropping of gel in Image Lab software. But the SOP does not specify the specific position for cropping of gels.    The SOP lacks information for removal of impurities from the gel image.    Selection of control sample: The selection criteria of control sample does not take into consideration of the age of the control sample.   Control sample selection is limited to previously QA-approved batch.    The technical requirements for the approval of gel run are not totally relevant (pt. 10.1.3 and pt. 10.1.4) and requires update of this section.  The criteria for product approval is not detail enough for sample validity and criteria for product release,  The current SOP fails to specify criteria for out-of-specification (OOS) investigation and non-conformance
Change to: Preparing image for analysisDefine lines for cropping of gels (CAPA119). Fluorescent ruler that can be used in the image reader will be used to define the line for cropping. The specification can be for example 0.5 cm above and below the first and last band of the ladder respectively. Alternatively the wells and dye front can be used as reference for cropping.      Selection of control sample: Specify in the SOP to avoid selection of product older than a year as a control sample, if possible (CAPA172).  Control sample from approved supplier can be used as an alternative to internally produced control sample (Ref CAPA119: evaluate the benefit of using reference batch in run).    Technical requirement for gel approvalRemove pt.10.1.4 The most intense band of the standard should be less intense than the most intense band of the sample." and pt. 10.1.3 "10 bands must be detected for the standard" (CAPA111). Update pt. 10.1.1 to mention that 10 bands must be detected for the standard.   Add: The control sample and/or the precision plus standard should meet the requirement from their respective certification of analysis or product inlet (Ref. CAPA119: Define criteria for run approval or rejection).    Product approvalProvide validity criteria for test sample like precision (duplicates of test sample must be comparable) and accuracy (control sample and test sample must be comparable).   Update the acceptance criteria for product release for different antibody isotype. IgM analysis: no visual bands, IgX analysis: according to PS.    Provide guideline to differentiate deviations leading to non-conformance report and out-of-specification investigation. Non-conformance: Failure to meet technical requirement for gel run and validity criteria for test sample.   Out-of-specification: Failure to meet acceptance criteria to approve product release. (Ref CAPA119: provide guidelines for OOS investigation)    Further "
Justification for Change: Implementation of fluorescent ruler on SDS-PAGE process. This will make the cropping step consistent.    -          preparing pictures for analysis: cropping, removing impurities, defining line, etc We will use ruler to define where to crop. We will use it to define certain cm. below well to certain cm. above the dye front. For impurities, we can define as a spot or a particle which is not a band can be removed if necessary.  -          define and illustrate what type of impurities can be removed during preparing the picture for analysis Put some examples of gels with impurities like air bubbles.  -          how to report results for reduced and not reduced conditions Maybe this can be performed later. We can continue with the current way of reporting.  -          define criteria for run approval or rejection, OOS results Run will be approved or rejected based on the ladder and/or positive control.  -          evaluate the benefit of using reference batch in run. We will use reference batch since the method is not a validated.  -          provide guidelines for OOS investigation Ask GYR about OOS, if we have to do it in Diagnostic production.

Change Title: Revision of ELISA procedures and related SOPs
Change from:: Two very similar procedures (SOP1007 Concentration of mAbs by ELISA " and SOP1025 "General ELISA"  to the point of being identical in the operational process of determining concentration of mAbs. The primary differences between the two procedures is less defined steps within SOP1025 which are supposed to open it up for special QC analysis based on production specification (PS) for certain cell lines. One such cell line is HE1/4b/3 whose QC analysis involves an ELISA run where OD of the sample is >0.1 from blanks.  One worksheet for document activities /abalysis conducted according to SOP1007, 1014, and 1025  SOP1007 Substrate incubation as a defined timeframe (10-60 minutes)."
Change to: One general ELISA procedure  - SOP1007 with defined terms for when the analysis is used for determination of concentration and when it is used to measure affinity or productivity. Include HRP conjugate protocol in SOP1007, instead of SOP1025. Specific SOP for specificity analysis of IgE samples SOP1025 SOP1007 substrate incubation being based on OD measurements.   Overall rewording and clarification. Introduce more specific QC requirements and procedure. Manual washing step added  Each ELISA SOP will have its own worksheet, DOC1007.1, DOC1014.1, DOC1025.1 Aligning of SOP1014 with SOP1007 and 1025
Justification for Change: ELISA process is frequently used, and has been underperforming, as can be seen by the sheer number of Non conformances and CAPAs connected to the process. It is therefore considered neccesary to update multiple aspects of the procedure. One aspect is that as SOP1007 and SOP1025 are very similar, in some instances it is unclear when to use SOP1007 and when to use SOP1025, even for an experienced operator. Therefore, it is considered beneficial to simplify the procedures to one procedure SOP1007, including HRP conjugation and check of cell bank productivity, increase its flexibility by basing incubation on OD measurment (CAPA227), and update SOP1025 to be a Quality Control specificity ELISA analysis for IgE (CAPA217) as well as implementing of manual wash step to ensure robustness of process even when ELISA washer malfunctions (CMID37).
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impacted areas: No impact to equipment, materials, or validation status of method. The operation remains primarily the same, with a few more options for flexibility if this would be required and a more defined worksheet. Methods are not validated, as previously approved batch is always included in the analysis run. - process: No impact to any critical parameter of QC methods.  - HSE: no impact to HSE as the process is optimised, but not new.  - Training: The training module needs to be updated (change action 522), and operators need to be trained (change action 521) - Documentation: 3 SOPs (1007, 1025 and 1014) are to be updated (change action 268), and current worksheet for process split into separate worksheets for each sop. (change action 324) Control plans for 7050 HE1, HRP conjugation and cell banks need to be updated to reflect new SOP numbers, all control plans with worksheet reference need to be updated (change action 531) - Customers: this does not impact customers as there is no major change to the Quality Control methods them selves, only a simplification and minor improvments of the procedure. - Compliance: no impact to compliance, as updates are according to the quality system, documented in a change control, method is not validated, and training will be given according to the quality system. - eQMS system: Training module (change action 522) and process list need to be updated with references to new names of SOPs and DOCs and include the additional aspects of the SOPs (change action 530). Risk assessment and mitigating actions: 1. as procedures are re-organised and repurposed there is a risk that the wrong process will be referenced in documents. for specificity of IgE, SOP1007 might still be used for performance, however as the procedure is currently used to perform the IgE analysis, the impact of that risk is considered quite low. In addition, this risk is mitigated by training of operators in updated procedures (change action 521). 2. No new criteria for reading or treating results has been added, thus there is no risk of new OOS from this change. Using OD value instead of defined time for incubation, is implemented to minimize the possibility of reading the results to early or to late, as products can have very different reaction time and poses no risk to the results. Method is remains the same, and to eliminate the possibility of running ELISA incorrectly, training will be given (change action 521) and training module will be updated (change action 522).
Planned implementation data - comment: Change can be implemented when documents have been updated, and training has been performed.

Change Title: Defining Introduction Training for temporary employees, consultants and interns
Change from:: As stated in SOP5047, Handling of Training:   Temporary employees and contractors: Introduction and training for temporary employees and contractors will be determined by the Department Manager responsible for the employees/contractors.
Change to: Update SOP5047 accordingly:  Temporary employees, consultants and interns: Introduction and training for temporary employees, consultants or interns will be determined by the Department Manager responsible for the employees/consultants and/or interns. Regardless of the work type to be performed or the duration of the engagement, the following training is mandatory:   Onboarding, with a general introduction and review of the contract, time registration, relevant IT tools, access to the facilities, relevant policies   Training in HSE routines, including a tour through the facilities to show emergency exits and the meeting point in case of evacuation      In addition, any person who will execute any of the production, logistic or quality activities that include recording on any documentation should receive training in Introduction to QMS part 1, sections:   Good documentation practice   Document management   Handling of training
Justification for Change: Justification:   In 2022 and 2023, Interns from the Universitet i Oslo (UiO) interned in the Diagnostic facility. The introduction program was executed according to SOP 5047. It was observed and communicated that introduction training was too comprehensive for their role and time of assignment in the organisation.This resulted in that the interns did not finilsied their Introduction Program, which was discovered in January 2024 and reported in NC247. CAPA194 was initiated to simplify the Introduction Program for Interns and all temporary employees and contractors. The SOP 5047 will be updated to clarify the minimum of Introduction training which must be taken by temporary employees, consultants, and interns.

Change Title: ASM (SOP0060):  SOP Segmentation & Process Control Enhancement
Change from:: 1. A single SOP (SOP0060) outlines all AcuSyst Maximizer (ASM) procedures and refers to the supplier’s manual for critical steps. 2. ASM system parameters are only visible in real-time on-screen. 3. Only a single inoculation is performed per batch. 4. Transfer volume is fixed at 70 mL for the entire production run.
Change to: 1. Split SOP0060 and worksheets into a set of specific SOPs and corresponding worksheets:    - SOP0086 / DOC0086.1: Starting ASM      - SOP0087 / DOC0087.1: Flowpath Assembly and Fill/Flush      - SOP0088 / DOC0088.1: Inoculation      - SOP0089 / DOC0089.1: Running ASM      - SOP0090 / DOC0090.1: Termination   2. Introduce automatic data logging for capturing process parameters, alarms, and events post-run. 3. Implement reinoculation procedure early in the production if needed. 4. Update process control to define transfer volume per bioreactor (70 mL/BRX), allowing increases up to 140 mL.
Justification for Change: - The segmentation of SOP0060 into smaller and more logical SOPs (SOP0086–SOP0090) where all operational steps are clearly defined and the supplier manual is used for reference only when explicitly instructed within the SOPs, simplifies onboarding and day-to-day use.  - Reinoculation and transfer volume adjustments are included to improve the flexibility of the procedure and optimize production conditions. Introduction of a reinoculation procedure enables continuation of batches, avoiding unnecessary restarts and reducing flowpath waste. Operators save significant preparation time by maintaining active cultures rather than restarting, allowing for smoother scheduling and better use of consumables. Dynamic transfer volume supports stable bioreactor conditions by preventing pressure buildup or nutrient washout during early process stages.        - Introduction of Alarm monitor, where alarm and process parameters are logged automatically. This provides traceability, supports deviation investigations, and contributes to building clone-specific process knowledge.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Facility, Utility, Equipment: AcuSyst Maximizer instruments are already implemented into QMS, however the new data loggers need to be implemented into the system (change action 545) Logistics: No changes in material flow, scheduling, or space requirements. Reinoculation uses existing consumables and infrastructure. Production: Reinoculation adds a new operational decision point. This is mitigated by clear SOP criteria for reinoculation eligibility and approval flow. Change action 264. There is a risk that some information from instruction manual necessary for production has not been included in updated procedures. This is mitigated by standard review of the procedures prior to approval and including a reference to the manual for larger operations (such as fill/flush, inoculation) in the new SOPs, for operators to use when needed. Change action 546. Quality Control: No direct impact on QC methods or turnaround times. Increased likelihood of contamination during production due to handling of open processes during reinoculation. However this is mitigated through already implemented procedures and practices, such as aseptic handling practices, operator training, operator aseptic validation program, in-process controls, and the continued use of antibiotics. Qualification & Validation: ASM is not qualified nor is process validated, but it is under preventive maintenance and service routines specified in the procedures. New data loggers do not require qualification as they are a minor supporting instrument, with no impact to the process, other than to improve understanding and investigation processes. No impact to qualification or validation. Health, Safety & Environment (HSE): No new biological risks are introduced. Operators are trained on standard procedures for handling reinoculation and alarm resolution. Training: All existing ASM training modules must be updated to reference the new SOPs (SOP0086–SOP0090), reflect reinoculation procedures, and include transfer volume calculations. Change action 547. Operators already trained in AcuSyst maximizer procedures must receive training in updated procedures prior to performance. Change action 89. Document Control & QMS Impact: - SOP0060 and all related worksheets will be retired. Change action 517. - New SOPs and batch records: SOP0086–SOP0090 and DOC0086.1–DOC0090.1 will be initiated and approved, reinoculation, handling of data loggers and increased transfer volume to be included. Change actions 88, 261, 263, 264, 265. - Updates to referencing documents: SOP0003, SOP1071, SOP1099, SOP2016, K2M Standard. Change action 520. - Training plan revisions (change action 547) and updates to instrument lists are required (change action 545). - Due to sheer number of QMS items connected to this change, traceability between QMS records can be challenging. To avoid this, all CAPA, change actions and Internal process relevant to SOP0060 and relevant worksheet/documents need to be connected to the new SOPs and corresponding worksheet for ASM. Change action 262. Customer Impact: There is no direct impact on the customer or product quality. These updates enhance traceability, process understanding, and batch continuity, improving the robustness of the production platform. Compliance:  All updates and document changes are tracked in Bizzmine. Training, document retirement, and implementation are managed in accordance with QMS and change control procedures. No impact to compliance.ent retirement, and implementation are managed in accordance with QMS and change control procedures. No impact to compliance.
Planned implementation data - comment: When instruments are implemented (Action 545), SOPs and worksheets have been updated ((88, 261, 263, 264, 265) and training updated and performed (547, 89), the processes can be used for maximizer production.

Change Title: Implement an independent procedure for batch revision and product approval
Change from:: No dedicated procedure for batch approval—process details listed in SOP5011 Realisation of Product. Checklists used by QA were not official documents and were handled outside QMS.
Change to: Dedicated Procedure for Batch approval. The checklists used by QA will be registered documents handled according to SOP5042 Document Management.
Justification for Change: The batch approval process was described in SOP5011 as part of the Product realisation process. The instructions provided in the procedure were considered general and did not provide all the required details to conduct batch approval in a consistent and repeatable way. Previously QA employees used informal documents, used “huskeliste” (ang: checklist) to execute the QA approval process. The documents contain basic checkpoints, which should be checked in relevant worksheets. This document was used for several years by previous QA members at ISO/Diagnostic site, however, none of the lists were ever incorporated into the QMS system and was not maintained according to SOP5042. This resulted in that several errors occurring in the last 2 years, when new employees joined QA Department at the Diagnostic site (refer to NC168, CCR074 and CCR075). The CAPA ID 06 was initiated to establish proper documents used by the QA team during batch approval. All the lists were revised and translated from Norwegian to English by new QA members. All the list checkpoints were assessed based on their criticality to product quality and compliance.  In addition, the KPI tracker for batch release must be updated and moved into BizzMine, to improve trending issues related to the batch approval process.   The batch documentation critical points are listed in the table:        Process Critical Parameters: Upstream production processes       Correct clone   used.   Correct type/amount of medium, serum, and additives used.    Downstream production process        Correct column material and elution pH were used.   The correct   final buffer was used.   Correct vialing and labelling.    Quality Control       The correct   analytical method was used.   System suitability (control used).   Control and   final results within defined criteria.    Documentation requirements evaluated during batch approval:- All comments are readable and self-explanatory.- The document is signed and dated by the operator.- Project details (customer name, clone, batch numbers).- All non-conformances related to all batches included in the final batch are listed and closed.- All corrections are signed and dated by the operator.- Number of pages, one side print.- Documentation follow Good Documentation rules according to SOP5028.     Positive side: Standardized approach for the batch approval process and reduced number of errors. Negative side: Additional documents established which will have to be revised periodically, which will be resource-demanding.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Risk: DocumentationDiscrepancies between SOP5011 Realisation of Product and SOPXXX Batch approval.  As there is overlap in the documents, there is a risk that different/contradicting information may be provided in the procedures. The risk is considered minor and an unlikely impact on product quality. Mitigation action: update SOP5011 with cross-reference to new SOP. In addition, section 4.11 has to be updated only to include references to the new SOP. In addition, some QA actions are also listed in all control plans used in the technical review. That double information should be removed. (Refer to change actions 229, 230, 231, 235).  ResourcesThe risk is that employees will use old systems/documents for batch approval. The risk is considered minor and an unlikely impact on product quality. The following actions will be taken to mitigate the risk: remove the old documents (refer to change action 232), update the training module (refer to change action 233) and provide group training for all relevant employees (refer to change action 234).   Critical process details overlooked during revision              There are significant differences between different production processes. The documentation for the batch produced in CL1000 bioreactor will vary from that in the AcuSyst system. The critical parameters to be checked will also vary for reprocessing or re-vialling batches. To ensure that all processes are inspected correctly, separate worksheets (lists) will be established for each of the main processes according to action 230: DOCXX QA Checklist for Batch Revision – CL1000 bioreactors. DOCXX QA Checklist for Batch Revision – AcuSyst System. DOCXX QA Checklist for Batch Revision – CL1000 and AcuSyst System. DOCXX QA Checklist for Batch Revision – Re-vialling. DOCXX QA Checklist for Batch Revision – Conjugation. DOCXX QA Checklist for Batch Revision – Cell bank. DOCXX QA Checklist for Batch Revision – Up-Concentration. DOCXX Batch release on risk.   4. Compliance with ISO9001  Trending, evaluating and improving critical processes is the core of the ISO9001 standard. Currently, the effectiveness of the product realisation process is evaluated based on data in Excel spreadsheet Key Performance Evaluation". However, this Excel-based tracker does not provide a clear overview of the reoccurring issues with released/rejected batches. It's also difficult to plan improvement of the process based of the output from that tracker. Also, several entry data require repetitive data entry. The tracker will be replaced with BizzMine collection and dashboard for visual trending to comply with ISO9001 standard. The tracker will be visible for the whole organisation, but data entry will be limited to personnel trained in QA batch revision (refer to actions 236 and 237). "
Planned implementation data - comment: The process was implemented in May 2024.

Change Title: Establish routines for Manufacturing Science and Technology department at Diagnostic site.
Change from:: No routine for Manufacturing Science and Technology department at Diagnostic site
Change to: Established routines for Manufacturing Science and Technology department at Diagnostic site, which covers: -          Policy for “Scientific activities at Curida Diatec Diagnostics site” -          Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) for “Handling of information in Manufacturing Sciences and Technology” -          Standard Operating Procedure for “General requirements to lab routines in Manufacturing Sciences and Technology facilities” -          Standard Operating Procedure for “Handling of equipment in Manufacturing Sciences and Technology” -          Standard Operating Procedure for “Planning, performing and reporting scientific activities in Manufacturing Sciences and Technology” Standard Operating Procedures for equipment in Manufacturing Sciences and Technology. These will be prepared when considered required or necessary.
Justification for Change: Impact assessment: Minor Impact  This change is categorised as minor as the change will not have a significant impact on product quality, QMS, compliance status or validation status. Justification for the change: Manufacturing Science and Technology department was established as part of the organisation change in March 2023 (refer to CMID35). Employees of the MS&T department were onboarded at the Diagnostic site according to CMID33.  The new department requires new routines and therefore this change control has been initiated.  The implementation of controlled documents for scientific activities in Diagnostics is based on the requirement to control such activities to the extent described in ISO 9001:2015.  At the Diagnostics site MS&T department will perform activities according to Policy for “Scientific activities at Curida Diatec Diagnostics site”.  If MS&T department or employees will take over responsibility in internal processes this will be handled via separate change controls. MS&T department will perform activities related to both the Diagnostics and the Clinical site at Curida Diatec.    Impacted areas: Manufacturing Sciences and Technology: The policy and SOP’s will primarily affect the MS&T team, with new routines for planning, performing and reporting scientific activities, and daily operation of the lab, instruments, equipment and so on. Training must be provided, see action 225. Production and Quality Control: Personnel from production and quality control may in various cases be involved in scientific activities at the Diagnostics site. Training module for MS&T documents shall be created (see action 224) and relevant training should be provided when needed (see action 225).  Facilities/Utilities/Equipment: FEU is overall responsible for the facilities and utilities in Curida Diatec which includes the MS&T lab. Facility lay out for the Diagnostics site will be updated in SOP5013 “Infrastructure” in relation to CMID40.  Since a separate SOP for Handling of equipment in MS&T will be established the SOP5043 “Equipment” for Diagnostics should be updated to clearly state this. (Action 227 Update SOP5043). Personnel from FEU may in various cases be involved in scientific activities at the Diagnostics site. Training module for MS&T documents shall be created (See action 224) and relevant training should be provided when needed.  Health, Safety and Environment: No changes to HSE routines in Diagnostics will occur. The current HSE SOP5017 shall be followed, relevant routines will also be described in SOPs for MS&T. Quality Management System: The Document Management SOP (SOP5042) and corresponding collection in BizzMine has to be updated with dedicated MS&T folder in document tree (see action 226 and 228). BizzMine: Update organisation with MS&T department and corresponding function (refer to action 228) Compliance: MS&T department will not perform product or service development, therefore there is no impact of current status of ISO9001 certification, which does not include section 8.3. Design and Development of product and service.  Logistics: Ordering of material is described in the SOP for General routines in MS&T facilities. However, the routines will not differ how it is practiced until now. Materials involved in Scientific activities is specified by the person responsible for the activities.  Business Development: Scientific activities will performed as part of customer projects. Business development personnel should therefore be well informed about the requirements for such activities. Training in the Policy for Scientific activities should be performed.  Risks and mitigations: Risk of implementing routines that will not be followed and lead to either audit findings or non conformances. Risk evaluation: Low. Rationale: Routines are meant to be simple and easy to follow.  Risk of routines being de-motivating since they may for some people feel like a burden to follow. Risk evaluation: Low. Rationale: The importance of the routines to secure the quality of the work has been emphasised in several MS&T department meetings. Most of the MS&T team members are well accustomed to GMP routines that are of similar or even more stringent nature, and acceptance of these routines is expected. Benefits:  The company will comply with ISO9001:2015 for scientific activities. Establishing routines for equipment and instruments, information handling and general routines in the lab is expected to secure the quality of work in MS&T, and to utilise the resources in the department in an efficient manner. Disadvantages (cost):  Maintaining routines always has a cost.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Additional information:  The scope of this change request is to introduce the Policy and SOPs.  The information in this change request is considered sufficient for implementing SOP’s for equipment and instruments where it has been found relevant to have that. The relevance of writing Such SOP for equipment is described in SOP “Handling of Equipment in MS&T” and is based on criticality or other needs defined. Other rationales for implementing SOP for equipment is not expected to be required, but the equipment responsible person may decide to create the SOP based on own judgement. Curida Diatec Diagnostics have stated that we do not perform design of products or services. An SOP describing design control will be made in near future, this is described in Corrective and Preventive Action plan 174.  Actions: Action 219:  Policy for “Scientific activities at Curida Diatec Diagnostics site” Action 220:  Standard Operating Procedure for “Handling of information in MS&T” Action 221:  Standard Operating Procedure for “General routines in MS&T facilities” Action 222:  Standard Operating Procedure for “Handling of equipment in MS&T” Action 223:  Standard Operating Procedure for “Planning, performing and reporting scientific activities in MS&T” Action 224: Establish training module for MS&T process and documents in BizzMine Action 225:  Train personnel in MS&T and other departments according to need. This action will be considered closed when MS&T team has been trained.  Action 226: Update SOP5042 “Document Management” and corresponding collection in BizzMine with MS&T. Action 227: Update SOP5043 “Equipment” to clearly state that it is not relevant for MS&T equipment Action 228: Update organisation with MS&T department and corresponding function in BizzMine
Planned implementation data - comment: The SOP and policy for MS&T should be valid by 01 JAN 2024. Training for employees will be given within two weeks after procedures are valid. Update SOP5043 Equipment and SOP5042 Document management will be updated within Q12024 due to the low risk associated with a change action.

Change Title: Transfer responsibility for environmental monitoring to QC

Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impacted areas of the organisation: Documentation: the SOP and corresponding worksheets must be updated to reflect the change of responsibilities. Agreement between QC Services and the Diagnostic Production site. Refer to change action 07 (ID255).   Identified risks: Unclear responsibilities of who owns the process. This is evaluated to be a minor risk, which will be mitigated by updating the SOP1076, section 3 Responsibilities. Refer to change action 5. The EM process is conducted incorrectly. This is evaluated to be a minor risk since the QC personnel has several years of experience in environmental monitoring of production. Change Title: Decommissioning of Mamut E5 ERP system
Change from:: Visma Mamut E5 is the operating ERP system at Curida Diatec AS used by Sales department for registering of customers, products, and orders from customers and invoicing customers. Logistics department use Mamut for registering of suppliers, products, orders, and stocks of inventory and for audit the trail of materials used in production.
Change to: Visma Mamut E5 system is decommissioned and no longer the operating ERP system for Curida Diatec AS. The license will transfer to read-only version “MamutFree”, which is only available for historical data and reports.
Justification for Change: The main reason for the decommissioning of Mamut is the implementation of a new ERP system, Nova (reference CMID26). Mamut has been the operating ERP system of Curida Diatec AS since 2012. In 2022 Diatec was informed by Visma (software supplier) that they would not keep Mamut in their portfolio. In addition to the system no longer being maintained by the supplier, the Mamut ERP system lacks the capacity to trace materials sufficiently, user transactions and audit trail reports. Mamut is also slow and outdated software. For instance, the user can only have one window up at a time, requiring the user a lot of manual labour to create reports or plan costs efficient purchases.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impact assessment:   1. Product (quality, safety, efficacy) – no impact. 2. Compliance – no impact 3. Facilities, Utilities, equipment – no impact 4. Validation/Qualification:5. Decommissioning of Mamut will change the validated status and the system will be taken out of use. Information in the system will be available in “MamutFree”, with a legacy user to retrieve information if necessary.    Computer system retirement assessment: Supplier Management Changes:No changes. Visma Mamut is not registered as a supplier in the Supplier Management at Curida Diatec AS.  System Overview: Mamut are not connected to any other computer systems in use at the company. Decommissioning of Mamut will only affect the validated status of the system itself, and the valid documents that reference to Mamut.  Quality Risk Management: No data will be lost when decommissioning of Mamut as ERP system.  Computer System retirement strategy: The retirement of Mamut ERP system by Visma has been planned since 2022 and is to be replaced by Nova ERP system by ECI Solutions.  Data and records migration and archiving: From the day of decommissioning, we will have access to “MamutFree”, which will keep all information and data as-is as a reading-view but will not be able to make any changes or create new data. This access is available throughout 2030.     Materials – no impact Specifications – no impact Analytical methods – no impact Manufacturing procedures - no impact EHS - no impact Supply chain – no impact Environmental control – no impact Documentation: Following documents needs to be updated: - SOP 5003 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - SOP 5010 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - Doc 5010.2 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - SOP 5011 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - Doc 5011.5 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - SOP 5021 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - SOP 5022 – to be obsoleted  - SOP 5024 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - SOP 5025 – to be obsoleted   - SOP 5036 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   - SOP 5046 – remove reference to Mamut and replace with generic “ERP system”   Training – training modules which refer to Mamut software have to be updated External parties – no impact   No risks identified with this change. No data will be lost when decommissioning Mamut. At the day for decommissioning, the current license of Mamut will automatically be changed to a free, reading version of Mamut (MamutFree) with all information intact as is. This can be extended if needed. Access to MamutFree is available throughout 2030. The risk for implementation of new ERP system is handled in CMID: 26, with a risk assessment included to identify and mitigate actions. Interim period (from when Nova is live to end of Mamut Licence in 31.DEC.2023): · Mamut will still be accessible after the completion of Nova implementation until 31.DEC.2023 for the following limited activities only:  o Closing of existing open sales orders and purchase orders (at shipping and invoicing for sales orders/reception of purchase orders these will be re-created in Nova and processed according to valid procedures for each tasks).  o Extracting required information related to stock and materials to be transferred to Nova if required. o Note: The users who will continue to have access to Mamut after complete implementation of nova (Approval of CR1201 part C) will be notified that they are not to create any new orders or other types of records within Mamut after this point. This will be communicated to relevant persons with access to Mamut and acknowledgement of this notification will be documented within this CR. ·  Access to Mamut will be limited (After CR1201 is closed and Nova is live) to the following users only (until 31.DEC.2023):  § ACS – logistics Clinical/QC § MHa – logistics Clinical/QC  § JLF – logistics Diagnostics/MS&T  § YNE – logistics Diagnostics/MS&T  § ADS – Sales § MIB – Sales § EFP – Finance controller The users listed are considered to be necessary to be able to maintain daily operations in this interim period – it is also taken into consideration to secure access if anyone is on sick-leave or absent by any reason. Change Title: Temporary change for use of expired material: pH reference standard pH 6,86 (e.g. 107202 Merck Millipore)
Change from:: Use of pH reference standard pH 6,86 (e.g. 107202 Merck Millipore) within expiry date
Change to: Use of expired pH reference standard pH 6,86 (e.g. 107202 Merck Millipore) expired on 31.AUG.2023
Justification for Change: Current lot of 6.86 reference standard available in-house expired (on 31.aug.2023). A new order for 6.86 ref standard was sent to supplier on 02.AUG.2023, which usually would be more than enough time to ensure that new lot would be in-house prior to expiry of the product. However, supplier has not been able to provide new lot of the material and ETA is constantly changing. Therefore to continue to be able to measure pH in production and for buffer production, the expired lot will have to be used.   A certified reference standard is used during calibration of an off-line pH meter. A certified reference buffer provides a known and stable pH value that serves as a reference point for calibrating the pH meter. The reference standard is not used to calibrate the pH meter itself, but verify that calibration is within range, and measurements are accurate and reliable Manufacturer of the reference standard state 3 years expiry date on its CoA (Attachment 1). That means that they do not guarantee the quality after that date. The likelihood of the reference standard to have shifted pH value drastically over the span of 1 week after being stable for 3 years is very small. It is therefore considered a very small risk to use this expired product for a short while as we await new lot to be in-house.   Impact assessment:   No impact is identified for the following categories: Process of measuring pH: no impact to the process of calibrating and measuring the pH, as the standard procedure will be followed, but expired goods will be used. facility: same material as before is used HSE: same material as before is used Qualifications/Validation status: pH meters are calibrated prior to each use, as material is recently expired the possible drifting of the standard is considered to be minimal and reference standard is used to verify that calibration is within specification   Impacted areas: Logistics: all expired 6.86 reference standards are to be labelled to be used according to CMID42", see action 198 and as soon as it is possible to get non-expired reference standard, the expired lot should be removed from the shelfs and discarded, action 200 Documentation: for traceability a comment with a reference to this CMID needs to be added to all worksheets and batch documentation involved while this temporary change is active. This will be ensured by performing training of the production team, action 199   Risk assessment and possible mitigating actions: The point of using a reference standard for pH calibration is to ensure the accuracy of pH measurements. When a reference standard has expired,  it may not provide the exact pH value as it should, leading to inaccurate pH measurements. Even if a recently expired reference standard appears to give pH values close to the expected values, there is a risk of calibration drift over time. This can lead to unreliable pH measurements. As the reference standard is newly expired this is considered an acceptable risk, and material will be moved out of stock as soon as non-expired material is received, action 200 and all operators will be informed of the end of temporary change, action 201.   pH is measured for AcuSyst production to monitor the health of the production unit. According to SOP0060 v3.0 the AcuSyst should be monitored by pH measuring multiple times per week and for that the pH meter needs to be accurately calibrated. It has a direct impact on the production, where in-line pH measurement is adjusted according to off-line value. However, only one AcuSyst unit is running at this time and the production is in a stable period where pH does not vary much off-line to in-line, but for the health of the production it is beneficial to do these minor adjustments instead of few larger adjustments. It can also have a potential impact to not to measure the pH and adjust it as the in-line measurement of the AcuSyst tends to stay the same despite variations when measuring off-line. As the possible drifting of the 6.86 value is expected to be minimal so close to the expiry date, it is a higher risk to not monitor the AcuSyst production than to have a minor calibration shift. pH meter is also used to measure pH in buffers used for in process measurements, QC analysis and final product. As material is recently expired the possible drifting of the standard is considered to be minimal and expired reference standard can be used to verify that calibration of equipment is within specification."
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Expired material can be used immediately after this change is approved.
Planned implementation data - comment: implementation is effective immediately after approval

Change Title: Implementation of ultra freezer DIA409
Change from:: The Diagnostic site of Curida Diatec is currently using Nuaire ultralow temperature freezer (DIA261) for storage at -75˚C. The freezer is located in Room204/BIA room. The freezer is used to store raw materials used for production as well as final products requiring temperature of -70 to -80˚°C.
Change to: New Brunswick -86° C Freezer will be implemented in the diagnostic site of Curida Diatec for storage at -66˚C to -80˚C. This freezer will replace the currently used ultralow temperature freezer (DIA261). The freezer is located in room 204/BIA room.
Justification for Change: The ultralow temperature freezer (DIA261) which is currently in use had a technical failure and the temperature in the freezer rose to -30C from -75C (Ref. NC184). The instrument was critical and had to be replaced immediately.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Impact assessment:  According to SOP5038 this is a major change, as additional validation or qualification activities are to be completed before implementation.   Service providers/service: External service is performed annually by Langeland Lab Service AS (LLS), which is a Curida Diatec-approved supplier. The maintained temperature should be continuously monitored using the Sirius system. A trend review of the temperature will be performed in a defined time frame, in order to detect any changes in instrument performance. In case of observed malfunction / under-performance the instrument will be repaired or replaced. No further actions are needed. Validation:  Equipment should be qualified according to SOP5018: Qualification and validation activities  a. Installation: performed in-house by Curida Diatec employees   b. Operation: performed in-house by Curida Diatec employees   c. Performance: performed in-house by Curida Diatec employees   Process/product/security: ActionID 195: The ultralow temperature freezer will be used to store the final product, therefore the freezer should be equipped with a lock according to SOP1098. Access to it should be distributed to dedicated personnel (Production, Sales and QA).    Documentation: ActionID 194: Equipment must be implemented in the Diagnostic site QMS according to SOP5043: Equipment. Form DOC5043.2: Instrument implementation must be filled out. An IQOQPQ protocol and report must be carried out and documented.  ActionID 335: SOP4045 must be updated: - Section 4: Add use of gloves when handling racks in the ultra freezer. - Section 6.1: Removing mention of the previous faulty freezer (DIA261) - Section 9.6.2: The ultra freezer is now connected to an emergency power unit (UPS) - General clean up of document, see logo etc.  HSE:  Protective gloves should be used when taking racks out of the ultralow temperature freezer, see ActionID 335.  Facility:  The freezer requires ambient temperatures in the room. Space, electrical power requirements and utilities are defined and addressed in IQ.  Risk assessment: HSE: Cold to touch materials. Mitigating action is to use protective gloves when handling cold materials, see ActionID 335. The instrument is a second-hand instrument which means that there is possible wear and tear on the performance. If the freezer fails, this will ruin materials and possibly also finished products. This is a high risk which needs to be mitigated. These actions will mitigate the risk, see ActionID 196:- Correct voltage to use the instrument (208-230V and 60 Hz) will have to  be ensured. This is secured as the power is supplied through a UPS  unit. - In case of power failure, the power output is also monitored by a facility monitoring system logger. This logger will notify by SMS and e-mail if there is a power failure. - The air temperature inside the freezer will be monitored continuously with facility monitoring system 	loggers. The facility monitoring system will automatically alarm by SMS and e-mail in case of a temperature threshold breach.  Selected Curida Diatec personnel will thus be notified in case of a power failure and high to take appropriate action.  - There is always a risk of temperature fluctuation in a freezer caused by on/off cycle of the compressor and the regular opening/closing of the freezer. The openings and closing of the freezer will not impact frozen material even though the air temperature might fluctuate. The full capacity of the freezer is 760 litres.Additional information:   The Nuaire ultralow temperature freezer (DIA261, 050678) stopped working on Friday 04.AUG.2023, after working-hours (Ref. NC184). This was discovered on the following Monday, 07.AUG.2023. The New Brunswick ultralow temperature freezer (DIA409, C760-86, 1005-6620-0510) was purchased second-hand by Curida Diatec, 22.APR.2021, from Langeland Lab service (LLS) as a back-up freezer and stored at Atlas Spedisjon, before moved to 2Park area FBU on Monday, 07.AUG.2023, and taken into use Friday, 11.AUG.2023.   It was decided to decommission the Nuaire ultralow temperature freezer (DIA261, 050678) and remove it from site, 02.MAY.2024. It was replaced by the New Brunswick ultralow temperature freezer (DIA409, C760-86, 1005-6620-0510) in room 204..
Planned implementation data - comment: Setting the implementation date a month from now to ensure proper time to complete actions, as the actions are divided onto several persons, some of whom is on sick leave.
