Change Title: Update on SOP1064 Infection in production
Change from:: A major revision of SOP1064, version 4.0 has identified the following: - No information on handling of mycoplasma positive samples - Sample are to be before first decontamination and continue monitoring ~2-3 times per week for at least 14 days after last infection was observed. - Cell culture suspected to be infected are incubated in Quarantine incubator in analysis lab. Incubation for at least 10 days without opening unit for splitting will show if unit is infected or not.  - No information on who makes the decision on using UV light - Decontamination actions should continue for 14 days after last infection was observed, before returning back to standard cleaning routines.
Change to: 1. Add how to handle mycoplasma positive samples (CAPA28). This entails the following: 2. Change of decontamination and sampling regime from 2-3x per week for 14 days to until two consecutive clean from contamination status" after decontamination.  3.  Add a requirement for double control of the suspected unit and how to proceed if inspection is inconclusive (incubate in quarantine incubator). 4. Add clarification of who decides when to use UV-lights (production management). 5. Responsibilities (QA removed from to be informed about possible infections, Qa is informed by Non-conformances raised) 6. minor updates to language and layout"
Justification for Change: This change control document outlines the updates made to the SOP for handling bacterial, fungal, and mycoplasma contamination in production facilities. Changes improve clarity and scope of procedure, expanding to include more specific instructions for sampling, decontamination, and documentation, as well as handling of infected material and disinfection and update safety measures for handling cleaning agents and high-risk materials. Summary of changes are as follow:
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Product quality: possible impact to product quality if a production unit is in quarantine for an extended period of time while performing an investigation. Change action 125: include instructions to consider the criticality of the production unit prior to placing it in quarantine  Personnel Training: All production and facility personnel must be retrained in new procedures (change action 216). The training module for infection in production needs to be updated to reflect the changes to the procedure (change action 246).  Qualification and Validation: no impact on qualification or validation status of equipment, facility or process.  Equipment and facility: sampling and decontamination time is reduced from (2-3x per week for 14 days to 2 consecutive samplings confirm infection free areas). This reduces time and resources used for sampling and decontamination, as well as ware and tare on equipment and facility due to extesinve exposure to harsh chemicals. Risk of increased infection due to reduced sampling/decontamination is considered very low and acceptable, as 2 consecutive samplings eliminate the possibility of it being a one-off chance to not detect the infection. The production facility is monitored weekly for microbiological background according to SOP1076. This routine monitorings further reduces the risk of infection in the facilities going undetected.  Documentation: SOP1064 must be updated with the changes outlined here. Anchored in Change action 125. Doc1064.1 will be updated to reflect the new procedure, according to change action 518. No other SOPs, Worksheets or logbooks are impacted.  EHS: No impact as no new chemicals are introduced, and changes made to the procedure enhance the safety of the environment and personnel with clearer and more detailed instructions.
Planned implementation data - comment: NA

Change Title: Implementation of new ERP system Nova
Change from:: Curida Diatec is currently using «Mamut» delivered by supplier Visma. Mamut E5 is an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system used by logistics for planning, purchasing, handling of material movements and transactions. Mamut is also used by Sales Department for invoicing and shipments to customers. Visma is phasing out Mamut as Visma has moved on to new projects and will no longer support Mamut to as expected by Curida Diatec. Project “​PDIA1003 Starship Enterprise (RP)” was started to investigate new alternative systems. After evaluating multiple suppliers and systems, “Nova” delivered by Supplier “ECI solutions” was chosen. This project is lead by, and was initiated by, the Clinical production site operating at GMP level. Since Clinical production site and Diagnostics production site per today operates in separate QMS systems, this change is based on the Change Report from the  Clinical QMS paper-based CR1201 (attached). AS-IS: System name: Mamut E5System application: Used for planning, purchasing, sales and handling  of material movements and transactions. Electronic material management tool.Supplier: Visma Norge AS
Change to: TO-BE: System name: NovaSystem application: Will be used for planning, purchasing, sales and handling of material movements and transactions. Electronic material management tool.Supplier: ECI Solution AS
Justification for Change: Visma Norge AS is phasing out Mamut as Visma the system will be obsolete in 3-4 years.Mamut is currently shared with Curida Diatec Clinical. Diagnostics and Clinical have separate QMS systems and requirements and guidelines to adhere to. The Diagnostics site has access to manage, change or delete Curida Diatec Clinical stock, and vice versa. Unauthorized changes and mix-ups in the system are prone to happen. In addition, Mamut does not meet current GMP Guidelines or requirements for IT Security and Data Integrity (21 CFR Part 11, EU GMP, WHO GDP ect.). The Mamut ERP system lacks capacity to sufficiently trace materials, user transactions and audit trail reports. Thus, CSV cannot be performed on Mamut. Mamut ERP system is also slow and outdated. For instance, the user can only have one window up at a time. Requiring the user, a lot of manual labor to create reports or plan cost efficient purchases.    Impacted areas within the organization:Validation status: New ERP sysmtem Nova requires new validation. Computer system validation is not required by ISO 9001:2015 standard, however, the system will also be implemented at the Clinical site operating according to GMP pharma guidelines. Therefore the validation which will be performed at the clinial site should be sufficient. Validation reports will be inspected and evaluated prior to the implementation at the Diagnostics site.Logistics and Sales: new procedures for handling of suppliers and customers. Incoming and outgoing orders. Material handling. This will be handled in the CR action ID 121.Facility: No impactIT sequrity: Its status and/or impact will be assessed by the IT service provider, Braathe AS which is an approved supplier for both Curida Diatec sites. The impact will be evaluated and addressed by Braathe, and a part of the supplier qualification by the QA team for both sites in Curida Diatec.  Production and QC: no changes for the production processes - material withdrawal from storages to production will be performed as before.QA: Responsible for the supplier qualification and approval for both Braathe and ECI Solution, supplier of Nova ERP. QA team will evaluate the need for audit of the suppliers. QA is also responsible for final approval of documents for validation, protocols, processes and other documents affected of this change.   Return of investment for this project: New system will meet current requirements for computer systems for the Pharmaceutical industry. This means a system with less gaps and room for error. See URS-2022-1174 for complete list of requirements. Saving resources/man-hours. Paper based procedures can be performed electronically (like goods receipt). Automated printing of labels will save time (no manual signing and reduce NCs related to labels.) Will help perform cost effective and on-time planning of purchases. Will lower risk of “stock out” situations drastically as current system cannot provide cost/Inventory/benefit analysis or reports.   Negative impact of new system:As a result of the transition from Mamut to Nova, we will loose the historical tracking of the material audit trail. At obsoletion of Mamut we will get some reports of historical data, that will be available to search in if needed. This is considered to be accepted.   Risk assassment: IT sequrity/access: There will be limitations of access to the different modules in Nova ERP system, based on role in the company and function. A non-authorized person will not have access to the ERP system, and all changes and enterings are audit trailed, deleted information can be tracked and restored. The Nova ERP system is also to be implemented at the Clinical site of Curida Diatec, operating by strict pharma restrictions and requirements. The Diagnostics production site evaluate the validation processes and protocols from the Clinical site before approval and final implementation. The QA team at Curida Diatec will revise the agreement and qualification of Braathe AS, and also assess the approval of ECI Solution AS, supplier of Nova ERP system. This is a part of CR action ID 123.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Scope of this change:Implement Nova as the new ERP system with suitable qualification.Transfer existing stock into the new ERP system.Evaluate status of open orders within Mamut and historical records and scope for transfer or archiving of historical records. Updates of documents to phase out Mamut E5 as the primary ERP system after replacing it with Nova. Out of scope:The system will also be installed for Curida Diatec Clinical at Fornebu, but Clinical’s implementation must be handled according to Clinicals QMS and are outside of the scope of this CR.Mamut E5 will not be decommissioned as part of this CR immediately as it needs to be maintained active until a suitable assessment about legacy records from historical activities and current open records within this system is performed. Based on the outcome of this assessment a separate CR will be raised for decommissioning this software. However, after completion of qualification of the Nova software platform is complete and it has been implemented, Mamut E5 will no longer be used for any new activities and will only be maintained active with a single active admin user account for legacy activity management for a temporary period, until it is decommissioned fully. Nova will be the primary software platform within Curida Diatec for planning, purchasing, sales and handling of material movements and transactions (Electronic material management tool).  Both the current SOP for Mamut E5 and the new SOP for the Nova system will be valid within the Curida Diatec GMP QMS. However only the Nova SOP will be followed for any new orders. The Mamut E5 SOP will be retired within the separate CR which will be raised for decommissioning of the software.   Impact on YesNoValidation  X   Process/Product    X Documentation  X   Specifications    X Analytical Methods    X Manufacturing procedures    X EHS   X Other parts of the organization  X   External partners   X
Planned implementation data - comment: The planned date for GO LIVE is 28 August 2023 (confirmed by email with the Head of operation).

Change Title: Temporary change for service frequency of AcuSyst Maximizers
Change from:: Annual service of AcuSyst Maximizers and Xcellerator by external service supplier due in february and may 2023 (see table below).   Instrument Calibration due    DIA029  11/FEB/2023  DIA079  11/FEB/2023  DIA202  11/FEB/2023  DIA203  11/FEB/2023  DIA277 (ASX)    25/MAY/2023  last day to perform service according to valid version of SOP5043: 11.MAR.2023/25.JUN.2023.
Change to: Postpone service of MAximizers and Xcellerator until July 2023
Justification for Change: Aiming for a production downtime in July every year is considered beneficial to ensure better resource coverage throughout the year. During this downtime, the Maximisers and Xcellerator should be serviced as service/Calibration requires the instruments to be available and not in production. Only production ongoing in Maximizers as of March 2023, is 2D2  (project 2022-33). A test production is planned to start in march 2023 in Xcellerator and run until Service in July. In 2021 due to the Covid pandemic, the Service of AcuSyst was postponed for many months as it was difficult to get a service supplier to Norway due to travel restrictions. At this time, a risk assessment was performed (QRM-2021-136) where the risks identified were related to the production timeline and not product quality. The risk assessment scored identified risks low and acceptable without any further actions. Production in AcuSyst Maximizer is upstream and in the first phase of the production chain. The risks are evaluated as highest related to productivity and not product quality. The harvest will be purified, and purity tests will be performed on all customer batches. The main risk of using the Maximizers without the annual calibrations is that production can stop before planned or produce less product than expected. The monitoring of the instruments will give info when this is happening. The quality of the product is monitored in the QC analysis before batch approval. This risk assessment form 2021 is still relevant as the Service is performed similarly, the risks are the same and are still considered acceptable for the four months until Service is planned.              Furthermore, Curida Diatec has been in contact with other companies using AcuSyst system and received the information that they have less frequent calibration/service (2-5 years) without any issues, which supports the QRM-2021-136 conclusion.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: The pause of calibration&service of the Acus System instrument is planned to align service activities to the production timeline.No risk to product quality is unlikely, as documented in the attached risk analysis.

Change Title: Generelize formulation of pNPP substrate used

Change to: Specify concentration and kind of substrate (pNPP) in SOP1007/1097/1039/1025/1014, without mentioning formulation of the substrate. Hence powder, tablets and liquid pNPP can be used if the type and final concentration of substrate reagent is the same.
Justification for Change: Justification for change: There are delivery difficulties with pNPP substrate (CAT: S0942-200TAB), which use is specified in all ELISA procedures (SOP1007/1097/1039/1025/1014), both in process tests and QC testing. If substrate is not available, it prevents the production from performing the necessary IPC and QC analysis according to the procedures. Since these analysis are required for release of product, all of the ELISA SOPs will be updated to only include mention of substrate kind and appropriate concentration, instead of formulation of pNPP substrate in tablet form. Risk assessment: 1.Facility New reagents will be stored according to suppliers recommendation and storage is not at capacity. Chemical is not hazardus for the environment, Facility is not affected by this change.   2.HSE According to the safety data-sheet, pNPP does not pose any hazardous risk to health, safety or the environment. Hence, there are no specific considerations needed for HSE purposes. 3. Suppliers Thermo fisher scientific, an approved supplier of Curida Diatec Diagnostics department, is the supplier for both new and old pNPP substrate, therefore there is no impact to suppliers. 4. Quality Managment System (QMS) QMS is not affected by this change, other than the necessary update of procedures.   5. Personnel Personnel who perform ELISA analysis will need retraining in the new procedure. 6. Logistics Product is from an already approved supplier, therefore the product only needs to be registered in our Mamut system prior to purchase and use (CR action 114). 7. Qualification/Validation As the analysis method itself is not changed, already approved instruments (pipettes, scales, ELISA equipment) will be used, therefore this change does not impact the Qualification/Validation status of instruments or method. 8. Production The chemical changed to is like-to-like, that is the same product just in a different formulations therefore production is not affected. 9. Equipment As the instruments required for change of chemical formulation are already in use for similar purposes, and have SOPs and maintenance schedules this change does not impact the instruments. Traceability of instruments used for weighing of chemicals will be ensured by specifying DIA-number used in ELISA worksheet (CR action 115-116), as is done when other reagents are prepared. More than one scale is at our disposal and can be used for the task at hand, as alternatives. 10. Training Before the change can be implemented in production training of this process must be ensured. The addition of equipment requires all operators without training in this equipment to receive it. And change in procedure require re-training of operators performing this analysis. The use of scales is part of the introductory laboratory training, and all current operators have completed this training module. Hence, no training of operators is necessary at this moment. If SOPs is updated and approved, all operators will receive retraining in the changes made as is common practice. To ensure that future operators also receive the appropriate training, the training module for ELISA will be changed to emphasize that training in use of scales is obligatory. 11. Procedure When a process is changed, the procedure and supporting documents must be revised. The change might increase the difficulty of preparing this reagent. Which can cause more failure in preparing the reagent which affect the quality of ELISA analysis. All ELISA documents will be updated (CR action 109-113) and verified before any change in process is made. Likewise, re-training in new SOPs will be performed before they are valid (CR action 108). The change in procedure is minor and the increase in difficulty is also small. If the preparation of the reagent is performed correctly, the assay’s reliability and precision is not affected by this change. The substrate reagent has two ingredients and is not know for solubility issues. the required education and experience in laboratory work for all operators at Curida Diatec, as well as training in reagent preparation and scales, is sufficient to mitigate the associated risk. Based on risk assessment, Change actions will be initiated.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: Due to difficulties to secure delivery of ELISA substrate, alternative substrate formulation should be included in the procedures and worksheet for ELISA process. Training in updated procedures to take place prior to use of new formulation.

Change Title: Simplify the Non conformance handling process
Change from:: The nonconformance (NC) process is described and followed according to SOP 5034 Nonconformance. Main changes to the process will impact the listed aspects:1.	There are no clearly defined criteria for which NC should be investigated and which can be closed only with a short description. NC Classification is based on three categories: minor, major and critical2.	Option to forward the investigation to another case worker by using option Investigation further".3.	Lack of trending of NC reoccurrences4.	NC process is split into three parts A – Registration, B – Investigation, verification,  QA Approval, and Batch Disposition, C – CAPA and CAPA Effectiveness evaluation. "
Change to: Clear define criteria for which NC has to be fully investigated, and each can be only registered. Clearly define what information must be included in the NC description for non-investigated NC. CLassife NC to low risk and high risk. Remove the option for further investigation. Only one person will be responsible for the investigation.  Add to NC collection in the BizzMine options to link previously reoccurred NCs, associated procedures, and processes. Replace the division on Part C of Nonconformance. The nonconformance process will be divided into the following part: Registration, Investigation, conclusion and CAPA Initiation, Technical verification, batch disposition, and QA approval.
Justification for Change: Justification Proposed change: Clear define criteria which NC has to be fully investigated and which can be only registered. Clearly defined information which must be included in the NC description for non-investigated NC.  Justification: This change helps to better focus on investigating major/critical NCs and correctly define root causes, leading to higher CAPA effectiveness and small NC reoccurrence. Within 2022, 101 NCs were registered in the Bizzmine eQMS system. 75% of the registered cases were classified as minor (with no impact or unlikely to impact product quality). Half of the minor NCs (42 cases) were fully investigated, and half were closed with a short description and classification justification. Approximately 40 % of all NCs raised were classified as minor, the operators spent 40% of the time on NC process on issues deemed to be insignificant. The employee's and management's feedback on prioritizing the investigation of major/critical NC was positive after one year of testing that option in BizzMine.  However, the lack of clear criteria for when Non-conformances should be investigated was considered difficult for the operator. Therefore, over half of the minor NCs were fully investigated, and 45% were just registered.   Approach: Clear criteria should be defined to streamline the process. Criteria will be used as follows: the non conformances defined as low risk and are not considered as reoccurring can be registered but do not require investigation. The NCs will be considered high-risk NC if fulfilling the following criteria: - Known or potential negative impact on product quality - Known or potential negative impact on employee safety - Known or potential effect on the validated state of equipment/process/system - Without  significant incompliant issues with customer agreements (Product Specification, Quality Agreement, etc) All NC which will be classified as high-risk or reoccurring low-risk NC has to be investigated. The NC is considered to be reoccurring if:  the same type of event or the same process/work area reoccurs more than three times within 12-months, 18-months for the semi-annual event and 3-years for annual events. Risk associated with this change is that NC will be wrongly classified, therefore, an investigation will not be conducted. This can happen if an employee incorrectly defines associated risk or evaluates NC reoccurrence. Mitigation action: All registered NCR will continue to be verified within NC collection in the eQMS system, and the verifier will double-check if the NC was classified correctly and, if possible if the reoccurrence was well evaluated. In addition, reoccurrence evaluation will be strengthened using search and filter function and crosslinked BizzMine collection for process/procedure/instrument/supplier/projects. This will be reflected in the NC collection flow in BizzMine and SOP 5034. Refer to Change action no 104. Additional training will be provided for all employees. Training will be provided tp all employees (refer to action 105).   2.   Proposed change: Remove the option for further investigation. Only one person will be responsible for the investigation. Justification: Currently, this approach is not well used among the team and causes long delays in the NC investigation process. NC is often sent back forward between 2 case workers. It was observed that this has a negative impact on the execution of the NC investigation timeline. Risk: The risk that the NC investigation will be wrongly assigned to a person without knowledge in the field or who is currently absent. The risk is insignificant, as the investigation can be reassigned to a new user on the request using the Forward action" function. This "Forward actions" is already a built-in function in the eQMS system, therefore, no mitigation action is needed. This will be reflected in the NC collection flow in BizzMine and SOP 5034. The collection workflow has to be modified, therefore, the collection's re-validation must be performed. Refer to Change action no 104. The SOP updated is registered under action 106. Mitigation action: NA as no risk is associated with the change.    3.    Proposed change: Add to NC collection in the BizzMine options to link previously reoccurring NCs, associated procedures, and processes. Justification: The trending of NC is currently not efficient. The collected information doesn't provide a good overview of which processes or procedures are most often impacted. Currently used a system based on manual entry data and search for similarities using "search function". In addition, the electronic system is in use for only one year. Therefore the majority of NC is still stored in pdf/word versions on F server. When associated collections are linked, it will be easier to substrate information on how many NCs were registered to each process or procedure. Risk: Event if several collections/list will be linked with NC collection, the data from list will have to be selected manually. NC collection is already linked with other collections like the project list, the instrument list, and the supplier instrument, which allows good trending of all NCs associated with different projects/suppliers/instruments. However, it was experienced that not all information was selected properly when a NC was registered. In addition, not all lists used in production are in BizzMine, therefore, manual entry data will still be required for some information. This will impact trend review in first year of system use.  All associated collections will be linked with NC collection. NC collection will be tested after updated.  Mitigation action:  The SOP5034 will be to be updated. Refer to Change Action 106. In addition, training will be given to emphasise the importance of in selecting associated information from crosslinked lists/collections. 4.            Proposed change:  Replace the division on Part A-C of Nonconformance. The nonconformance process will be divided into the following part: Registration, Investigation, conclusion and CAPA Initiation, Technical verification, batch disposition, and QA approval. The NC will be considered closed when QA approval and batch disposition are performed. CAPA effectiveness evaluation will be performed according to SOP 5039 Corrective action and preventive action. Justification: In the currently used system, NC remains open until all associated CAPAs are evaluated for efficiency. At this same time, CAPA effectiveness is evaluated in the CAPA module in BizzMine. This created double work for personnel who perform effectiveness evaluation (QA personnel).  Risk: The NCR collection has to be updated to reflect the new workflow. CAPA can remain in the system for a long time, and be closed without being performed, which can increase the reoccurrence of the NCs. However, this risk is considered very low, as 98 % of approved for execution CAPA was performed. The CAPA's effectiveness is expected to improve when only major/critical NCs are investigated and a better routine for  NC reoccurrence is introduced. Additional resources will be available in the organization, which can be dedicated to major NC investigations and complex CAPA execution. The change will require the NC collection workflow to be updated and revalidated. Refer to Change action no 104 Mitigation actions: NA as no significant risk is associated with the action.  Impacted area of the organization: Validation status: BizzMine collection workflow has to be redesigned and therefore revalidated.   Documentation: The SOP 5034 has to be updated to reflect the new process.   Personnel: Training must be provided to all employees. Additional training for NC verification personnel and approval must be provided.  "
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: The updated process should be implemented within mid-August. All the actions have to be completed before the implementation date.

Change Title: Preparation of tanks without sartobran filter
Change from:: Preparation of tanks with unsterile tube with Sartobran filter according to SOP2016.
Change to: Addition of alternative way of preparing tanks to SOP2016. Without Sartobran filter, but rather use of steam sterilization (autoclave) of the tube to prevent infection.
Justification for Change: Delivery issues of sartobran filters has consequently produced the need for alternative ways of preparing tanks. The purpose of Sartobran 300 filter in this protocol is to preserve the tanks sterility and prevent infection of Acusyst productions. This can also be accomplished by proper sterile techniques and steam sterilization. Our protocols for autoclaving and sterile technique, hereby SOP4037 and SOP4005 respectively, are well established and effective. On the other hand, the current procedure for tank preparation has some challenges. The tubing is rinsed with RO-water rather than sterilized before use. Which calls for bringing unsterile materials into the LAF bench. Steam sterilization is considered as effective as current practice and the risk of infection is low with already established protocols for sterile technique and autoclaving. CAPA is opened for making the necessary changes (Ref. CAPA91).
Impacted areas of the organization and r: SOP2016 Version 2.0 point 9.5 describes the current procedure for preparing tank.
Planned implementation data - comment: The update of the procedure was performed through CAPA91, and the updated procedure was valid from 03.11.2023. Therefore this Change is to be voided.

Change Title: Change of low-risk supplier qualification and requalification criteria
Change from:: Currently, the low-risk supplier requires qualifications based on reputation, questionnaire or certification. Moreover, the requalification has to be performed periodically every five years
Change to: The the low-risk supplier will not requires qualifications based on reputation, questionnaire or certification. Moreover, the requalification doesn't have to be performed for low-risk supplier
Justification for Change: The supplier qualification is conducted according to SOP5026 Supplier Quality Program. The supplier qualification criteria and requalification period are defined in the procedure and depend on supplied material/service associated risk. According to the current version of the procedure, version 2.0 low-risk suppliers should be qualified based on reputation, history, certification, and filled quality questionnaire. All approved low-risk supplier has to be requalified every five years.    The currently used criteria were defined when one Quality System covered both ISO (currently named Diagnostic) and GMP (currently named clinical) sites. The procedure had to comply with the GMP-defined criteria for Supplier Management. However, the sites were separated in 2019, and two independent quality systems were established. There is no more need to comply with GMP criteria at the Diagnostic site, therefore, criteria were re-evaluated and adapted to the current operation process. Currently, the number of supplier complaints is considered low and does not exceed three complaints per year (refer to management review reports). Moreover, no non-conformance related to low-risk material was reported in the last two years.    The criteria are considered too strict for the current operation of Curida Diatec, a Diagnostic site. Therefore qualification and requalification criteria for low-risk suppliers will be changed. The qualification and requalification for medium and high-risk suppliers will remain the same.    The risk associated with the change for low-risk supplier qualification/requalification is considered minor. Supplied low-risk materials have no direct impact on product quality. Moreover, the defect in supplied service or material can be easily detected or fixed. The low-risk suppliers will still have to be listed in the Approved Supplier List, and the associated risk to supplied material/service has to be documented using the Supplier Qualification form for each low-risk supplier.    The change in procedure will reduce the time needed for low-risk supplier qualification and requalification. As reported in NCR42 and NCR78, the current supplier qualification system has not been properly followed up since 2019 due to insufficient resources and very strict qualification demand/criteria. The resources will be used to perform more thorough qualification/requalification of more critical suppliers when the low-risk-supplier criteria are loosed.         Risk management: The main risk associated with change is: 1) Use an unapproved supplier to purchase goods or services. The risk of using an unapproved supplier (outside the approved supplier list) is considered minor. Each low-risk supplier still has to be listed in the Approved Supplier List, supplier classification has to be documented using the Supplier Qualification form connected with the Approved Supplier List. This task will be performed by a trained employee who will also update the Approved Supplier List (refer to action 102). Moreover, the procedure SOP5026 will be updated (refer to change action 101, and proper training will be given to all impacted departments: Logistics, QA, and Production (refer to change action 100). The logistics department orders service or materials using the ERP system and ensure the ERP system complies with the Approved Supplier List. 2) Low-quality of supplied material/service. The change only impacts low-risk suppliers, which supply material/service without directly impacting final product quality, or each defect/low quality can be easily detected. Each of supplier will be categorized based on the type of supplied material/service and associated risk. The process will be documented using the Supplier qualification form (refer to change action 102). Therefore the risk is considered minor.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: The process was updated in February 2023 as planned. However, the training of employees was delayed and completed within August 2023. The reason of the delay was the digitalization of the process, which was conducted according to the CMID12 and the lack of resources within the organisation (the change was down prortised).

Change Title: Change of 3P Storage for RPMI medium (1L, 100L, 200L)
Change from:: RPMI medium (1L, 100L, 200L) QC released from Life Technologies are stored at their production site in Inchinnan, and released batch are shipped to Curida Diatec according to Call off-orders of requested amount of medium.
Change to: RPMI medium (1L, 100L, 200L) QC released from Life Technologies - full batch are shipped to Norsk Medisinal Depot AS, 3P storage for Curida Diatec.
Justification for Change: Life Technologies AS is the supplier of Curida Diatec's standard medium RPMI 1640 (cat no 04194218A (1L), 04194218BX (100L), 04194218DX (200L)), this is manufactured in their production site: Life Technologies Limited, 3 Fountain Drive, Inchinnan Business Park, Paisley PA4 9RF UNITED KINGDOM. This custom-made medium has been the standard medium for Curida Diatec since approximately 1996/1997, and as the ordering increased during the years, there was a limit to the number of units that could be delivered at Curida Diactec's premises at the time. It was then established an agreement of that a full batch of medium QC released at Inchinnan was to be stored at Life Technologies premises, and the stock was called off for shipping and delivery at Curida Diatec when needed. There is no documentation of this agreement, and Curida Diatec has not been invoiced for this storage for all years. By the end of December 2021, Life Technologies Inchinnan ended this agreement due to a lack of space at their premises. All remaining stock of RPMI units where shipped to Norsk Medisinal Depot AS (NMD), in Oslo, Norway. NMD is an approved supplier for Curida Diatec and are used as a 3P storage of medium and other supplies. The change of 3P storage gives Curida Diatec a better oversight of the stock level of all sizes of RPMI, and NMD sends monthly reports of stock level and transfers by e-mail. This service also includes notice if any damages are observed and provide a high-quality storage with guaranteed +2-8 degrees Celsius. NMD is a cGMP-qualified 3P storage with high quality and control of its facilities and documentation.  When stored at Inchinnan, Life Technologies, we had no records of storage temperature nor regular updates of stock level, and did experience problems with ordering/transport of call off several times, see SCR050 and SCR052 for examples. Therefore this change of 3P storage for our released standard medium RPMI 1640 are considered to be an increase in quality and control of our stock of standard medium and entails a lower risk of that production being impacted because of transport delays. This change also provides us with better security and flexibility in production since we can order delivery of medium and have it delivered the next day. There are no other risks identified with this change.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NMD provides transport of medium to customers, but Curida Diatec AS will continue to use Atlas Spedisjon AS for transportation from NMD to Curida Diatec FBU - no change from previous version of SOP5046. NMD's transport services will be used as a back-up solution for Atlas Spedisjon AS in case of sickness, vacation or any other reason.
Planned implementation data - comment: The change of 3P storage was implemented in January 2022. The Change was implemented before Change was approved for initiation. This was reported as NCR100.

Change Title: Refinement of the siririus monitoring system and SOP4032
Change from:: The current routine and protocol of the MySirius Monitoring System following SOP4032. The routine surrounding Sirius monitoring involves the super-user checking the JRI cloud for Technical alarms" and that all instruments are within "Acceptance criteria" once a day. Additionally the data for each quarter is reviewed for deviations and trends. "
Change to: Establish alarms for communication errors and out of limit, to be notified when loggers go offline or when instruments are approaching alarm threshold respectively. Make a separation for criticality of alarms and establish protocol for how to handle the different alarms. Changes are made to prevent further non conformances regarding system failure, review of data and notification of operators.
Justification for Change: An increased number of NCR related to the Sirius monitoring system has been observed in the last year. This includes NCR ID: 8/26/28/36/60/64/77, some with identified issues were reoccurring. The NCR investigation identified several major issues with the currently used protocol (procedure), which CAPA and this change control will address.  Based on these non-conformances, the following issues were defined and listed below. For each defined issue, the following solution is recommended.  Observed issue 1: Some instruments, like incubators, are frequently turned on and off depending on the ongoing production load. All incubators are constantly monitored, and alarms go off even though the instrument is turned off. Currently, the responsible person has to keep a list of which instruments are turned off and disregard these alarms. This approach proves chaotic and difficult to follow. The alarm for instruments that are turned off distracts from the task production operator. ·        Suggested solution: Utilize JRI clouds alarm criticality function, so non critical alarms" hereby offline equipment are notified differently than "critical alarms". Criticality is a function in software that corresponds to an instrument being off/on. The alarm will be considered not critical if is coming from the switch-off instrument.  This will make the panel more organized and cause less confusion about units to monitor. This requires a system to notify super-user when equipment is turned on/off so its status in JRI cloud can be updated. ·        Risk assessment: Changing the criticality of different equipment has the risk of not being up to date. Misinformation can cause critical alarms to be overlooked during monitoring. However, this is not a change in the current system since it already relies on operators telling the super-user which instruments are in use. ·        Mitigative action: More clearly define how to communicate the instruments state to super-user, by updating instrument worksheets to include this and establishing a routine like a point in the weekly laboratory meeting (change action 91). Observed issue 2: Every now and then, Sirius loggers go offline and do not transmit data to the JRI cloud. When this happens, it is sometimes possible to recover lost data. It is not explained in the protocol how to do this, nor how to handle it when it is impossible. This gap in the procedure has led to non-conformances being written every time this type of incident occurs. ·        Suggested solution: Update the procedure to include how to download lost data and how to evaluate a risk related to the alarm. Create a system for handling loggers going offline. Change the battery percentage limit in the SOP from 10% to 20% (the next available limit). This since loggers go offline more frequently at lower battery percentages. ·        Risk assessment: There is no risk associated with this change, however, it will require re-training of operators involved with the Sirius monitoring system (change action 92).  Observed issue 3: There is currently no alarm set up for when the Sirius loggers go offline and stop transmitting to the JRI cloud. All instances of this has been identified during quarterly trend review, often after a potentially impacted batch has been released. ·        Suggested solution: Set up alarms for communication errors in JRI cloud and update the SOP hereafter. ·        Risk assessment: There is no risk associated with this change, however, it will require re-training of operators involved with the Sirius monitoring system (change action 93).  Observed issue 4: During trend review, it is unknown how to read comments written when alarms are acknowledged. This gap makes it difficult to approve the review with unanswered questions. ·        Suggested solution: Update the SOP to include instruction on analyzing data during trend review and see comments attached to acknowledgments. ·        Risk assessment: There is no risk associated with this change, however, it will require re-training of operators involved with the Sirius monitoring system (change action 94).  Observed issue 5: Currently, super-user monitors Sirius daily, and production operators are not notified of alarms when they occur. The delay makes it impossible to rectify the situations when they occur. ·        Suggested solution: Evaluate if it is necessary to have notifications by calling or texts when certain equipment are outside threshold specifications. If before mentioned, notifications should alert during/outside working hours. Which equipment is prioritized and if alarms should follow the same acceptance criteria? ·        Risk assessment: SMS/Phone based alert system will reduce the risk of critical alarms going unnoticed. However, it has to be clarified who should handle the alarms on a daily basis and outside working hours if this is deemed necessary. The changes in roles and responsibilities can cause critical alarms to be overlooked. Additionally, it is a risk of false alarms if the different thresholds are not well enough established. There is also a risk of exacerbating the limited laboratory resources since frequent alarms can cause longer working hours.  ·        Mitigative action: Define clear responsibilities for different Sirius operators. Evaluate if job descriptions have to be updated. Enquire the legal requirements to which the company has to comply concerning operators having to rectify alarms outside working hours. Evaluate which instruments and kinds of alarms are considered critical and establish guidelines for acting on said alarms (change action 95).   Observed issue 6: Alarm limits are set to threshold specifications for all equipment, This means that alarms only occur when the instrument is outside its allowed limits. ·        Suggested solution: Set up "out of limit" alarms in addition to the current "out of threshold" alarms for critical equipment. Make a proposal for alarm limits and update the SOP and JRI cloud. ·        Risk assessment: More alarms mean more time and resources used for monitoring. Therefore the limits for the dedicated instruments have to be established with taking into consideration both, risk and benefits.                                                                                                                  ·        Mitigative action: Conduct a risk-benefit analysis to evaluate if out-of-limit alarms should be established for critical equipment (change action 96). "
Impacted areas of the organization and r: The sirius alarm system is composed of JRI cloud which is a panel for controlling the systems specifications and monitoring every unit under surveillance, The sirius loggers, responsible for storing and sending data to the cloud solution. The sirius sensors, which monitor temperature/CO2/humidity of each unit. And lastly the units (equipment) in the laboratory that is monitored, these include freezers, refrigerators, rooms and incubators.
Planned implementation data - comment: NA

Change Title: Introduction of section for Reinoculation of ASM" in SOP0060"
Change from:: Procedure to re-inoculate flowpath in ASM is not described in the SOP0060.
Change to: Introduce a procedure to re-inoculate flowpath for ASM in SOP0060.
Justification for Change: There are situations where it is desirable to reinoculate ASM which is described in operational manual from manufacturer.  According to operation manual, page 6-97, Seeding a BRX more than once may be considered an option in the following situations: low inoculum density and/or viability, poor initial growth, re-use of the flowpath. Ideally the first situation should be avoided, but time constraints or other factors may take precedence over preparing a suitable inoculum. In the other two situations, it may be best to flush the cells out of the BRXs before re-use, although cleaning a BRX that was densely populated is usually difficult and may not be recommended because of manufacturing regulations involving the desired product. Poor initial growth in the MAXIMIZER culture may be attributed to many conditions that can be avoided before inoculation".  Positive impact 1) Starting of ASM is an expensive procedure. Flowpath for ASM is a single-use and expensive disposable cultureware component. It can be reinoculated with same clone on certain conditions mentioned in the manual. So it is economically beneficial to reinoculate the flowpath. 2) It takes several days to prepare inoculum, and workforce to start a new ASM and inoculate it. Thus, it can save time, resource and production efficiency.    Negative impact  1) The procedure increases the chance of infection due to extra handling of cells and ASM 2) Risk of overloading the bioreactor (BRX) with cells Risk assessment Major impact due to introduction of this procedure is assessed and summarized below:  1) HSE: hazard to human health and safety and the environment should be assessed, and appropriate steps should be taken.  a) The main hazard to human health and safety are considered: - Risk related to handling biological material – as most production at the ISO site is related to biological material, all operators are trained in safely handling biological materials, the topic is covered by HSE training performed at the ISO site. Therefore there is no additional risk to personnel related to handling biological material. 2) Suppliers –  No extra instrument service and calibration is needed other than that is scheduled for ASM.  3) Personnel- The procedure should be introduced in SOP0060 and the operators will be trained. The operators trained in SOP0060 will be able to perform it.  4) Qualification and validation- The procedure doesn't require qualification and validation, since it an additional procedure on an already qualified and validated instrument.    5) Production:  a. The procedure increase the risk of infection which can occur during cell culture or during reinoculation. But the possibility of occurrence is low as the operators are well- trained and the process is similar to routine operations performed by operators.  b. There is a risk of overloading the bioreactor (BRX) with cells. The actual number of cell in BRX is difficult to find. Inoculating a lot of cells in the BRX possess risk of clogging hollow fibers in the BRX. According to SOP0060, the recommended number of cells during inoculation is 300-500 millions per BRX. Thus, it should be taken into consideration to inoculate cells towards lower limit of total cells recommended during reinoculation.   "
Impacted areas of the organization and r: The decision to start a Change" was made after discussion among the operators and production management teams, about the need and benefits of reinoculation. "
Planned implementation data - comment: The implementation of a procedure for reinoculation for maximizers is incorporated into CMID48, as SOP0060 is going through major revision. Therefore this change can be voided.

Change Title: Moving SharePoint lists to new server in Office 365
Change from:: SharePoint lists are stored on SharePoint server at dias.plugg.no. The lists are accessed through a Microsoft Access application user interface (which is referred to in corresponding SOPs).
Change to: SharePoint lists will be stored at Office 365 SharePoint server at diatecno.sharepoint.com. This will be performed through a backup-restore process. The lists will be made available through the SharePoint web interface.
Justification for Change: It has been decided to upgrade Microsoft IT-systems to a newer version. This will improve the IT system functionality.  This change is a minor change as it does not have an impact to product/process quality or validated status. It does not require regulatory notification. The migration will be done during an agreed time window, less than a half working day after business hours. The files will not be accessible for changes during that time. The move to the new file location/server will be arranged by Braathe Gruppen AS, the IT supplier for Curida Diatec AS. Today's SharePoint OnPrem 2013" (on premises) will be phased out at our IT supplier and replaced by SharePoint online (Office 365). This means that all documents documents and functions stored on the current server will have to be migrated. No other change in functionality of other programs or functions. Impact: No change in functionality of the lists, only change of server where the files are stored. The files will be available through the new SharePoint site. Users must be informed and trained to understand how to use the system. Risk assessment: Backup is being done on a daily basis and the transfer are to be done outside working hours, the risk for any information to be lost during transfer is considered to be very low. Braathe is responsible for the transfer and the migration check. There is no increased risk related to this, i.e. there is always some risk for data loss or corruption when storing electronic data (as new data is stored by users frequently). Backup of the old files will be available for some time after the transfer. Old files will be given Read-only access to users to ensure that only data at the new locations are modified or created after the transfer."
Impacted areas of the organization and r: SharePoint lists actively in use at Curida Diatec Diagnostics are: - CELLSTOCK - Consumables and chemicals register/Chemical exposure
Planned implementation data - comment: Braathe will move the files to the new location/server after workhours on November the 1st

Change Title: Outsource processes to QC services (Curida Diatec)
Change from:: Curida Diatec ISO (hereafter ISO) -  sample, incubate and read results. If necessary: positive findings are sent from ISO for identification (fungi to Veterinærinstituttet, bacteria to Eurofins). Curida Diatec ISO - autoclaving of lab equipment and material in DIA086 autoclave
Change to: ISO samples according to procedurs SOP1076 Environment Monitoring, SOP1064 Infection in productions, SOP1075 Validation of Aseptic Cell Culture Processes, SOP1074 Validation of Cleanroom Gowning, Curida Diatec QC services (hereafter QC services) are responsible for correct incubation and reading of results.    QC services is responsible for identification if positive results (samples to be identified in agreement with Diatec)    QC services trend results and reports to Curida Diatec ISO according to contract. ISO performs autoclaving in DIA086 or when needed outsources autoclaving to QC services
Justification for Change: DIA299 Binder Hybridization Incubator used for environmental monitoring (incubation of plates at 30-35 degrees) is on loan from Pre-Diagnostics AS, and they have requested the incubator back to be delivered to them in week 41.2022. There is no other qualified instrument available to perform the 30-35 degree incubation of plates at ISO site. Previously the incubation and reading of the samples was performed by Catapult AS until they could  no longer perform this service. Outsourcing of this part of the environmental monitoring was beneficial for Diatec ISO, freeing up resources for other activities. QC services have the competence, capacity and the equipment needed to perform microbiological analysis according to required quality. Their incubators are already qualified and in use. QC services are located at Technopolis, Fornebu same as ISO production, and outsourcing the environmental program incubation, reading, typing and trending  will ensure that ISO staff can focus on other activities. QC services is a part of Curida Diatec GMP site and its GMP QMS system and performs these activities for GMP production. ISO site autoclaves a lot of important materials i.e. pH probes for AcuSyst, tubes for aseptic validation, buffers for QC analysis etc. The DIA086 autoclave is sometimes at full capacity, and there is no qualified backup instrument at ISO site. QC services have the resources, qualified equipment and possibility to perform autoclaving when needed.  Impact of this CR: Process/product: outsourcing a large part of the environment monitoring process, will free up time for the operators to focus on the production, other processes and improvement of them. Outsourcing autoclaving when needed will ensure that operations run smoothly at all times. Documentation: Following documents include mention the environment monitoring process and need review and update: SOP1076 Environment monitoring, DOC1076.4 Environment monitoring, SOP1064 Infection in productions, DOC1064.1 Sampling infection in production. Update of these documents are addressed as actions in this change.DOC1076.4 and DOC1064.1 should be reviewed by QC services before approval as these documents will follow the samples for documentation of results. For Autoclaving SOP4011 cleaning and autoclaving of laboratory equipment needs to be updated with the possibility of using QC services to perform the autoclaving. EM process: current process for sampling for environmental monitoring will stay as is, including the tolerance limits. Process needs to be updated to include transfer of samples from ISO to QC services and handling of data received from QC services. Incubation, reading of plates and typing of findings will be according to QC services processes, reviewed by ISO site and found acceptable prior to start of agreement. trending of results and reporting of findings will be agreed upon with QC services and detailed in a written agreement. Specifications: no impact to specification, for EM the current sampling and tolerance limits for fungi/bacteria within the facility will remain the same. For autoclaving: the autoclaving temperature will sterilise the equipment/material. Facility: sampling of facility will remain the same and be performed by ISO personnel. As the plates will not be incubated and handled in the facility it can have a positive impact on the facility with possible reduction of spores/bacteria/fungi detected during environmental monitoring. Removal of the environmental station in the analytical lab at ISO site, will also free up space for other activities. EHS: less handling of bacteria and fungi can have a positive impact on the health and safety of the operators, reducing their handling of biohazard material. QC services have internal procedures for discarding of biohazard according to regulations. Possible risks and any mitigating actions: Capacity/availability: While incubation and reading is performed at ISO site, the capacity of incubation is rarely tested as it is only ISO samples incubated and ISO operators are available for all planned and unplanned sampling that is needed. Outsourcing process to QC services might limit both capacity (as it is not only ISOs samples incubated in the incubator) and availability as QC services need to receive and handle the samples. This will therefore require careful planning and good communication between ISO and QC services. If possible it should be included in the agreement between the two departments specific dates/times to submit samples, how to communicate if there are a lot of samples to submit and any unplanned sampling that needs to be done at any given time. Contamination/destruction of samples: Today samples are transported directly from cleanroom to environmental station within the same facility. When outsourcing incubation etc to QC services the samples will be transported over a longer distance and therefore there is a higher risk of contamination or destruction during transport (via operator on foot) as plates might open or be dropped. To counteract this risk, all plates will be taped together immediately after transport out of cleanroom, collected in a box with a lid prior to transporting to QC servcies.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Note: SOPs and Docs impacted by this CR will undergo a transition period where old version of the documents will be used until a new version is approved. Required information will be given to all personnel impacted.
Planned implementation data - comment: During implementation there will be a transition period where QC services can be used while SOPs and DOCs are being updated.

Change Title: Change of company name from Diatec Monoclonals AS to Curida Diatec AS
Change from:: Company name Diatec Monoclonals AS
Change to: Company name Curida Diatec AS
Justification for Change: On September 22nd 2022, Diatec Monoclonals AS was aquired by Curida Biologics AS.  100 % of the stocks were transfered to the new owners. In this process the company name was also changed to Curida Diatec AS.  Impacted areas and risk assessment: 1. Customer management The company name change was registered in Brønnøysundregistered on the 22nd of September 2022.  In June 2022, before the acquisition of Diatec by Curida, a pre-change notification (CATTID 23), regarding the change of control was sent out to Abbott Longford and their change notification team: add-scn@abbott.com . The notification stated that if Diatec were acquired, there would be no company name change. At this stage of the process with Curida, the intention was to keep Diatec Monoclonals AS as the company name, but it was later decided that the Curida brand would be used. Abbott Longford had no objections to the information stated in the memo (CATTID 24), but the change of company name will most likely lead to the need for document updates from Abbott's side. The update process might cause a delay in the order/delivery of products, as the documentation needs to be in place before they can receive a product from Diatec. As requested by the legal team from Curida, it was also sent an email (CATTID 25 and 26) to Abbott Jena and IDL Biotech informing them of possible new owners. The company name was not mentioned in these emails. None of the customers had any objections to the new ownership of Diatec. All the customers will be notified officially that Diatec Company was acquired and change the name to Curida Diatec AS. In addition, the social media and the company website will be updated with the new company name and logo to ensure the new potential customers obtain up-to-date information from the internet. No related risk is identified. Refer to change actions: 62 and 64. 2. Supplier management The organization's name is included in the service and material suppliers contract, therefore, all contracts must be updated, and the suppliers must be notified. As the organization number 950372 737 remains the same after the company accusation, the risk of incorrectly addressed fracture is considered minor. Refer to change action: 63 3. Production So far, all the products have been labeled with Diatec Monoclonals' name and logo. The Certificate of Analysis (CoA) associated with the product also contains Diatec Monoclonals' name and logo. The CoA and label templates will be updated to reflect a change in the organization. However, all the products manufactured so far will not be relabelled. The relabelling for a product in stock can be performed at the customer's request (when a product from stock will be purchased). The CoA with an updated company name and log can be issued upon the customer's request for products from stock. Products stored in the fridge (t=2-8 ⁰C) can be relabelled without impact on product quality. However, products stored in a freezer (t<-20 ⁰C) and ultra-freezer (t<-60 ⁰C) have to be thawed before a new label can be placed on the vial. The freeze and thaw cycle can impact product quality, therefore, relabelling will be performed at the customer's request only when the customer is informed and agrees on the risk associated with the process. The product whose production was already initiated will be labeled and released with the old logo and company name. The CoA and label template must be updated to reflect the new organization name. The product for which the manufacturing process was initiated after the 22nd of September will be released with the label and CoA updated with the new organization's name and logo. This information will be provided in a change notification distributed among customers. The change will not impact the production timeline and will not cause product delivery delays. The product in stock can still be sold to customers if needed, despite being labelled with old company name and logo. Refer to change actions 62 and 65. 4. Documentation The company name and logo are presented in all internal procedures, worksheets, and templates. In addition, some of the procedure has a direct reference to the company name. Therefore, all templates and internal documents must be updated. All documents directly referencing the company name must be identified and updated. This action will be executed over time and cause several documents to refer to the old company name during the transition. However, this will not impact any production process either product quality. No related risk is identified. Refer to change action 65. 5. Organization Due to the company's acquisition, Ole Dahlberg was nominated for the CEO role on the 22nd of September 2022, replacing the General manager role. Bjørn Pedersen, who held a General manager position, will lead the R&D team for GMP and ISO sites, his job title will be Senior Advisor R&D. Therefore the internal documents must be updated to reflect this change: organization chart. No related risk is identified. Refer to change action: 67. 6. QMS The organization's name is included in the ISO certification. The certificate confirms that the Diatec Monoclonal, ISO Production site complies with current requirements for ISO9001:2015 standard. The document is provided to the customers on request. The updated certificate has to be obtained from certifying body, Kiwa AS. The Kiwa AS has to be notified, the company details have to be updated on the company profile on Kiwa AS portal, and an updated certificate has to be provided. No related risk is identified. Refer to change action: 66 7. Logistic The Mamut system in the logistics department generates a document with the company name and logo. The system has to be updated to reflect a change in the organization. No related risk is identified. Refer to change action: 68
Impacted areas of the organization and r: NA
Planned implementation data - comment: The company name change was registered in Brønnøysundregistered on the 22nd of September 2022. The change will be implemented on 7th of October, when customers and suppliers will be informed. The social media and the company website will also be updated within this timeline. The internal procedures, templates, certification, and generated logistic document will be updated over three months. Documents that are sent to the customers (Product Specification, COA, Notification template, etc) will be prioritized during document update execution.

Change Title: Implementation of additional freezer for final product storage
Change from:: The final, approved products are stored in a locked cabinet inside the cooling room (DIA283, t=2-8C) and freezer (DIA067, <20C).
Change to: An additional freezer will be placed in the BIA room to extend the capacity for the final products storage.
Justification for Change: The new freezer will be implemented as the storage capacity for the final product reaches its limit.   Risk management: Temperature fluctuation during the daily operation of the freezer. The temperature inside the freezer will be monitored constantly using a temperature monitoring system, Sirus system, and temperature loggers. The loggers will measure the temperature of the air inside the freezer. Frequently door opening during product placement or removal can cause a spike in monitored temperature. To mitigate the risk of temperature fluctuation, the freezer will always be at least 50 % loaded to prevent a quick temperature spike during inventory or other activities. In addition, the storage system will be established, and all shelves will be labeled accordingly to the defined system. Refer to change action 61. The temperature fluctuation and time recovery for different scenarios will be investigated during performance qualification. Refer to change actions 57.    Facility – facility requirements are limited to ambient temperature in the room, space, and electric power requirements. Facility suitability will be evaluated under the IQOQPQ study. Refer to change actions 57.    Documentation – the SOP covers all the freezers, fridges, and cooling rooms used at Diatec. It should be evaluated if the procedure must be updated regarding the new instruments. In addition, an established routine for the freezer should be followed up. Refer to change actions 60 and 58.     Security – freezer will be used for storage of final product. The freezer should be equipped with a lock, and the code to the lock should be distributed among dedicated personnel. Refer to change action 59.  Service - This is a new commercial freezer purchased from Elkjøp. There is no routine for the service of such instruments at Diatec ISO  since the instruments have a hermetically sealed compressor/cooling system. The maintained temperature will be continuously monitored using the Sirus system. Refer to change action 60. The trend review for temperature will be performed in a defined time frame to detect any changes in instrument performance. In case of observed malfunctions/underperformance, the instrument will be replaced.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: The instrument performance was briefly investigated by monitoring temperature over a few days. This preliminary study demonstrated that the freezer maintains a stable temperature. The temperature chart is attached.
Planned implementation data - comment: There is still some room left in the original freezer, but the new one will be used as soon is available.

Change Title: Implementing supplier qualification module in BizzMine
Change from:: Supplier qualification is performed according to SOP 5026 Supplier Quality Program. The system is paper-based and requires manual document signing and archiving. In addition, qualification status is tracked using an excel spreadsheet. Supplier Quality Program_Diatec_ISO
Change to: Supplier qualification will be performed and documented using a collection in eQMS software, BizzMine.
Justification for Change: The supplier program at Diatec is defined by internal Procedure SOP5026:Supplier Qualification Program is followed in the excel spreadsheet Supplier Quality Program_Diatec_ISO". The excel list contains all used suppliers and their qualifications status. The supplier's initial approval is documented using Supplier Approval form DOC5026.1. All documentation for supplier approval (initial supplier qualification) is traceable and stored on the local server: F:\KVALITET\Suppliers.    According to the procedure, re-qualification of the supplier should be performed periodically, dependent upon the rating. When a supplier is qualified, the re-qualification date is defined and documented in the supplier approval form. During the investigation performed for NCR42, the approval form was inspected for critical suppliers to verify if the Supplier Quality Program_Diatec_ISO excel spreadsheet was up today. It was found that supplier qualification was not performed (or not documented) since at least 2019. The re-qualification should base on several factors such as delivery on time, number of supplier complaints, service history etc. No assessment based on those criteria was found in inspected Supplier Quality Program excel spreadsheet or other documents.     The Supplier Quality Program list was established and maintained by the previous Quality Manager. When the previous Quality Manager stepped down from his position and left the company in August 2021, there was no instruction left on how to update/maintain the supplier excel list. During the gap in position and onboarding process, there were no resources to follow up on the Supplier Program. Moreover, the procedure SOP5026 provides general instructions on how the supplier should be requalified. No instructions are provided on how re-qualification should be documented, and there is no dedicated form for that purpose. The previous system owner did not follow the internal procedure, at least from 2019.    The new system will be based on a collection (form) in eQMS software, BizzMine. The collection will be connected with the already established BizzMine “Supplier List” list. The scanned supplier qualification documents will be transferred from the local server into eQMS software. The gap assessment will be performed to update the status of all currently used suppliers.    The risk associated with the implementation and use of eQMS software was evaluated during Change Control CMR204_Change from paper-based to electronic QMS and Risk assessment QRM134_Change from paper-based to electronic QMS. The risk associated with using eQMS software for supplier qualification:    -        Deleting information /records. The risk is mitigated by defining different permission levels. Only QA Manager will have permission to delete records from the collection. If the record is accidentally deleted, it can be restored from Trask bean by Admin or BizzMine support. Overview of all deleted records can be found in Trash bean for the collection    -        Missing re-qualification due date - Implementing the software will reduce the risk of missing the re-qualification deadline. The notification system will be a step up, sending a reminder to an assigned team member (QA Team).     -        Untrained/unauthorized persons will perform a change in a documented record. Different permission levels (for reading, editing, creating, approving etc.) will be established and tested during collection validation. Only personal with authorized access will be able to edit or create a record in the collection."
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Collection in sQMS software will be tested according to procedure 5048.1.
Planned implementation data - comment: NA
