Change Title: AcuSyst-Xcellerator Moved from GMP-site to Fornebu to be implemented in production.
Change from:: Production of monoclonals antibodies at the ISO site is currently conducted in a CL1000 bioreactor (low-scale production) and AcuSyst Maximiser (high-scale production).
Change to: In addition to currently used bioreactors, CL1000 and AcuSyst Maximiser, Acusyst Xcellerator will be implemented into the ISO site to increase production capacity
Justification for Change: Justification:	 The Acusyst Xcellerator (Xcellerator) is an advanced mammalian cell culture system for large-scale efficient and economical production of cell-secreted proteins. This automated system provides an optimal environment for cell growth and production. The Xcellerator is comprised of: -	Xcellerator cell culture instrument, containing an incubator and refrigerator sections. -	Built-in control computer running ACUSOFT-Xcellerator software -	Two independent, disposable flowpaths that may be operated simultaneously -	Two medium warmer modules Xcellerator is the second-hand instrument used at the GMP site of Diatec Monoclonals for pharmaceutical production. Due to the termination of the agreement with the customer, the GMP site discontinued using the instrument and offered it to the ISO site free of charge. Positive impact The Xcellerator is a similar instrument to the Acusyst Maximiser bioreactors (Maximiser) currently used in production on the ISO site but has higher production capabilities. It operates with the same technologies and hollow fiber system as Maximisers, with either 6 or 10 hollow fiber cartridges instead of 2 cartridges of a maximisers cultureware, identical in size and composition to the Xcellerator. Implementing Xcellerator can help increase the production productivity up to 5 fold, and clones already produced on the Maximiser will most likely produce well in Xcellerator. Another advantage is the integrated software that can be connected to a network. Integrated software will allow for remote monitoring and can be connected to an external monitoring system (Sirius system), something the Maximisers do not have. Using the Xcellerator will increase the detection rate of any issues with the production and the possibility of correcting them before it results in a run termination. The same approved supplier will provide services for Maximisers and Xcellerators, Cell Culture Company (C3).  Instrument implementation and test production will be supported by an operator from the GMP site, considered an expert on operating the instrument. Therefore, implementing the instrument and initiating a test production should be executed efficiently and without many resources required. The instrument was given free of charge, therefore cost of the implementation is limited only to the working hours of the employees involved in the process. The GMP site provided a consumable flow path, tubings, and medium that can be used during instrument qualification. Negative impact: Due to high operational costs, like the cost of consumables and needed working hours, The Xcellerator is only suitable for a large order of antibodies. Moreover, this instrument cannot be suitable for all types of clones used in production at the ISO site. Using the Maximiser instrument for test production using a new clone will be recommended. The Xcellerator instrument will be located in room 106 (AS production room) in the ISO production facility. Room 106 is currently used for large-scale production using Maximiser bioreactors. When the Xcellerator is placed in this room, adding more bioreactors in this localization will not be possible. If the demand for a product continues, the production facility will have to be redesigned/ extended or the Maximiser instruments should be replaced with instruments of higher capacity.  Like the Maximisers, the Xcellerator requires a constant medium supply. The instrument will be connected to medium tanks, stored in a cold room, and supplied via tubings. As utilizing just one of the Xcellerator flowpaths uses more medium than a Maximisers run, additional tank(s) should be available at all times. The space in the cold room will be fully utilized, which will put more pressure on the cold room storage capacity at the ISO production site. If the demand for a product increases and multiple AcuSyst instruments run simultaneously, the production facility must be redesigned or extended. Risk assessment Implementing the Xcellerator bioreactor will impact several areas of the ISO site organization. The main impact is assessed and summarised below: 1.	Facility: facility requirements will be defined and addressed during installation qualification. Instrument and medium tanks needed for production will challenge cold storage capacity at the ISO production site. The topic will be addressed in Change action 42. 2.	HSE – hazard to human health and safety and the environment should be assessed, and appropriate steps should be taken.  a)	The main hazard to human health and safety are considered: -	Risk related to handling biological material – as most production at the ISO site is related to biological material, all operators are trained in safely handling biological materials, the topic is covered by HSE training performed at the ISO site. Therefore there is no additional risk to personnel related to handling biological material. -	Risks related to handling CO2 gas – all operators are trained in handling CO2 gas, the topic is covered by HSE training all operators must complete. Therefore no additional risk to personnel related to this change. -	HMS aspects will be partially covered by operator training and instrument SOP - The topic will be addressed in Change actions 43 and 44. Training will address the risk related to weight lifting and the risk of electric shock. b)	The main hazard to the environment: -	The risk associated to waste management, including used biological medium and bioreactors consumables (flow path)  –  all consumable parts used in production will be utilized according to the local procedure for waste management (SOP4006). -	The risk associated with handling biological material and the risk of biological contamination – Xcellerator will not increase the risk of biological contaminants in the production facility. The Xcellerator bioreactor is a closed system, which highly limits the production facility's risk of biological contamination. After cell inoculation, there is no higher risk of contamination from an Xcellerator than already used Maximiser instruments. The Xcellerator requires a higher amount of cells to inoculate, about 5x the amount needed for a Maximiser. With increased handling of the cell culture, the chances of infection are higher, with the risk of spreading within the facility. However, the operators are highly trained in expanding cells, screening for infection during the expansion phase, and are validated annually on their aseptic technique. In addition, environment monitoring is performed in the production facility according to a local procedure (SOP 1076). The result of the monitoring conducted during test production will be inspected if instrument implementation did cause an increased risk of biological contamination in the production area. The topic will be addressed in change action 45. 3.	Suppliers –  Cell Culter Company (C3) will provide the instrument service and calibration, in addition to the bioreactor and its consumables. C3 is already a qualified and approved supplier for the ISO production site. However, the agreement with the supplier should be revised to evaluate from a production and business perspective (evaluate service and consumables delivery). The topic will be addressed in change action 46. 4.	QMS – Implementation of the instrument should comply with the local procedure for Equipment (ref SOP 5043). The instrument implementation form should be prepared and approved for Xcellerator and all other supporting instruments used in the test production process.  The Instrument implementation form (DOC 5043.2) covers the following aspects of the QMS: instrument labeling, maintenance and calibration, documentation, and training. The topic will be addressed in change action 47. 5.	Personnel – Sufficient training should be provided for production operators. The training module should be established according to the local procedure for training handling (SOP 5047). The training records should be prepared and approved for all trained operators. The topic will be addressed in change actions 44 and 45. 6.	Logistic – all consumables must be defined and categorized according to the local procedure for incoming material control (SOP 5010). The specification should include a quality control approach and release criteria. The topic will be addressed in change action 50. Multiple tubes are needed to run an Xcelerator, YY-tube for medium supply, Y tube for drain, and inoculation manifold. The YY- and Y-tubes can be purchased from Thermo Fisher Scientific or produced in-house. The agreement with Thermo Fisher Scientific will not be considered due to the long lead time, and all consumables will be prepared in-house. The inoculation manifold is produced in-house. For in-house production of tubings, document SOP 1082 needs to be updated with the instructions on the assembly of the tubings. This topic will be addressed in action 49. 7.	Qualification/Validation – The instrument will be used in the manufacturing process of monoclonals antibodies. Therefore, a complete instrument qualification should be performed according to the local procedure for Qualification and Validation activities (SOP 5018). The qualification should be divided into three steps:  a)	Installation qualification – performed by service representative from C3 Company official instrument suppliers and consumable manufactures b)	operation qualification – performed by service representative from C3 Company official instrument suppliers and consumable manufactures c)	performance qualification – performed as a test production using 1A12 clone. The topic will be addressed in change action 51. 8.	Production -	Instrument: Xcelerator is similar to Maximisers, however, the software and interphase of the instrument are more advanced, allowing for more monitoring and better control of the instrument. Furthermore, the pumps are similar but not the same. These differences might confuse when running both Maximisers and Xcellerator at the same time by the same operators. This is mitigated by proper training of all operators involved (actions 44 and 45) and the generation of instructional documentation (action 48). -	Expansion of cells: inoculation of the Xcellerator requires a high amount of cells (3-5x 109 cells) and, therefore, significantly more T175 flasks (up to 80 flasks) expanded over more days than other bioreactor systems used by Diatec. This can cause incubator capacity problems during expansion for Xcellerators and must be planned accordingly so as not to impact other productions running simultaneously. This issue will be evaluated after starting test production, and the need for the proper solution will be addressed. -	IT security: as Xcellerator is connected to LAN for remote monitoring, unauthorized access to production data and tampering is possible. However, all access outside of Diatec is protected by a firewall administered by Diatecs IT provider, Braathe. Only designated computers (that contain a linked Teamviewer account) can access the Xcellerator monitor and log in from outside the facility. Based on risk assessment change, actions will be initiated.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: All aspects related to documentation and validation will be addressed in change actions.
Planned implementation data - comment: The planned implementation date reflects when Xcellerator can be used for production. However, as there is currently no order for such a high scale production, the timeline is flexible, but must be completed prior to first production.

Change Title: Change of raw material in Diatec std BPC for RPMI 100L,200L
Change from:: BioProcess Containers (BPCs) for Diatec standard RPMI 100L/200L tanks are produced with film type: ASI 9101 -          This is the direct contact material with media in the BPC   Supplier: Life Technologies ASManufacturer: Thermo Fisher Scientific – film type exclusively produced in Millersburg, USA Lead time: 35-50 weeks
Change to: BioProcess Containers (BPCs) for Diatec standard RPMI 100L/200L tanks are produced with film type: Aegis5-14 (alternative film type, in case of production and/or delivery problems: CX5-14) -          This is the direct contact material with media in the BPC   Supplier: Life Technologies ASManufacturer: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TFS), both film types are produced in most of TFS manufacturing facilities in Europe and USA Lead time: approx. 20 weeks
Justification for Change: The main reason for this change is to shorten the lead-time of our RPMI 100L/200L tanks, cat no 04194218BX/04194218DX, Diatec standard use of media in AcuSyst Maximizer production. These are also used in CL1000 production in use of filling system. The ASI 9101 is an old film type used from the original BOM from 2012.Because of low demand of this film type, this is only produced in TFS manufacturing facility in Millersburg, USA, leading to very long lead-time of our final RPMI 100L/200L tanks. That is also a risk if there would be any issues with that single production site at Thermofisher Scientific.  Another reason for changing film type is to increase the quality of BPC, by using a film type – the material with direct contact with the media – with less leachable extracts and better puncture resistance to ensure highest quality of the product. See details in product data sheets. Use of the Aegis5-14 film was recommended by Thermofisher Scientific, and also to have the CX5-14 film as an alternative if there would be any issues with production or delivery of the Aegis5-14 film. These two types of film was suggested by Thermofisher Scientific Senior Technical Sales Specialist, expert on  BioProduction/Cell culture/Cell & Gene Therapy, PhD Cathrine Choy. These film types are according to her best suited for our use.

Change Title: Two Acusyst Maximizers implemented in production.
Change from:: Three Acusyst Maximizers was in use at ISO Production site, those include DIA030, DIA031 and DIA209 instruments.
Change to: Two additional Acusyst Maximizers, DIA079 and DIA029 were moved from our GMP-site at the research park into ISO Production site on Fornebu. This was done to increase production capacity at the ISO-site.
Justification for Change: Reason for the Change initiation: The instrument implementation is performed in response to the growing demand for monoclonal antibodies and customer requests for larger-scale production. The production scale-up also complies with Diatec Monoclonals' strategy and goals for 2022 - 2027.  Risk assessment:   The risk associated with the instrument implementation is considered minor. The two Acusyst Maximizers are identical to the three instruments already used in production. The operators are trained and experienced in using and maintaining the instruments. There is no need to establish a new procedure as the topic is already covered by the internal procedure SOP0060 and associated with its worksheets. In addition, there is no need to create a new product specification, as all the spare parts and cell culture mediums are currently used in production. There is no The potential risk was identified:  increased workload,  increased risk of cross-contamination, logistic challenges supplying the machines and decrease of storage capacity. At this time, the production team is confident that the increased workload and risk of cross-contamination are manageable and that no immediate changes should be made. DIA283/Room105 was reorganized to increase its capacity for medium-tank/ EC-medium flask/harvest bag storage, which no longer cause issues. A total capacity and medium delivery logistics require better planning but are also manageable.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Acusyst Maximizer is a bioreactor that utilizes hollow-fiber technology to produce retain cell and cell products isolated. Diatec monoclonal uses Maximizers to produce antibodies from several hybridoma cell lines. At ISO Fornebu Maximizers and CL1000 are exclusively used for this goal, for higher and lower yields respectively. Instrument Qualification: These instruments can, under SOP5043`s definition, be considered a like-for-like. The machines will be serviced and certified according to SOP0060 before use.
Planned implementation data - comment: Change Control will allow to increase production capacity and efficiency.

Change Title: New cleaning routine for Storage room (110)
Change from:: Cleaning performed by internal personnel according SOP1099 version 4.0 pt. 9.1.6:Weekly - all floors with 75% ethanolMonthly - all floors with peroxideEvery 6th month - all available surfaces with diluted actril Cleaning in the common areas performed by external personnel was documented in Attachment 1 to the SOP1099.
Change to: Cleaning performed by external personnel twice per week according to agreement as other common areas at Diatec.1 annual cleaning performed by internal personnel at Diatec including all available surfaces with commercial cleaning detergent currently used in the office space. Replace Attachment 1 to SOP1099 with new document: Doc 1099.4 for documentation of cleaning of common areas by external personnel.
Justification for Change: The cleaning schedule was revised after being valid for one year and was not fully suitable for its needs. The main purpose for cleaning of the storage room is to remove dust accumulated in the daily operation of the room. The use of ethanol and peroxide was introduced first to reduce the risk of infection in production from consumeble materials. This procedure is considered unnecessary since all material are wiped before entering clean room, transported through unsterile conditions. All materials going into the cleanroom are individually cleaned using peroxide before being transferred into clean room. Therefore using of ethanol or peroxide in the weekly routines in the storage area is considered unnecessary. The weekly and monthly cleaning will be performed using commercial detergent that usually is used for common areas. This can be outsourced to the company responsible for the weekly cleaning in the other common areas in Diatec.  The described cleaning routine in SOP1099 v4.0 is from when Diatec ISO department was located in Forskningsparken, and storage room was placed inside the analytical lab. Therefor the cleaning routine in storage room was reflecting the cleaning routine as in the analytical lab.At the ISO Fornebu site, the storage room is located next to the office and other common areas, where external personnel are responsible for cleaning. When Diatec moved in to current facilities in December 2020, the routine for cleaning storage room was changed from, beyond weekly and monthly cleaning, 1 annual cleaning to 2 semi-annual cleaning for unclear reasons.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: We have twice reoccurred non- conformances where the weekly cleaning of the storage room was not performed due to Holidays and few people in the office. By outsourcing this task to a company we prevent this from happening again. The company suggested is already engaged in the weekly cleaning in the Diatec facilities as office area, kitchen and mezzanine. Ref. CMR203 When working with this Change Management it was discovered that the Attachment 1 to SOP1099, Cleaning of Common areas, was not transferred to Bizzmine. This will now be a separate document, Doc1099.4 updated to include Storage area (room 110).

Change Title: Implement a new Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer System
Change from:: Replace loaned instrument Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer System DIA302
Change to: Implement a new Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer System
Justification for Change: The Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer System DIA302 was borrowed from Yke J. Arnoldussen, Account manager LSG (Biorad), and is temporarily used at Diatec Monoclonals. The instrument was borrowed since the delivery of the new instrument was delayed. The Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer System is used for western blotting procedure “SOP1100 Western Blot analysis on anti-NGAL antibodies using semi dry blotting” to determine immunoreactivity of product #9980 and product #9990. Only one customer, Abbott Ireland Diagnostics division requires using this technique in quality control of their products.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Description of instrument: The Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer System is a high-performance western blotting transfer system designed to provide rapid transfers with high efficiency. The system enables blot transfer of protein in as little as 3 minutes without sacrificing performance when compared with traditional tank protein blotting. The Trans-Blot Turbo Transfer Packs include an optimized buffer, membrane, and filter paper combination that provides superior blot transfers when paired with the Trans-Blot Turbo blotting apparatus. Protein blotting consumables are available in a ready-to-use, pre-packaged stack or in bulk quantities that require assembly. Principle of blotting: Proteins are transferred from a gel to a membrane (for example PVDF membrane) by applying a current The Trans-Blot instrument must be qualified. IQ will ensure that the equipment is installed according to manufacturer recommendations.   Diatec employees at the ISO Production Site will be informed. There is no need to inform customers or authorities. OQ will ensure that the equipment perform as intended. Instrument function is to transfer proteins from an SDS gels to a membrane. OQ shall demonstrate that the instrument performance ensures that the blotting procedure works across a range of different protein sizes and along the whole surface of the membrane. To do so, OQ should be performed with following procedure: all the well of a gel will be loaded with a standard containing different protein sizes. Suggested standard: Bio-Rad cat.no 161-0376. Use SOP1100 draft version for run conditions and standard volumes. PQ is not applicable as the instrument is used for western blotting procedure  which results is not dependent on instrument apart for the transfer efficiency already tested in OQ.

Change Title: Extending tank medium shelf life, from 12 to 18 months
Change from:: Temporary change for extending the shelf life of one single lot of tank cell culture RPMI1640 medium. The timeframe for change is from 01.JAN.2022 (start) to 30.JUN.2022 (end). The RPMI cell culture medium has a 12 months shelf life. Material cat no: 041-94218
Change to: Extend the shelf life of RPMI cell culture medium from 12 to 18 months for lot2234714. Amount of remaining tanks of lot 2234714: 15 x 04194218BX – 100L 14 x 04194218DX – 200L
Justification for Change: Justification: The Covid-19 pandemic has a strong negative impact on the global supply chain. This situation leads to insecure and extended lead times for several operational products. The RPMI cell culture medium's lead time has been extended to an 39-52+ weeks by the supplier (see attached email notification). Therefore, Diatec needs to place orders a long time before the production plan is set. In this case, the amount of RPMI tanks ordered and released did not correspond to the incoming orders from customers and actual production during 2021. This was a risk taken by Diatec when placing PO83573, for this batch of RPMI.   Risk assessment: The RPMI cell culture medium used at Diatec, cat no 04194218, is a custom medium produced by Thermo Firsher Scientific based on Diatec’s specifications. The Diatec Monoclonas AS is responsible for setting the shelf life for this material. The cell culture medium has currently defined a 12-month shelf-life. The impact of extending the shelf life of the medium was evaluated with the collaboration with the product supplier. The supplier confirms that the medium should retain its quality for additional six months, however, some degradation and descries in the concentration of some amino acids over time can be expected. Therefore, two cell culture medium lots were tested in September 2021 to evaluate the impact of extending product shelf life on its quality. -          Cell culture medium, lot 2167300 (“sample 1”) with an expiration date of 12-2020. It was tested 9  months after passing its expiration date. -          Cell culture medium lot 2234714 (“sample 2”) with an expiration date of 12-2021. It was tested 4 months before passing its expiration date. The level of vitamins and amino acids was determined in both medium lots. From all tested molecules, only the level of L-Glutamine significantly decreased in the expired medium (“sample 1”), reaching approx. 40% of the concentration after 21 months after production. No significant changes in    vitamins or amino acids (except L-Glutamine) were observed in medium lot 2167300,   indicating that the medium will retain its quality for at least 9 months after passing its expiration date.    L-Glutamine supports cells' growth with high energy demands and synthesizes large amounts of proteins and nucleic acids. It is an alternative energy source for rapidly dividing cells and using glucose inefficiently. Low-level L-Glutamine amino acid can potentially cause decrees in the yield, but not the quality of the antibodies produced by the cells. The risk associated with using a cell culture medium with an extended shelf life is considered minor and is defined as a business risk only.  The medium will be supplemented with L-Glutamine before its use in production to reduce the risk of low yield. The L-Glutaimin level will be supplemented to 75 % of the level in the freshly released medium. The Production team will carry the L-Glutamine supplementation according to internal procedure.   The risk associated with an increasing amount of L-Glutamine degradation products (ammonium and pyroglutamate) is considered minor. The scientific study demonstrated that for cell cultures used in vaccine production, the media retain their functionality regarding cell growth and viability  over a specific time window despite L-Glutamine degradation (Cytotechnology. 2016 Aug, 68(4): 1171–1183).     The quality of the antibodies produced using this cell culture medium will not be affected. In-process controls will be performed weekly, using ELISA technique to monitor the yield. In the worst scenario, if the yield will be significantly lower than expected, additional production units can be set up to increase production on the company cost.
Impacted areas of the organization and r: Results from performed testing and communication with the medium supplier are saved as an attachment to the change.
Planned implementation data - comment: NA
